Nama Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Prishtaya Bhutale Srimate Bhaktivedanta Swamin Nityanamine Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Paschati Deshadarine Jai Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nityananda Shri Advaita Gadadhara Shri Vasadi Gaurabhakta Vrinda Namo Mahavadanyaya Krishna Prema Pradayate Krishnaya Krishna Chaitanya Namne Gaurathushe Namah Namo Brahmane Devaya Go Brahmane Hitaya Cha Jagadhitaya Krishnaya Govindaya Namo Namaha He Krishna Karuna Sindhu Deena Bandhu Jagatpate Gopesha Gopika Kanta Radha Kanta Namostute Tapta Kanchan Gaurangi Radhe Vrindavaneshwari Vrishabhanu Sute Devi Pranamami Hari Priye Vansha Kalpatru Bhyasya Pripa Sindhu Bhaye Vachya Pati Tanam Pavne Bhyo Vaishnave Bhyo Namo Namaha Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare

---
#### Maya, Krishna, Surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xAGKTxpgXiE?t=247
**Verse References:** BG 7.14
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "If you surrender to me, I will free you from this illusory energy."
**Summary:** The speaker begins the discussion on Maya, explaining that Krishna refers to Maya as 'my energy' in the Gita, clarifying that while Maya appears to trap and entangle us, it is ultimately under Krishna's control, and surrender to Krishna is the means to be freed from its illusory influence.

So the discussion is on Maya. Krishna in the Gita says, 'Devi Hi Esha Gunamai Mamma Maya Dhurataya.' Mamma Maya means my Maya Shakti. Generally Maya is regarded as some kind of a villain, some kind of a energy which is pulling us, which is trapping us, which is entangling us, but in this verse Krishna is saying, 'Mamma Maya,' it is my energy. On one side we understand that Lord is our loving father. He is very kind. He is very merciful. The other side we get to understand that there is an energy of that loving father which is trapping us, which is entangling us, which is binding us. Looks some kind of a contradiction, loving father trying to entangle his children. So what is this Maya Shakti? So in the Bhagavad Gita Krishna says, 'Devi Hi Esha Gunamai,' this is my energy. 'Mamma Maya Dhurataya,' Dhurataya means it is very very difficult to surmount. It is very difficult to come out of the traps of this Maya, which is my energy. And then the next line Krishna says, 'Maam eva ye prapadyante,' if you surrender to me, 'Maayam etam taranti te,' I will free you from this illusory energy.

---
#### Illusion, Maya, Desire
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xAGKTxpgXiE?t=377
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "They fall in this trap of mirage."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the meaning of Maya as 'that which is not' or illusion, using the example of a mirage in the desert to illustrate how Maya deceives beings seeking pleasure, leading them to endlessly pursue false hopes and ultimately suffer.

So what is Maya? It is comprised of two words. Ma means not. Ya means this. Another word of Maya means illusion. For example, what is example of illusion which all of us are aware of, if not seen at least we have read in our textbooks, mirage in a desert. It looks like many of us while we are driving on highways would have also noticed, especially on summer days. It appears that there is some kind of water simmering on the road. Or in the desert it appears that there is some kind of a desert, some kind of a water. And in deserts there are animals who when they are thirsty, when they are moving around in search of water, they fall in this trap of mirage. It appears to them that there is water body. It looks like water. They run to that apparent water body and they find that there is no water there. But still that illusion is not over because they are thirsty and because they are in search of water, there is a promise of water way ahead. The goal post changes and they again start running behind that mirage thinking that it is water. And this goes on. No doubt sometimes animals are intelligent. They can figure out that it is an illusion. There is actually no water. But sometimes animals, if they don't realize this trap, can keep running in search of water and eventually may even die out of thirst.

---
#### Pleasure, Maya, Entanglement
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xAGKTxpgXiE?t=501
**Verse References:** SB 1.1.1
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Those very pleasures become the source of our bondage and entanglement."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the nature of Maya, explaining that while seeking pleasure is natural, the illusion of Maya leads us to pursue worldly pleasures that appear to bring happiness but ultimately result in bondage and entanglement, using the example of sense objects.

So likewise there are many examples of Maya. Maya means that which is not. We all are pleasure seeking beings. It is not wrong to seek pleasure. Anandamayo Bhyasat. Goal of spiritual life is not to become inert and have no desires, seek no pleasures and just engage in tapasya, austerities, penances and then aim for moksha. No. There is a positive pleasure, a genuine pleasure which we all are entitled to experience and pursue. But unfortunately under the influence of Maya, Maya means that which is not, there are some pleasures in this world which looks like it will make me happy, some sense objects for the eyes, the forms, for the ears, some beautiful melodious sounds, for the skin, some sense objects in the form of pleasurable touch sensations. There is a promise of happiness and under the influence of Maya, since we are pleasure seeking beings, we pursue those pleasures with the understanding, with the hope that I will be fully happy, fully satisfied. Now because it is Maya, it is not real, it is illusion. There are many pleasures in this world which appears to be the source of happiness, source of lasting bliss. But Krishna in the Gita says because it is under the influence of Maya, those very pleasures which we pursue in the hope of being happy, those very pleasures become the source of our bondage and entanglement.

---
#### Maya, Animal Kingdom, Entrapment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xAGKTxpgXiE?t=663
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "That very object which is going to be source of pleasure for the fish is the cause of its death."
**Summary:** The speaker provides examples from the animal kingdom—a fish caught by bait, a deer lured by music—to illustrate how the pursuit of pleasure, driven by Maya, can lead to death and entrapment.

I will give you a few examples from the animal kingdom. A fish, when a fisherman goes to catch fish, we all have seen, some of us may have also done some kind of fishing. A fisherman generally goes with that fishing rod. It has a thread with a hook in front of it. And the fisherman knows the psychology of the fish, knows what the fish likes, what will entice the fish. We call it bait. So the fisherman puts a bait on the hook and puts that hook in the water. And the fish thinking that this bait is going to be source of pleasure for me, going to quench my or other, you know fishes maybe in search of food, is going to make me happy. The fish without knowing that there is a hook behind the hook, behind that bait, goes and catches hold of that bait and in the process gets trapped. Mind you the fisherman had very intentionally planned the whole thing. The fish goes for this bait, thinks that it will become happy, will satisfy its hunger, but that very object which is going to be source of pleasure for the fish is the cause of its death. It seems that deer, you know because they jump and move very fast, it's very difficult to to catch them. And a hunter who goes for hunting in the forest and wants to catch a deer, they generally know that the weakness of the deer is melodious sounds. So it produces some melodious music. It seems that the deer helplessly get attracted to that music, come completely become dazed and stunned hearing that music and just sit like ducks hearing that music. The hunter comes and can shoot them without any chance for deer. So here we have an example of melodious music melodious sounds which is very enticing for the deer. It is also seeking pleasure, but that very pleasure becomes a cause of death for the deer.

---
#### Maya, Senses, Human Life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xAGKTxpgXiE?t=849
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Each of these five senses are compared to wild horses."
**Summary:** The speaker continues with animal examples, then contrasts animal entrapment with the human condition, stating that humans have five senses, each like a wild horse, pulling in different directions, making entrapment more complex.

Another example is given of an elephant, untamed elephants, male elephants, wild elephants. Now it's not possible for a human being to be much smaller in size compared to an elephant to catch hold of that elephant and tame it. So what they do is they have some trained female she-elephants and they somehow go past this male elephant, rub the skin of the male elephant, gets agitated and then pursues, starts moving behind this female elephant. And because this female elephant is trained, she actually passes on a way where the trap, a very big huge pit covered with green grass is already laid out. And because this she-elephant is trained she passes by the side of the pit knowing very well where the pit is. And the male elephant helplessly pursuing or walking behind this female elephant gets trapped, falls into that pit and gets trapped. So our scriptures say that in the animal kingdom, each of these animals which I mentioned about, three examples I so far shared, there is one sense which is very very prominent. Like for example deer, the sense of hearing, for fish the sense of eating the tongue, rubbing the skin by a female elephant. So in this animal kingdom we have examples of animals where one sense being prominent, that very sense becomes the cause of death of that particular animal. Our scriptures say that in human form of life we don't have one sense which is a cause of trapping us. We have five senses. In one sense, each of these five senses are compared to wild horses. Each of them trying to pull the chariot in different directions.

---
#### Senses, Mind, Intelligence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xAGKTxpgXiE?t=999
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The reins in this example, in this analogy represents the mind."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a chariot to explain the roles of the senses (horses), mind (reins), intelligence (chariot driver), and soul (passenger), highlighting the state of bewilderment when the senses are untamed and the mind and intelligence are weak.

And here this example which is given of a chariot driven by five horses, it's important for us to understand. The five horses represent the five senses. The chariot is being driven by the chariot driver using the horses. The horses are being controlled using the reins. The reins in this example, in this analogy represents the mind. The chariot driver represents our intelligence and the passenger in the chariot is being controlled. The chariot represents the soul. Now imagine a chariot in which five horses are there. All the horses are untamed, unruly, each one pulling in different directions. And the reins, the chariot driver has lost control over the reins. That's why people have hypertension, high blood pressure, anxiety attacks, depression because mind is not fully in control and the intelligence which is supposed to control the mind is weak. It has lost the power of discerning, choosing between right and wrong, making the right choices. And the soul who is like a passenger in the chariot is being bewildered. Actually that's the state of practically all of us in this material world.

---
#### Pleasure, Maya, Bondage
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xAGKTxpgXiE?t=1111
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Those very pleasures become the cause of my bondage."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that under the influence of Maya, we identify with the body and senses, seeking pleasure through sense objects, but these very pleasures become the cause of bondage, distress, anxiety, and ultimately, death.

We want pleasure. We are seeking pleasure. We are pleasure seeking beings. But we think that under the influence of Maya, we think that we are this body. The senses when they come in sense objects, it will give me pleasure. And because when the senses come in touch with the sense object, there is some pleasure sensations, thinking that these pleasure sensations will make me happy, will make me satisfied, we pursue those, engage our senses with the sense objects more and more. How we can optimize those sensual pleasures, those sensations. Now it seems that there is nothing wrong. What's wrong in seeing some beautiful objects? What's wrong in hearing some melodious music? What's wrong? Krishna in the Gita says this very enjoyment, some sparsha, some sparsha means that enjoyment which we experience by senses coming in contact with the sense objects, some sparsha, bhoga means enjoyment, that very enjoyment, dukha yonahi evate is the cause of misery. Again it's a contradiction that pleasure is the cause of misery. In the words Krishna is saying those pleasures are not real pleasures under the influence of Maya. Maya that which is not. We think that those pleasures are the source of real bliss, real contentment, real joy, real happiness. And we try to maximize those pleasures. We pursue those pleasures. We set goals. We have aspirations. We lead our lives thinking that this will make me happy and those very pleasures become the cause of my bondage, cause of my distress, cause of my anxiety and eventually the cause of my death.

---
#### Maya, Krishna, Government
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xAGKTxpgXiE?t=1263
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "We have come in this world to enjoy independent of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker poses the question of why Krishna created Maya, then uses the analogy of a government that runs both educational institutions and a police department with a prison to explain that Maya serves a purpose, like a prison, for those who want to enjoy independent of the Lord.

So Maya that which is not. Now what is? Krishna says it is my energy. Now why Krishna has created this Maya energy? Why Krishna has created this illusion? So to say we get trapped in that illusion. Before we get into that, as I mentioned Maya is energy of the Lord. For example, even in our country or in a city, let's say in a city there is a municipal corporation, like here we have BMC in Mumbai. It runs many many educational institutions. It provides education for the welfare of students, citizens. The same municipal corporation may also run a police Chowki or a police station. I don't know the whole structure who runs what, but nevertheless every city has a police station. Or let's say government runs a police department. The same government which is running an educational institution to provide education to deserving citizens, to children, the same department also invests money, resources, land in running a police department, running a prison house. In fact it spends money. Government servants are there who are also engaged in administration in the police department. They are paid salaries and the people who are in the prison house, they are also taken care of. There is a lot of expenses which are involved in the whole thing and it's not wrong. In fact it's justified that yes although it's meant to serve the citizens, to provide various amenities, it's not wrong on the part of government to also have a prison house, to have a jail because as we all discussed last time in the last session the previous session, there will be some citizens who will obey the laws, who will break the laws, who will commit crime and they need to be so to say kept aside, kept aloof so that they don't disturb the normal citizens, law abiding citizens. They don't create nuisance in the society. So we have come in this world to enjoy independent of the Lord.

---
#### Servant, Master, Maya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xAGKTxpgXiE?t=1461
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So when a servant tries for master, he is heading for trouble."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a servant trying to become the master to explain that we are constitutionally servants of the Lord, and when we try to become masters and enjoy independently, we head for trouble and become entangled in Maya.

Yes this world is created. An opportunity is given. Okay be enjoy, be an enjoyer, try to enjoy. But because it is not our constitutional position. For example, many houses we have maids and servants. They are employed and they have a particular role to play. They are servants. They cook food or they engage in various activities to serve the master. That's how they have been engaged in what they have been engaged for. For example, a person who is employed as a cook, he cooks. The meal is served to the master, whosoever is the lead member in the house. In Hindi we call Malik. This is just a crude analogy. And after the master has partaken the meals, the servant can take the meals. We generally don't expect the servant cooking as per his wish and enjoying the meal even before serving the master. This is not a normal scenario. It's not expected of a servant. The servant is made or rather is expected to make the dishes or cook things based on the requirement of the master and after the master is satisfied, he takes the food. So here in this material world we have come to become masters. Although constitutionally we are servants. So when a servant tries for master, he is heading for trouble. He forgets that he is a servant. Then he is heading for a problem. So in the same way we have come in this world. We want to enjoy forgetfulness, forgetfulness of Lord, thinking that I am the enjoyer, I am the proprietor, I am the well wisher. And that mentality becomes the cause of our bondage. That becomes the cause of our entanglement.

---
#### Maya, Illusion, Surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xAGKTxpgXiE?t=1641
**Verse References:** BG 7.14
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Maya Shakti makes us forget that I am servant of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Maya Shakti creates the illusion that we are masters and enjoyers, leading to entanglement, and emphasizes that surrendering to the Lord is the way to come out of this illusion, which is difficult because most people don't even understand that they are entangled.

Maya Shakti, illusion. Because unless we forget our real position. So Maya Shakti makes us forget that I am servant of the Lord and I take the position of master, of enjoyer, try to maximize my enjoyment independent of the Lord, pursue various sensual pleasures and in the process get entangled or completely so to say come under the clutches of Maya. So that's why Krishna in the Gita says, 'Devi esha gunamai.' This Maya, for a person to come out of this Maya or illusion, it's not easy. Most of the people don't even understand Maya. Don't even understand that we are completely entangled. We are completely bound. We are in illusion. So first step when we start pursuing spiritual life, start reading Gita, the first thing which will dawn on us is that we are in Maya. Getting out of Maya, it takes time. It's a long process. Again it depends on how sincerely we practice the spiritual principles. To that extent we can experience relief from the entanglements from Maya. Now what role Maya plays?

I gave example of just like in a city you have a prison house. There are also amenities like educational department like hospitals, like schools and so many other things. So this Maya Shakti can also be compared to an examiner. Maya, just like in our schools and colleges we have an experience that when we were in a particular class in a school, we were we are taught different things. There is well defined curriculum. We study that classes are organized and at the end of a particular semester or a year an examination is conducted. An exam is conducted and only if you pass the exam you graduate to the next class. You get promoted to the next class in fourth class. And if he or she passes the exam, the final exam, the annual exam, although there are many so to say other exams, quarterly exams could be there or internals could be there. The final exam when a child passes, he gets promoted to the fourth class or the next higher standard. So here we are there in the material world and if you want to be the enjoyers independent of the Lord, we are here and an opportunity is given. You want to be an enjoyer, go ahead. You want to see beautiful forms, go ahead. You want to hear beautiful music, nothing do with connection with the Lord, go ahead, all the best. are adamant about it we want it okay go ahead a facility is created but just like a child who is very very i want this i want this father may say that it is not good for you but if the child is adamant the father okay go ahead what can i say what can i do so same way this world is created where there are a lot of illusory pleasures we have come to become the enjoyers take the position of the lord and fall prey to such illusory pleasures and get entangled or entrapped.

#### Examination, Qualifications, Spiritual World
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xAGKTxpgXiE?t=1909
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public

**Summary:** The speaker continues the analogy of Maya as an examiner, emphasizing that just as qualifications are needed for jobs, spiritual qualifications are needed to enter the spiritual world, and Maya tests us to ensure we are ready.

So like an examiner exam is given only if you pass you move to the next class there is no point in graduating Graduating a child or moving a child to the next standard, if he does not know the subject, if he is not conversant with what's taught in a particular class, if he has not improved in his level of understanding of physics, chemistry, math, so any particular subject which is being taught, there is no point in him to raise him to the next class. He will be a big failure. He will not be able to understand the curriculum which is there in the next class. So that's how the educational system works. We go to a particular level. We are taught different things. There is a well defined curriculum. We pass the exam. We move to the next level. We move to the next level. Next level. Next level. That's how we pass our 10th. We pass our 12th. We do our graduation. And then we are called so-called educated beings. Now while giving exams also, an interview is conducted. Okay, what is your qualification? What have you passed? Because different jobs require different qualification. For a person who has to do surgery in a hospital requires a qualification. The person has done MBBS or MD or MS. And only if he has that qualification, it makes sense for him to do surgery. Otherwise, without that qualification, there will be havoc in the hospital. So we have come in this world. Now we have completely forgotten the Lord. We are entitled to be blissful. We are entitled to love the Lord. We are entitled to be a display. We have this afterwards but we don't believe in the Lord.

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### VALUE YOUR LIFE - SUICIDE IS NOT A RIGHT OPTION! HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### suicide statistics, youth, mental health
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XZ7cdVB3HRM?t=570
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** General public
**Key Quote:** "Every 40 seconds a person is committing suicide, it's a big number."
**Summary:** The speaker presents alarming statistics about suicide, particularly among young people, highlighting the increasing rates and common methods used.

Suicide is the second leading cause of death in the world for those in the age group of 15 to 24 years. More teenagers and young adults die from suicide than from cancer, heart disease, AIDS, birth defects, stroke, pneumonia, influenza, and chronic lung diseases all put together. In this age group, it's the second leading cause for death when combined with all other so-called common causes of death. There is a 30 percent increase in the rate of death by suicide worldwide between the year 2000 and 2016. On one side, we are seeing that we are living in a technologically advanced world with a lot of innovation happening, a lot of R and D happening. We now have better amenities and better gadgets to communicate with our near and dear ones, but more and more people worldwide are feeling lonely, going through situations where they consider this as an option. Males take their own lives at nearly four times when compared to females. Of all the deaths by suicide, about 77 percent are males. Females are less prone to take this step of taking their own lives. Firearms are the most commonly used method of suicide among the males. 56% of males end up taking their own lives using firearms, more so in advanced countries where you can have a license and you can keep guns with you. Among the females, poisoning is the most commonly used method, and that is close to about 34% of females who end up committing suicide. The prevalence of suicidal thoughts, suicidal planning, and suicide attempts is significantly higher among the age group of 15 to 24 years. Teenagers are more prone to such kind of thoughts when compared to other age groups, and more so in the present time because there's a lot of flux, a lot of changes in the environment. Things are moving very fast, and at times for a person to cope up with those changes, with the pressures on the society, a person may end up taking his own life. Every year we hear so many people from very premier institutes like IITs and other very prominent colleges end up committing suicides. So these are few statistics which I thought I should share with all of you so that we understand it's a matter of great concern. 800,000 people are committing suicide every year. Every 40 seconds a person is committing suicide, it's a big number.

---
#### suffering, material world, Bhagavad Gita
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XZ7cdVB3HRM?t=866
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna in the Gita says that this material world is dukh halayam, it's a place of misery, and ashashvatam, it is temporary."
**Summary:** The speaker transitions to a scriptural perspective, questioning why a loving God would create a world filled with suffering, referencing the Bhagavad Gita's description of the material world as a temporary place of misery.

Now let's understand from the perspective of scriptures, from the Bhagavad Gita, why a person has to go through so much suffering, so much pain, so much despair that a person thinks that better let me just take my life, enough is enough. On one side we see that the Lord is our father, a loving father. He is all-powerful, he is all-knowing, he is all-loving. We are his children. Then why has the Lord created this world where there is so much pain and suffering? In fact, our day starts with a prayer called Mangala Aarti, where we sing eight stanzas glorifying the Guru. The prayer starts with Samsara Dawa Nalali Dhaloka. This material world is compared to a forest fire. Even in advanced countries like Australia and the US, miles and miles, acres and acres of forests are under fire. Such mighty countries with such resources, they are just helpless. Nobody goes and ignites forest fires. They just start all of a sudden, maybe because of friction or rubbing of trees. Likewise, this material world is also compared to a forest fire because although we wish that no miseries should land up in our lives, we make a lot of plans to be peaceful, to be happy, but suddenly from nowhere miseries land up in our lives and they are completely rattled, completely bewildered. That's the nature of this world. That's why Krishna in the Gita says sometimes we all have opinions, and our opinions can be wrong based on the experience we have had in the world so far. Somebody who has had a very wonderful life, has seen a lot of success with good people around him, everyone supporting him, good relatives, good times, he might have an opinion that this world is wonderful, everyone is so good. But at times people go through tough times, through crisis. At times even their near and dear ones desert them, nobody comes forward to help them, and having gone through such an experience, a person may conclude, may have an opinion that this world is very mean, people are very mean, it is not good for them, it is not a place for a gentleman to live. He can have an opinion like this, but let's understand what's the opinion of the one who has made this material world, who is the creator of this material world. If I were to use a laptop or anything which is from Microsoft, Microsoft engineers are the best to know about that product because they have created that product. So Krishna in the Gita says that this material world is dukh halayam, it's a place of misery, and ashashvatam, it is temporary.

---
#### problems, material world, perspective
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XZ7cdVB3HRM?t=1111
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "It is not that if you are educated, if you are staying in a country where good infrastructure is there, good resources are there, there is no poverty, there is no illiteracy, then people are all very happy and such occurrences will not happen. No."
**Summary:** The speaker shares an anecdote from a seminar with students from a premier institute, discussing the real problems of the world and highlighting that even in prosperous countries, mental health issues persist, suggesting that material solutions alone are insufficient.

Yes, somebody may go through a phase in life where everything is so nice, has a good job, good family, good children, economic prosperity, there is no scarcity, everything is going quite smooth, but then Krishna says it is ashashvatam, it is temporary. Krishna in the Gita says for one who has taken birth is sure to die, and for one who has left his body, who has died, is sure to take birth again. So this is the nature of this material world. I attended a meeting, a seminar, and in that seminar, I was addressing some of the students. This was way back in year 2016. Some students from a very premier institute had come to our temple in Bangalore. So I asked the students what is the real challenge which if we address, what is the real problem in this world which if we address, if we find a solution, although for many problems we don't have a magic solution, people have been battling with many problems for many many years, many many decades, poverty, illiteracy, diseases. So I asked them what according to you are the real problems of this world which if we address, the world will be a better place to live in. So one of them raised hand and said it's poverty, and another person says it's illiteracy because of illiteracy people get into this poverty cycle and they're not able to come out of it, and then some said corruption. So like that, intelligent students from a very good institute and they were coming up with some brilliant answers and their reasons to suggest that these are the real problems, logically speaking, rationally speaking, they had facts and figures to substantiate what they were saying, poverty, illiteracy, corruption. But we have examples of some countries who have very less poverty, a lot of people are literate, and the government policies are such, very less or negligible corruption. Most of the answers which I had got, there are countries where these problems are not existing or to a very small degree. For example, the US, although corruption may be there but compared to third world countries, poverty is less, corruption is less, systems and processes are there, a lot of discipline is there, but still I was reading an article, one person out of every five person in the US consults a psychiatrist. Why? There is no economic problem. They are prosperous, replete with resources, good infrastructure, and as I told you earlier, it is not that only rich people, only poor people suffer because of financial problems, because of debts, they end up taking this drastic step. We have examples of millionaires and billionaires also who have committed suicides. In fact that article says many of the psychiatrists themselves may be consulting a psychiatrist in turn because everyone out of every five person consults a psychiatrist. So it is not that if you are educated, if you are staying in a country where good infrastructure is there, good resources are there, there is no poverty, there is no illiteracy, then people are all very happy and such occurrences will not happen. No. This made us to think, in fact in that discussion I triggered this for people to think what are the real problems of life. We may say illiteracy is a problem, we may say poverty is a problem, but in spite of not having those as a problem, people are struggling. What are the real problems? That way our scriptures have done a wonderful analysis of what's happening in this world. Actually our great rishis, saintly personalities, self-realized souls have true understanding of the world. They are able to see things clearly. They are enlightened souls, and their eyes are open to the realities of this world. They are not in illusion. They are able to see things as they are. They have very clearly analyzed what are the real problems in this world. Problems also are relative.

---
#### problems, birth, death, suffering
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XZ7cdVB3HRM?t=1573
**Verse References:** BG 8.16
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So long as the soul is entangled in the meshes of birth, death, old age, and disease, punar api janma, punar api marna, we will go through a lot of ups and downs in this world."
**Summary:** The speaker defines a real problem as one that is universal, unavoidable, and unwanted, identifying birth, death, old age, and disease as such problems, and emphasizing the cyclical nature of suffering in the material world.

So what is a real problem? Our shastras have analyzed this. For a problem to be a real problem, it should have three characteristics. The first characteristic of a problem is that it should be common to everyone. Not just for Indian people, not just for males and not for females. It is for everyone. Rich people, poor people, everyone experiences the problem. That is one characteristic of a real problem. The second characteristic of a real problem is that no one can avoid it. No one can avoid it. And the third characteristic of the problem is that no one wants it. No one wants it. Everyone wants to avoid it, but it still happens. The problem is forced upon the person. No one avoids it and still no one can avoid it. So if we analyze all the problems that we know in life, some of those problems are temporary, they come and go. They are like changes in the season. But there are some problems that meet the three criteria. It is common to everyone. No one wants it, and still no one can avoid it. Although a person may be very rich, very educated, very intelligent, still cannot avoid it, even though he wants to not have that problem in his life. Cannot avoid it. And a verse says, Janma Mrityu Jara Vyadhi Dukha Doshanu Darshanam. Someone who can avoid it. This is in Gita. Someone who can avoid janma, birth, mrityu, death, jara, old age, vyadhi, diseases, is a real problem that we can find a solution for in this human life. That person is in knowledge. And what is the reason, the main reason? The main reason is janma, mrityu, death, jara, old age, because as a spirit soul we are eternal, we are never dying, we are sat, chit, and ananda. But we have got this material body, this deha, this yantra, which is undergoing this janma, mrityu, death, jara, old age. And we have to experience that because we have identified ourselves with the body. So, so long as we are in this material world which is called as samsara, davanala, like a forest fire, which is dukh halayam ashashvatam, so long as the soul is entangled in the meshes of birth, death, old age, and disease, punar api janma, punar api marna, we will go through a lot of ups and downs in this world. Sometime good time, sometime bad time. Sometimes very very bad time, sometimes very good times, we will be going through that flux. And a time can come when we will be so bewildered that we may even consider suicide as an option.

---
#### death, pain, attachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XZ7cdVB3HRM?t=1807
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "It's said that the death, at that point of death, the pain a person, the soul experiences is equivalent to 42,000 scorpions biting simultaneously."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the painful experience of death according to the Garuda Purana, comparing it to the pain of 42,000 scorpion bites, and uses a personal anecdote about losing a laptop to illustrate the pain of losing something dear, relating it to the soul's separation from all attachments at death.

Let me share with you another interesting fact. This is in Garuda Purana. Garuda Purana says that the moment of death, it's a very painful experience. Garuda Purana says that the amount of pain which a person goes through at the point of death is tantamount to, is equivalent to 42,000 scorpions biting a person at the same time. Most of us may not have an experience of a scorpion bite, but those of us who are from village background have stayed in villages, I'm sure might have had some experience. I was speaking to a person who has stayed quite some time in villages. So he was saying that yes, it's quite common in villages. And many times, many times, people also die because of scorpion bite. And it's also figured out that although there could be venom, it could be poisonous, that bite could be poisonous, but most of the time people don't die because of venom. They die because of intense pain because of that bite. Now imagine 42,000 scorpions biting simultaneously. That's the kind of pain a person goes through when he's leaving the body. Now somebody may say, how do we understand this? How can we come to this understanding that it's such a painful experience? Last year, I was traveling from Mumbai to Bangalore. And after we got down from the aircraft, I went to the restroom. I had a laptop bag which I had kept just outside. There was another person, gentleman, traveling with me. And when I came back, I took the laptop bag in my hand. I suddenly felt that it's quite light. The bag is very light. It's quite unusual. And then I opened the bag just to double check. Is the laptop there? And the laptop was not there. It was missing. I became very panicky. Imagine, laptop, we do a lot of things on the laptop. Emails and all kinds of work we do. And even I had not backed it up. The work was not backed up. So, imagine two years, two months, three months of hard work. Whatever efforts have gone in, it's there on the laptop and it's not there. And then I recollected that while I was in the aircraft, I did some work on the laptop. And I just took a little rest and I had kept the laptop in that pouch which is there, the seat in front of us. And before deboarding, I had forgotten to collect the laptop. Unlike a train, you know, it's come on the platform, you have forgotten something, you can again go back and you can pick up the things which you have. You can't go back. By the time we deboarded the plane, it's already 10-15 minutes. So, I became very panicky. In fact, I was in a lot of anxiety. A lot of pain. Imagine all the work which you have done last two months, three months. All the mails, all the work which you have done, all the documents you have created, it's all gone. Okay, laptop still you can buy a new one, but what about the work? What about all the data which is unsaved? And the person who was traveling next to me, with me, he said, Swamiji, please don't worry, don't worry. Swamiji who knows so much of philosophy, actually I couldn't help but be anxious. All of a sudden, from nowhere, this situation came up when I have to lose the laptop. So, he consoled me and then we went to that booth where Lost and Found and Indigo Airlines had this thing. And we told them that we have left the laptop in the aircraft. Suddenly, on a walkie-talkie, they could talk to the personnel there in the aircraft, cleaning staff and all that. And they confirmed that they have got the laptop. And then they asked me the model and the details and all that. And in 15-20 minutes, I got the laptop. But the point what I want to make is that pain was quite intense, having lost the laptop. Imagine, when compared to laptop, you go through a situation where all what you have achieved, all what you have done, all the relationship what you have had, all the time and energy you have invested in acquiring things, possessing things, it's being snatched away from you in no time. All relationships, all assets, all properties, all wealth, all name, all fame. Everything what you have is being taken away. How attached we are just to a small gadget, to another individual. We get so attached. Now, imagine a situation where the soul is so-called separated from all that. All the things, possessions are being taken away. It's such a painful experience. Although, yes, we may see a person while sleeping, leaving the body. That's a different thing. But the soul has to go through that pain. Externally, it may appear that the person was sleeping and the eyes were closed and he left the body. But the soul, death is not just that moment when a person leaves the body. The soul goes through an experience. A painful experience. Just like I gave an example. Something which is very dear. Something which you're very attached to. If it's taken away, it's a painful experience. Sometimes there's a death in the family. We are shattered for years together, for months together. So, it's said that the death, at that point of death, the pain a person, the soul experiences is equivalent to 42,000 scorpions biting simultaneously.

---
#### suicide, karma, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XZ7cdVB3HRM?t=2249
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "From the Vedic perspective, death is not a solution."
**Summary:** The speaker argues that suicide is a cowardly act, driven by the desire to escape pain, but from a Vedic perspective, death is not a solution, and devotees should remain humble and dependent on God.

Somebody commented, although the action of suicide, you can call it bravery in the sense that a person has the guts to go and jump from a cliff or shoot himself. Action may be brave, but the act is cowardly. Now, a person may go through, at times you would have seen, earlier used to have the trains in which you can pull the chain to stop the train, and there it's written, penalty for unauthorized pulling of chain, 300 rupees or one year in prison. Both options are painful, but a person generally chooses the less painful one. A person may just pay the fine and avoid going behind the bars. For some people who cannot pay the dues, cannot pay the penalty, probably they may choose going behind the bars. But the option is given. So when does a person commit suicide? When the pain, agony, the turmoil a person is going through is much, much more than the pain of death. For many people, death is a problem. When the world trend centers were attacked, people jumped from the 72nd floor. For many people, death is a problem. But somebody can go through a situation where death could be a solution. He feels it is better for me to just take away my life. Na rahega baans, na bajegi baans hoi. A person may think wrongly that if I die, all my problems will be finished. We have an example of Siddhartha, on the day of cafe-cafe. He just flew to the river, and two days later his body was found. So people may experience a lot of pain, a lot of fear, a lot of pressure, where people may think, okay, better for me to despair and take my own life. Now from the Vedic perspective, although Prima Facet looks like a person who kills himself, all his problems are gone. Now Siddhartha may not need to pay the dues, all his problems are gone. But from the Vedic perspective, death is not a solution. And it is very important for us to understand why death is not a solution. Because even we, as long as we are in the material world, today I may be speaking about great things, but yes, when kala experiences different things in life, there are people who are not able to sleep for a few days together. They have to take sleeping pills, so they are not able to sleep for a few days. And imagine someone who is not sleeping for three days, four days, how the system of the body and mind will be? Kala can experience such situations in someone's life. That way we cannot be too sure. That way we cannot, so sometimes we often put on the brave side with a positive attitude. Kala can experience something in our life where we can be really hurried. So, a devotee should always be very respectful, always dependent upon God, understanding Maya, Kala, the power of God is very strong. Not to be too sure, not to be too proud. Now I have money, now I have good friends, all resources, now I am safe. No, a devotee should always feel that unless I have the protection of God, unless I am in the shelter of God, the energies of the material are very strong. Every moment I can be besieged from the life that is called security.

---
#### relationship with God, free will, depression
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XZ7cdVB3HRM?t=3886
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Unless I have the protection of God, unless I am in the shelter of God, the energies of the material are very strong."
**Summary:** The speaker answers questions from the audience, emphasizing the importance of proactively cultivating a relationship with God, exercising free will to choose devotion, and recognizing God's impartiality in offering help.

If we do not utilize time and energy to utilize relationships, then maybe we will be hurried when there is a problem. So, if we go to God when there is a problem, then we have a problem. So, we have to be a little proactive. Not just go to God when there is a problem. Preferably, we should go to God, knowing that He is the Father that we love. Be careful with Him, following His instructions, taking His attention, hearing His words, His teachings, His instructions. Following His instructions in our life, and then our life will develop. So, you can download this prayer. Thank you. And give these prayers. And also, let us utilize that relationship, utilize our time and energy, so that we have a relationship with God. And when difficult times come, which are not possible, we can contact God, and seek His grace, seek His teachings, seek His strength, so that we can endure such times with a smile on our face. That is quite possible. So, we will take some questions. We have a question from Aryant Jain. Is evil one of the motivators of death? If yes, then why humans who are more evil than humans do not try? The main thing in humans is that we have free ability. Humans, in that way, free ability is relatively discarded. For example, we have a choice, what should we eat, what should we not eat. Humans, carnivores, means they can eat only meat. Harbivores, only eat soil. Because we have free ability, we suddenly combine our lives popularly. And as a result, we go through many... In the temptation to maximize free ability, we maximize our happiness. Eating, drinking, together, we take many things to maximize happiness in life. Even, do not stop doing wrong activities, wrong activities, criminal activities. And as a result, we combine our lives, even more than human life. Next question from Chanchal. Sah, no hatred has the right to be angry with God. Why does God not come to help people who are depressed, directly like he did with Arjuna? Why this happiness? Does God prefer Arjuna over others? Krishna in Gita says that He is the same with everyone. Namai dvesho satina pria. He is not happy with anyone. Arjuna chose to be the carrier of God. Actually, we all know that Arjuna was given an offer, whether you want me or want my army? Arjuna said, I want you. And then Krishna reminded, I will not fight, I will not hold hands. Arjuna said, still, I want you. So, at all, without knowledge, without intention, we choose not to have God in our lives. We choose to buy our time and energy in something else. Our knowledge for something else. Not directly, we indicate to God, I will think about my life. You think about your life, you think about your business. Alhamdulillah, we go to God only when we have a problem. And that is a problem. So, if we are a carrier like Arjuna, then God will definitely help us. He is not happy in that. If we surrender to God, He promises, He ensures that He will protect us. But, we have to take that step, because He has given us free truth.

#### suicide, enlightenment, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XZ7cdVB3HRM?t=4791
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The trend of suicide can be reduced if more and more people are enlightened, more and more people have access to this wonderful knowledge, more and more people take shelter of the Lord, more and more people become devotees of the Lord, definitely, for sure, this trend can reduce drastically."
**Summary:** The speaker answers questions from the audience, emphasizing the importance of proactively cultivating a relationship with God, exercising free will to choose devotion, and recognizing God's impartiality in offering help.

Can this trend of suicide be reduced? Also, whoever, who does it, what happens to their soul? As I mentioned, the soul continues to suffer the sinful reactions, and also the suffering can get aggravated because of the additional karmic reaction of having taken one's own life. It's a misuse of free will to put an end to one's own life. Untimely. So, the problem gets aggravated. So, can the trend of suicide be reduced? Yes, the trend of suicide can be reduced if more and more people are enlightened, more and more people have access to this wonderful knowledge, which Krishna has shared in the Gita. More and more people take shelter of the Lord, more and more people become devotees of the Lord, definitely, for sure, this trend can reduce drastically. In fact, Shri Prabhupada, when he went to America, he met a lot of eminent leaders, so there was a big challenge in the society because in spite of so much of police force, there was a lot of crime, especially in downtown areas and cities and all that. So, he said, Give me enough facility to distribute prasadam and for Harinam, chanting of the holy names, I can assure you that the crime will reduce. And in some pockets it was tried and definitely, yes, the crime did reduce because there is a change and transformation of heart. You know, this criminal mentality is there in the heart, which is impure, which is rebellious, which is the profession of the Lord. When we become purified of karma, krodha, loba, mada, matsarya, automatically crime will reduce.

#### limitations, expectations, prayer
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XZ7cdVB3HRM?t=5140
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So long as we are in this material world, janma mrityu jara vyadi is must."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the limitations of everyone in the material world, including powerful figures, and emphasizes the importance of having reasonable expectations when praying to the Lord, understanding that certain aspects of material existence, like birth, death, old age, and disease, are unavoidable.

So, all of us have limitations. Such limitations don't exist in the Lord because He is all-powerful. He is omnipotent. Now, somebody may say that a person in my family got infected or he was diseased and then we prayed to the Lord, but the Lord did not save him. Why the Lord did not save him? Was he not all-powerful to save that person? At times, we can be unreasonable in our expectations. So long as we are in this material world, janma mrityu jara vyadi is must. Now, when we are in this world, if we pray to Lord that please do not break the relationships, this expectation is unreasonable so long as we are in this world. Krishna in the Gita is giving us knowledge that one who has taken birth is sure to die. And for one who dies is sure to take birth again. If we are in ignorance, out of ignorance we have false expectations and when those expectations are not met, we become crestfallen, we become dejected, we become bewildered. So, that's why it is very important to have this knowledge. Then we can have the right expectations. For example, if a person says, Krishna, I should not die. I don't want to die. This prayer will not be fulfilled so long as we have this material body. Yes, as a soul, yes, we are eternal. But if we have that ignorance that I am the body and I am praying that I want to live. Even Hiranyakashipu prayed, he wanted to be eternal. He asked a benediction from Brahma ji. Brahma ji said, I am myself not immortal. How can I give you this benediction? So, indirectly he took the benediction that I should not die day or night by a man or an animal, by a weapon, by a land or in sky. But still, Hiranyakashipu was killed. So, that way many times our expectations are wrong. At times our prayers are unreasonable, not knowing the facts. And therefore we get dejected. Otherwise, Lord is all powerful. If we pray to the Lord sincerely and with knowledge, He can do anything. We have many many examples of Lord. But we should also not ask for some petty miracles. Krishna going to Him and like to solve our problems. We should not use Lord as an order carrier. So, that way genuine spirituality is completely different ball game. We are not supposed to use Lord as, you know, fulfiller of all our demands and needs.

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### Vedic Epics: Fact or Fiction? | HG Amitasana Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### Transcendental nature of Vedic epics, Reality vs. Mythology, Purpose of Lord's activities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gh6fq2NK_Zo?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Out of His causeless mercy only the Lord comes to this earth and leave behind His merciful activities so that the devotees may derive transcendental benefit."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the transcendental nature of Vedic epics like Ramayana and Mahabharata, contrasting them with the perception of them as mere mythology, and highlights the purpose of the Lord's activities in purifying and uplifting living entities.

So here Prabhupada is mentioning that all these auspicious narrations about the Lord, His activities, they are of transcendental nature. These are all not fictitious narrations. Many times people, when they narrate some stories from Ramayana, Mahabharata, they call it Indian mythology. Many who truly study the Vedas or even Ramayana, Mahabharata, actually may have an element of faithlessness where they believe that these are all mythology stories. No doubt they have important lessons, they have wisdom. So Srila Prabhupada very nicely, very faithfully, very diligently presented Bhaktivedanta Purports and instilled in us the conviction that these are all not fictitious stories. Bhagavatam is actually talking about the things which have happened in the past, actually happened. May not be in this planet earth, may not be in this yuga, because what we know of this world, what we know of ourselves is very small, insignificant portion of the reality. So if a person is humble, understands his true position, understands that life exists in all the planets, there are many many brahmandas, this universe is not all in all. There is a spiritual world, there is a material world. Understanding we get is our universe is just like a small mustard seed in a bag of mustard seeds. And these are all material universes, which is one fourth of the Lord's creation. So we are in that universe, one small insignificant, in that universe we have billions of galaxies. And the Milky Way, one galaxy in which we are part of, it's huge. Millions of stars, each of them having family of planets. So how small insignificant we are, how arrogant we could be to say that all these are all, life exists only here. We know about this world. We hardly know about our body, how it functions. Yesterday I was studying about, it was described in the camp which we attended, about the human digestive system. It's so complex, so complex. The way, the moment we put the food in the mouth, till it finally we evacuate. It's so complex, so many processes happening in different parts of the digestive system. So it's not just come on its own. There is Lord, He is a reality. He has designed this world for a purpose, He has designed us, we have come in this world for a purpose. So Prabhupada mentions that when the Lord comes in this material world, He enacts various pastimes, a narrative history which is transcendental as Himself. So when we hear about the Lord and His pastimes and His activities, we also rise above the modes because the nature of these activities are transcendental. They have the potency, they have the power to purify us and uplift us. We are soaked, we are entrapped in the lower modes, the modes of material nature which is binding us to this material world. So here Prabhupada mentions that every living entity of a superior quality as a spirit soul and nothing material can be auspicious for Him. In this material world we have our own concoction, this is auspicious, this is inauspicious, this is good, this is bad. So Prabhupada says, we being spirit soul, nothing material can be auspicious. So only those things which are of transcendental nature, the Lord, His activities, His holy name, His pastimes, the deities, prasadam. All these are all actually auspicious for the living entity. We are intelligent persons so therefore hear personally and cause others to hear the descriptive narrations of Lord's activities for that will destroy the pangs of material existence. Out of His causeless mercy only the Lord comes to this earth and leave behind His merciful activities so that the devotees may derive transcendental benefit.

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### WHAT IS HEAVEN & HELL? | HG Amitasana Das | GITA LIFE Live
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### heaven and hell, scriptural understanding, spiritual world
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9k_j_1KSDYg?t=30
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "For many people, Goloka, Vaikuntha, Swarga are all one and the same. So let's properly understand what is heaven and hell."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the topic of heaven and hell, referencing various scriptures and clarifying common misconceptions about spiritual destinations like Vaikuntha and Goloka.

Haray Krishna, a warm welcome to all of you for today's session. The topic for the day is Heaven and Hell. In all scriptures, we have some understanding of Swarga and Naraka, Heaven and Hell. So today we are going to discuss in detail, quoting from our scriptures, what is the right understanding, what exactly is Heaven and Hell. So before we get into the discussion, let us all together pray, seek the blessings of Srila Prabhupada, Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and Lord Sri Krishna, so that we can have a very fruitful, meaningful discussion and with a lot of practical day-to-day life applications, takeaways. So please join me in offering the prayers. Nama Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Srimate Bhaktivedanta Swamin Nithinamine Namaste Namaste Rama Rama Hare Hare. So today we will be talking about three important aspects in our discussion relating to heaven and hell. The first aspect is in all scriptures. I repeat, in all scriptures, be it Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavatam, be it Ramayana, Mahabharata, or for that matter, Bible, Quran, in all scriptures, there is mention of heaven and hell. The details may be different, the descriptions might be different, but the concept is there. Like, when I was studying, I studied in a convent school and we used to pray, 'Our Father in heaven, hallowed be thy name.' And then in Islam, Jannat, you've heard this word Jannat, it's referring to some kind of heaven. And we also have words like Vaikuntha, Goloka, and a lot of people get completely confused when we hear these words. For many people, Goloka, Vaikuntha, Swarga are all one and the same. So let's properly understand what is heaven and hell.

---
#### spiritual world, material world, planetary systems
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9k_j_1KSDYg?t=360
**Verse References:** BG 8.20
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The Svarga and Naraka is referring to the places of enjoyment or comforts in the material world."
**Summary:** The speaker differentiates between the spiritual and material worlds, explaining that heaven and hell are material realms with varying standards of living across different planets.

First and foremost, we should understand that, broadly speaking, there is the spiritual world. Krishna in the Gita says, 'There is another world, which is beyond this material world, which is vyakta and avyakta.' Vyakta means, it manifests. This world has come into being, and one day, it will get annihilated. That's why we have, for creation, Brahma, maintenance, Vishnu, and annihilation, Lord Shiva. We talk about Trinity in Hindu religion. So, vyakta, avyakta, sanathan... there is another world, which is beyond this material world and that is called the spiritual world. So Lord's creation is this material world, this material creation and there is another world which is ever-existing, eternal beyond this material world and Sanatana, that's the spiritual world. The Svarga and Naraka is referring to the places of enjoyment or comforts in the material world. For example, even on this planet earth, we have different countries offering different standards of living and prosperity to its citizens. For example, in America, the average man enjoys a better standard of living materially speaking, in terms of social security, in terms of comforts, in terms of amenities, infrastructure. And there are some poor countries where there is no even proper provision for drinking water, people have to walk miles for fetching water, no proper sanitation, people have to go to fields to evacuate things like that. So just like in this planet earth, we have some places which are very opulent and some places which are the standard of living is very bad, so likewise in this material world which has many many planets, 14 planetary systems we talk about. So there are some planets where the standard of living is very very high, Indra Loka, Brahma Loka, they are called heavenly planets or heavens. Just like here in this material world we have beautiful women, men enjoy the company of women, their apsaras there, far more beautiful celestial beauty and the standard of enjoyment is very very high. The lifespan is very high, the comforts are very high, the standard of enjoyment is very fine, people have very fine tastes. Just like even in this world we see people who are very very opulent and rich, they have very fine taste, taste for classical music, fine arts, they may buy a painting for 15 lakh rupees, they have very fine taste. Likewise, there is standard of enjoyment is very high in some planets in this material world which is called Swarga and just like we have third world countries and very poverty stricken countries, likewise there are some planets where the standard of living is very bad, people by and large suffer, there's a lot of pain, lot of misery, a lot of agony and such planets are called Naraka or hell. As I mentioned, different scriptures may call them by different name, nomenclature may vary, the details might vary but the concept is the same. So what we have now understood is we have the spiritual world which is far above the material world which is called like a spiritual world, the kingdom of heaven and we have we have the material world in which there is different standards of living, some planets where this standard of living is very high is called heaven and some planets where this lot of misery is called hell. Now who goes where?

---
#### karma, heaven and hell, material world, suffering
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9k_j_1KSDYg?t=665
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Whether we go to the heavenly planets or the hellish planets, although we may consider it as a promotion, but still they are part of this promotion of this material world and one is not absolved of one is not freed from the miseries of janma mritu jara vyadhi birth death old age and disease."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that pious activities lead to heavenly planets and sinful activities to hellish planets, but both are still within the material world and subject to birth, death, old age, and disease.

Now who goes where when a person is very very pious engages in lot of pious activities, punya. Punya means opening hospitals, serving the poor and needy, opening old age homes, these are all activities which are termed as pious activities or punya. When we accumulate lot of punya then we are promoted to heavenly planets to give an example that when we are having the right qualification we can like Indians always aspire to go to America if we have the visa if you have the right qualification if you can somehow manage to get the right kind of sponsorship or a job you can go to US and enjoy it if you have the right qualification if you can somehow manage to get the right kind of sponsorship or a job you can go to us and enjoy it. That standard of living and likewise if a person engages in a lot of sinful activities which is also called Papa a person will get degraded to hellish planets after his death so in human form of life depending on the choices we are making the activities we engage in whether it is Papa or Punya we get either promoted or we get demoted we can get promoted to heavenly planets we can get demoted to hellish planets or we can remain in this earthly planet but one very important point which we all have to make note of whether we go to the heavenly planets or the hellish planets although we may consider it as a promotion but still they are part of this promotion of this material world and one is not absolved of one is not freed from the miseries of janma mritu jara vyadhi birth death old age and disease.

#### death, suffering, material world

**Verse References:** —

**Key Quote:** "Even though a person may be very rich, he is still not absolved of janma, mrityu, jara, vyadi, birth, death, old age and disease."
**Summary:** Using the example of the US during COVID-19 and a company that arranges funerals, the speaker illustrates that even in prosperous countries, death and suffering are unavoidable.

A crude example even in America we know it's a very prosperous country but still there is death and it is very very obvious now during COVID times we're seeing there's a lot of death happening in the US although it is prosperous comparatively it has a very very wonderful medical infrastructure everyone has insurance policies government has Social Security still there's a lot of death more than six lakh people have died I don't know the latest numbers but yes it's quite huge so even though a person may be living in palatial bungalow with all the amenities with all the Comforts with good infrastructure high standard of living still old age is there death is there diseases are there and finally rebirth is there the other day I was speaking to a devotee friend of mine who was prior to joining the temple was working in a company it was a US company and they had operations in India about 600 odd people working from India and he was explaining to me what kind of job he was doing and you won't believe it it was it came out as a surprise to me that company which is into billions of dollars is all about the main product is where a person can go online and book what's going to happen to them after death in in other words how the cremation has to happen a person can choose the coffin the person can upload the list of people who have to be invited for the final rights can upload the list of all the people with the email IDs whom all have to be communicated upon after death because unlike India the children after 16 17 once they become teenagers they just move away and that's been there as part of their culture for ages now they may have something who knows the parents gets divorce the father marries someone else mother marry someone else so they are quite loosely knit when compared to India so they're not even sure whether the son would be there for the final cremation so since they have social security numbers they have so moment a person dies it is the message goes to this company and depending on what kind of package you have taken the entire thing is triggered they will come with the coffin the kind of coffin you have chosen the body will be taken the right kind of people will be Called they will be invited and all the arrangements for the final rights would be made by this company so the point here is even though a person may be very rich, he is still not absolved of janma, mrityu, jara, vyadi, birth, death, old age and disease.
