#### austerity, Krishna Prasadam, consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zAtcWy1Fz7g?t=790
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Srila Prabhupada has offered us a spiritual mind where we can, we also make very nice elaborate feast, offer to the Lord."
**Summary:** HG Chanchalapathi Dasa shares a conversation among devotees comparing Srila Prabhupada's offering of Krishna Prasadam with the austere practices of another spiritual organization.

I must share another very interesting conversation that happened among the devotees in our temple here in Bangalore, just a few months ago. Bangalore temple devotees, we were all talking. It was in some meeting. And then we strayed off into talking a few other topics other than the main topics in the meeting, formal temple meeting. The discussion came up about one other spiritual organization from our country, very well known. They also have temples around the world and a lot of followers. They also have a lot of their sadhus, they call them followers, their sadhus. The discussion came up that how these sadhus of this organization, I don't want to mention the organization, very respectable, nice people, well known to us also. These sadhus of that organization, they are very austere. We learned that what they do, they have a feast and they offer that on special days, you know, like Ram Navami or something. They have their offering and they make a feast. Finally, the sadhus in the ashram. The sadhus in the ashram, what they do is that there's a big feast. Now they mix all the feasts, daal, sabji, gulab jamun, khichdi, shaag, bonda, pakoda, whatever. Everything, they mix it up, mash it up like that. Then pour water and then drink it. So that, you know, they don't enjoy the feast. They enjoy their senses eating good food or something like that. Very impressive, isn't it? Very austere. When we were talking about this, one of the devotees in our meeting group said, Wow, these sadhus, they are so austere. Oh my God, they just can't even have a feast. They do this every day. They have a bowl, supposedly. Whatever it fills in that bowl, only that much they can eat. They can't even take more than that. You can't overeat. You have to, this kind of a mashed food you have to eat every day. Oh my God, what an austerity. One of the devotees who heard this very spontaneously said, My God, next time I see these sadhus, I'll offer my pranams to them and tell them, Wow, hats off to the austerity that you are practicing. Another devotee commented, Look at what Srila Prabhupada said. Srila Prabhupada is offering us. Srila Prabhupada has offered us a spiritual mind where we can, we also make very nice elaborate feast, offer to the Lord. We also relish Krishna Prasadam with all of the very gatedness. After having enjoyed this wonderful Krishna Prasadam, Srila Prabhupada is offering us a state of consciousness far, far higher than all of these other sadhus and spiritual organizations are offering. So, Unnata Ujjvala Rasa Swabhakti Shriyam. This is what the Acharyas have explained.

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public

**Summary:** HG Chanchalapathi Dasa emphasizes that Krishna consciousness is the highest state of consciousness, a treasure from the Vedic literatures brought out by Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, and that it is the duty of Indians to share this with the world.

Everyone said, yes, we should offer our lives to Srila Prabhupada. Even that's not enough to repay what Srila Prabhupada has given us. Krishna consciousness that Srila Prabhupada was giving, was teaching around the world was something, it's a most elevated state of consciousness that the Vedic literatures talk about. Unnata Ujjvala Rasa Swabhakti Shriyam. That is the treasure. This is a treasure in the Vedic literatures. That treasure in the Vedic literatures was not known to many people. Chaitanya Mahaprabhu brought out that treasure. He wanted people to experience that. It was Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's desire that this treasure from the Vedic literatures is shared with the whole world. Everywhere in the world there are seekers. There are people who are looking for spiritual truths, spiritual knowledge, spiritual understanding. It is the duty of Indians to share this wealth, this treasure from the Vedic literatures.

#### transcendental meditation, Krishna consciousness, preaching
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zAtcWy1Fz7g?t=1107
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "So many of Srila Prabhupada's young American followers, they were all, formerly they had had some kind of an exposure to Transcendental Meditation."
**Summary:** HG Chanchalapathi Dasa explains how Srila Prabhupada encountered Transcendental Meditation and Maharishi Mahesh Yogi in the West and how it was a common topic of discussion with his early followers.

When Srila Prabhupada went to America in 1965-66, one of the things that a common theme that Srila Prabhupada would come across was, there was one other spiritual organization from India, Maharshi Mahesh Yogi. He had popularized what was known as TM, Transcendental Meditation. So many of Srila Prabhupada's young American followers, they were all, formerly they had had some kind of an exposure to Transcendental Meditation. Invariably in the question-answer session with Srila Prabhupada, they would bring up these questions about TM, Transcendental Meditation, and what is meditation. All of these kind of topics would very often come. Srila Prabhupada was hearing about Transcendental Meditation and Maharshi, and he also had established many centers in Western countries, in America, in Europe, and other places. Prabhupada had heard about that quite often. A few other organizations like that, but definitely Maharshi, Mahesh Yogi's Transcendental Meditation was quite well known among the young Americans. Much later, Srila Prabhupada was in Atlanta temple. This was March of 1975. So you can imagine, 1966 the temple was, ISKCON was incorporated. Now we are talking about 1975. Nine years have passed. ISKCON has spread around the world. Prabhupada has so many followers around the world. Prabhupada had come to Atlanta temple. Devotees brought one young American, who had become a devotee. They introduced him to Srila Prabhupada. They said, Prabhupada, Srila Prabhupada, this is Doug. An American name, common name, Doug. This is Doug. He has become a devotee of Krishna now. He is in the temple now. He was closely associated with the Mahesh Yogi's philosophy. He was, for two years, his personal secretary. Traveling with him, being with him, helping him in all his different activities and projects and all of that. Maharshi had created an executive council to help him reach out to the universities and create courses and all of those kind of things. That council was called the 108. It's not that it had 108 people, but it was called the 108. All of these kind of topics would very often come. Srila Prabhupada was hearing about transcendental meditation and Maharshi. He also had established many centers in Western countries, in America, in Europe and other places. Prabhupada had heard about that quite often. A few other organizations like that. But definitely Maharshi, Mahesh Yogi's transcendental meditation was quite well known among the young Americans.

---
#### Krishna consciousness, transcendental meditation, conversion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zAtcWy1Fz7g?t=1339
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "The highest state of consciousness is Krishna consciousness."
**Summary:** HG Chanchalapathi Dasa continues the story of Doug, the former secretary of Maharishi Mahesh Yogi, and how he became a devotee of Krishna after hearing Maharishi say that the highest state of consciousness is Krishna consciousness.

Much later, Srila Prabhupada was in Atlanta temple. This was March of 1975. So you can imagine 1966, the temple was, ISKCON was incorporated. Now we are talking about 1975. Nine years have passed. ISKCON has spread around the world. Prabhupada has so many followers around the world. Prabhupada had come to Atlanta temple. Devotees brought one young American who had become a devotee. They introduced him to Srila Prabhupada. They said, Srila Prabhupada, this is Doug. An American name, common name, Doug. This is Doug. He has become a devotee of Krishna now. He is in the temple now. Formerly he was closely associated with the Maharshi Yogi's philosophy. He was, for two years his personal secretary, traveling with him, being with him, helping him in all his different activities and projects and all of that. Maharshi had created an executive council to help him reach out to the universities and create courses and all of those kind of things. That council was called the 108. It's not that it had 108 people, but it was called the 108. Doug was a member of, one of the members in that 108 executive council and all of those kind of things. Prabhupada heard about that one and then after a little while of some discussion, Prabhupada became interested. So, how long you have been with Maharshi Yogi? Then he called, what is the highest, what have you understood from the meditation and Brahma Sutras and all of that? What is that? What is the highest state of consciousness? Because Maharshi always talked about states of consciousness and highest states of consciousness, cosmic consciousness and all of that. He asked him, what have you found out from your meditation in the mountains and meditation and notes on the Brahma Sutras? Maharshi, Mahesh Yogi, he said, the highest state of consciousness is Krishna consciousness. The followers were all shocked and Doug was shocked to hear that. After a little while, he started moving away and then he got in touch with some devotees and got to read Srila Prabhupada's books and he became kind of a devotee and then he had joined the group. He went to the temple and all of that had happened. Prabhupada very interestingly heard the whole thing and how Mahesh Yogi had, Maharshi had mentioned that the highest state of consciousness was Krishna consciousness. It is a fact, somehow it was maybe good fortune that Mahesh Maharshi also had realized that. Of course there was some discussion that whether Prabhupada should meet him and they could organize that meeting and all of that. Prabhupada sort of encouraged. He said, yes, we can. But then he has already popularized his process, his method. We don't know whether, you know, anything fruitful will come out of this meeting and nothing happened about it. Like this, we can see how Srila Prabhupada was creating this kind of an impact and influence of Krishna consciousness all over the world.

#### mission, compassion, sacrifice
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zAtcWy1Fz7g?t=1580
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "He has asked me to spread Krishna consciousness all over the world and I am completely helpless and I don't know how I can ever do this."
**Summary:** HG Chanchalapathi Dasa begins to explain Srila Prabhupada's motivation for spreading Krishna consciousness by recounting a story from the 1980s about a Bengali gentleman who had witnessed Srila Prabhupada's early devotion.

Now, it's interesting. What was Prabhupada doing? Why was he doing all of these things? Spread, setting up Krishna temples and writing books and having so many followers and traveling around the world and all of these kind of things. We will get a little bit of an understanding of what was, the background from one interesting story that happened. This was in the 1980s. I remember I was a young devotee in 1980s. I was in college and I was sticking to Krishna consciousness. There used to be a magazine, even now we have a magazine in ISKCON called The Back to Godhead. It was a very colorful magazine in the 1980s. It's not exactly the same format it is now. At that time, Srila Prabhupada's biography was just coming out. Only the first volume or maybe the second volume was released around that time. An excerpt of that, of the Leela Amrita was coming in a serialized form in the Back to Godhead magazine in those days. There was an article called A Lifetime in Preparation. It's the, first volume of Srila Prabhupada's Leela Amrita. We know that. There was an article on that book. One day in the ISKCON Mayapur Chandradaya Mandir, you should remember 1980s, it's not exactly the way it is now. Those days it was a small temple, big temple hall, but not the big palace, the big palatial temple that's coming up now. It was in a different situation. There was only Radha Madhav deities those days. The Ashtasakhis had not come. They came a little later.

#### devotion, mission, humility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zAtcWy1Fz7g?t=1707
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "My name is Bhaktivedanta Swami and I have been ordered by my revered spiritual master Bhaktivedanta Saraswati Goswami Maharaj and he has given me an impossible mission to accomplish."
**Summary:** HG Chanchalapathi Dasa narrates the story of a Bengali gentleman from Shantipur who recognized Srila Prabhupada's devotion and heard him express his mission to spread Krishna consciousness worldwide.

One Bengali gentleman, he came to the temple in Mayapur. He was holding this Back to Godhead magazine. He called a few devotees and he said, I want to talk to somebody about. The few devotees gathered around him. He started telling the story. He was from a temple in Shantipur. Shantipur is a small town when you travel from Calcutta to Mayapur on the highway. A little away from the highway is a small town called Shantipur. That is where Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's senior associate Advaita Acharya used to live. That was the residence of Advaita Acharya in Shantipur. Even now there is a temple of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and Advaita Acharya. This particular Bengali devotee who had come from Shantipur, he was one of the Sevaite, meaning one of those who render services there and one of the priests there. He was from that temple. He started telling, he told to these devotees, ISKCON devotees in Mayapur temple. He said that in the 1940s and 1950s I was there at that time too in the Shantipur temple and I was a Poochari. Now and then I would see one person about middle aged person 1940-1950 and he would wear a khadi dhoti and a khadi kurta and he would sit at the far side of the rear side of the temple far away from the altar and I would see him chanting very very intently absorbed in chanting Hare Krishna and he would chant alone. He wouldn't talk much to anybody. He would come on the weekends and he would be there for a few hours and then he would be seen sitting and very intently chanting, chanting. As a Poojari he would move around and then he would see and then he also saw that there were times when he was he saw this Bengali gentleman in khadi dress white dress and he would be chanting and there were tears in his eyes as he is chanting and he wouldn't disturb him because he was silently sitting and chanting. Just before he left after his chanting he would come and meet the meet him and with folded hands and then and appreciate him that he was doing puja here in this temple very important temple. I also wouldn't talk too much because he looked like he wanted to be to himself and then he would go away. Every now and then forties fifties he would see this elderly this middle aged person come and chant for a few hours and with tears in his eyes very feelingly chant and go away. Then for some time he stopped seeing him he was not coming at all and then he remembers it was August of 1965 so this sevait pujari he was doing his puja he was attending to the temple and suddenly he saw at the far end of the temple once again that same person has come but now he is in saffron robes and so he saw him and then he was sitting not in white khadi kurta and khadi dhoti but now he is in saffron dress and he was chanting chanting chanting and on that particular day he chanted for a long time and then so he didn't disturb him and then he came after the chanting he came and he paid obeisance to the deities there and he paid obeisance unusually long obeisance and then as a pujari he was watching all that and then he talked to him and then he spoke to him and he said yes I see you are the same person who used to come before and then he asked him what is your name who are you and then that saffron clad sadhu sanyasi he said my name is my name is Bhaktivedanta Swami and I have been ordered by my revered spiritual master Bhaktivedanta Saraswati Goswami Maharaj and he has given me an impossible mission to accomplish he has asked me to spread Krishna consciousness all over the world and I am completely helpless and I don't know how I can ever do this and I come here because this is the this is an important place Advaita Acharya's home this is where Chaitanya Maha Prabhu Nithyananda Prabhu and Advaita Acharya all three of them would sit here in this house and they would be scheming and planning how to spread love of Krishna everywhere in the world this is where the discussions would happen they would be planning how we can inundate the world with love of Krishna and so this place is very special so I come here and chant praying for their mercy praying for their direction how I can ever accomplish this impossible mission my Guru Maharaj has asked me to do to spread Krishna consciousness all over the world and he said tomorrow from Calcutta I am boarding a a ship and I am going in a ship to America.

I wanted to come here seek the blessings of Advaita Acharya and so I came here today and then he offered his obeisance to that Pujari and the Pujari said no no no you are a sanyasi you are how can you pay obeisance to me and this is the highest treasure in the Vedic literature this is the highest state of consciousness that one can ever achieve and this was this is the treasure in the Vedic literature brought out by Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and it was the desire of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and the Acharyas that this should go all over the world and now you see how Srila Prabhupada for preparing himself for so long according to the will of the Lord and then in 1965 going to America and going to the park and chanting Hare Krishna attracting the young Americans cooking chapatis for them 120 chapatis and making delicious prasadam giving to them going from continent to continent and going to Africa and there getting the Africans to become devotees to make them chant Hare Krishna this is an incredible story of Srila Prabhupada isn't it what a great Acharya how much he wanted to sacrifice how much he sacrificed to spread Krishna Bhakti all over the world this is the story of Srila Prabhupada an ever inspiring story and all of the things that Srila Prabhupada is so wonderful so it is not just another sadhu innumerables there are of course very respectable sadhus in our country but Srila Prabhupada the knowledge he had the message he carried the Bhakti that he had for Krishna that he wanted to share and the compassion he showed and the sacrifices he made still sticking on to all of the principles strictly as per the Vedic culture and spread Krishna Bhakti around the world. Srila Prabhupada Ki Jai. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

#### introduction, Srila Prabhupada, ISKCON

Hare Krishna Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Paschatya Deshatarine Jaya Shri Krishna Chetanya Prabhu Nityananda Shri Advaita Gadadhara Shri Vasadi Gaurabhatta Vrindha Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Very happy to be with all of you to share more stories about Srila Prabhupada's wonderful life. Some years ago I visited one Madhava Sanskrit scholar in Bangalore, and I wanted to talk to him about the issue in ISKCON, a major issue in ISKCON

---
#### Diksha, initiation, Madhva tradition
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vIdSB_kWFg4?t=114
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Diksha varies from tradition to tradition and a particular situation or a certain purpose."
**Summary:** The speaker recounts his conversation with a Madhava scholar about the different types of Diksha (initiation) in the Madhva tradition, leading him to reflect on the meaning of Diksha within ISKCON.

about initiation. So I went to him and asked him, what is Diksha or initiation according to Madhva tradition? So he thought for a moment and he said, Diksha, which Diksha are you talking about? So then I asked him, oh you mean to say which Diksha, there are more than one Diksha? Then he said, yes, we have a Matha Diksha, where you get initiated into a certain Matha, you are affiliated to a certain Matha. So we have something like that. We have Ashram Diksha, when somebody enters Sanya's Ashram, he takes Diksha and so that's called, there is a Diksha around that. Then he said, there is something called Vratha Diksha, when you take a Vratha, when you perform a Vratha, a vow, that for the next 21 days I will do this or something like that. There is a Diksha where you get initiated into that Vratha. And like this, there are many kinds of Diksha, which Diksha are you referring to? Because Diksha varies from tradition to tradition and a particular situation or a certain purpose. So then I started thinking, yeah, that's true. Diksha is an ancient Sanskrit Vedic cultural concept in Vedic culture. But what are we referring to when we talk in ISKCON about Diksha, Diksha Guru, who is the Diksha Guru and all of these kinds of things?

---
#### Srila Prabhupada, ISKCON, Diksha
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vIdSB_kWFg4?t=237
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So the best way is to understand what Srila Prabhupada had in mind when he talked about Diksha or even more how Srila Prabhupada articulated the meaning and the understanding of Diksha and how he implemented the practice of Diksha in ISKCON."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of understanding Srila Prabhupada's intent and practice regarding Diksha within the context of ISKCON, considering his role as a representative of an ancient spiritual tradition.

Then I started reflecting, we know how Srila Prabhupada representing a very ancient traditions beginning from Krishna to Narada, Krishna to Brahma to Narada to Vyasa and then the Acharyas and then Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, and he was representing that very, very ancient tradition. So I started reflecting on a very important and spiritual tradition. And now he was going to America, and he was founding a worldwide organization. And in the context of this worldwide organization, Srila Prabhupada was introducing all of these important spiritual concepts. And we know Srila Prabhupada, an empowered Acharyas, pure devotees. Absolutely. Krishna doing all of these things. So when Srila Prabhupada introduced Diksha in ISKCON, what did he have in mind? What was the meaning? What was the purport? What was the intent? And what was the practice that Srila Prabhupada was introducing? Because all that is relevant. We are talking about problem, about Diksha, Diksha Guru in ISKCON, not in isolation of ISKCON. It is in our institutional life, it is in our spiritual life, in the institution that Srila Prabhupada founded. So we can't take some esoteric, some Sanskrit shloka or some other tradition and try to understand what was, what is Diksha in ISKCON. And when we come back to this, I will start this. So when we come back, in our context, in ISKCON's context. So the best way is to understand what Srila Prabhupada had in mind when he talked about Diksha or even more how Srila Prabhupada articulated the meaning and the understanding of Diksha and how he implemented the practice of Diksha

**Key Quote:** "So it's a it's a store it's a shop there are many shops all around and this is also one shop if you use the Indian English language so it was a big hall it could be like a you know some kind of any kind of a store that is possible there and it was narrow and longish and then at the back there was one washroom and then there was another door there at the back the front is a big door as wide as the width of the door of the store and for some reason I also not figured out Americans use the word storefront for that"
**Summary:** The speaker discusses Srila Prabhupada's establishment of the first ISKCON temple at 26 2nd Avenue in New York City, describing the storefront and its significance.

in ISKCON. So what is significant is then I began to think that let's see how Srila Prabhupada introduced Diksha the first time he did and maybe several times after that when Srila Prabhupada presided over the Diksha ceremony, how would he describe that or how would Srila Prabhupada talk about that during lectures or conversations and such? So it's interesting how Srila Prabhupada introduced the first Diksha ceremony, initiation ceremony. It happened in September of 1966. Let's give a little background to it. Srila Prabhupada had established the first ISKCON temple and he had incorporated the ISKCON Society in July of 1966. That's when Srila Prabhutada had a fixed place to stay. Earlier He was moving from one place to another, sometimes sharing a room with another, sharing a loft with someone else and sharing the premises with another Yoga teacher and all of those activities and other things. Now he had a place for himself and that was 26 2nd Avenue which became the famous first ISKCON temple in ISKCON. So Srila Prabhupada had this storefront. As an Indian when I used to read Srila Prabhupada's biography that Prabhupada had a storefront, had rented a storefront and converted that into a temple I used to wonder what is a storefront you know we for Indians in Indian English language and our urban understanding we don't know what is a storefront and this was in the 1980s you know at least maybe now modern day young Americans, young Indians educated Indians may have an understanding but I definitely didn't have. I used to wonder what's a storefront but then when you read something you may not understand but you keep going on so that was what used to happen to me till at some point I visited the storefront on 26 second Avenue so it's a it's a store it's a shop there are many shops all around and this is also one shop if you use the Indian English language so it was a big hall it could be like a you know some kind of any kind of a store that is possible there and it was narrow and longish and then at the back there was one washroom and then there was another door there at the back the front is a big door as wide as the width of the door of the store and for some reason I also not figured out Americans use the word storefront

for that and so Srila Prabhupada had rented that place and he would conduct programs there where these young Americans who are looking for new life new meaning of life trying to understand Eastern philosophy Indian philosophy Buddhism and who are also experimenting on drugs for some higher consciousness experience all of these kind of young people would come there and Srila Prabhupada would conduct bhajans kirtans and he would give a class and Prabhupad was living in an apartment on the first floor just behind So Prabhupada would go through that rear door and then it would go to a courtyard and there is a flight of steps which would go to one flat in that apartment one and that place was so large so the room would be come up you know to their back one and that place would be before them and the rest was just in aultimate size that's where Prabhupada used to live. So Prabhupada used to live there, cook there, he would bathe there, there was a washroom and all of that and he would come here and conduct classes, kirtans and all of that and would go back. This was the kind of a situation that existed at that time. So this happened in July, July 13th, 14th when the incorporation of ISKCON happened. Now we are talking about July, August, September, not very long, just about two and a half, three months, little over two months. So Srila Prabhupada would conduct classes and then in one of the classes and there were a few people, young American boys and girls who were coming and they were trying to understand what Swamiji, they used to call Srila Prabhupada Swamiji at that time, what Swamiji is going to tell. And they would go meet Swamiji in the apartment also, where there would be a little more intimate conversations. They would ask questions and ask Swamiji about his life, about India and all their questions about philosophy and all of those kind of intimate discussions would happen up there in the apartment. And this was more formal and many newcomers would come. And Srila Prabhupada, we talked, about how he would go from here to the Tompkins Square Park and have kirtans on Sundays and all of those kind of things. So one day, actually he had spoken informally in the apartment, it looks like, and about initiation and his plan to conduct initiation. #### Krishna Janmashtami, fasting, Bhagavad Gita
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vIdSB_kWFg4?t=773
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So it was brilliant answer because Prabhupada did answer but increased the level of curiosity for all the young people."
**Summary:** The speaker recounts Srila Prabhupada's announcement of Krishna Janmashtami and the upcoming initiation, including the instructions for fasting and the use of Dr. Radhakrishnan's Bhagavad Gita.

communicator of Krishna conscious philosophy, his answer was, I will tell you later. So it was brilliant answer because Prabhupada did answer but increased the level of curiosity for all the young people. So it was brilliant answer because Prabhupada did answer but increased the level of curiosity for all the young people who are coming there. So they all were waiting to know what is Swamiji going to tell about initiation, but he said, I will tell you at the right time. I will tell you later. So they were even more curious and then Prabhupada had to know, had pointed out September 8th, 1966, is Krishna Janmashtami, the day Krishna appeared on earth 5000 years ago. So very important. Srila Prabhupada is telling about Krishna and we are supposed to become devotees of Krishna, bhakti to Krishna and the movement is called, the society is called International Society for Krishna Consciousness. So Krishna is so important and Krishna appearance day. So Prabhupada said, on that day we should chant more, we should hear about Krishna and we should fast till midnight. At midnight Krishna appeared and we will have a feast then. And so you can imagine for these young Americans, boys and girls who are coming there, just given off drug, some of them, some of them still taking drugs maybe, some of them had become vegetarians, some of them had not yet become vegetarians probably, all kinds of young American boys and girls and they are hearing from Swamiji about all these things and then they are told that they have to fast whole day. And then Prabhupada was very considerate. He said, if you find fasting becoming too difficult, you can go up to the apartment, there I'll have some fruits. You can have a fruit there. So this was the concession that Srila Prabhupada had given. So on the day of Krishna Janmashtami, Srila Prabhupada came down and he conducted the class. Of course there was another interesting thing that happened on that particular day. Usually Srila Prabhupada would speak on Bhagavad Gita and at that time Srila Prabhupada's own Bhagavad Gita was available. There was Bhagavatam available, but he would be speaking on Bhagavad Gita verses and he would use Dr. Radhakrishnan's Bhagavad Gita, Oxford University Press publication. So Prabhupada said Dr. Radhakrishnan's Bhagavad Gita was 90% accurate, but Prabhupada had warned that somehow in Dr. Radhakrishnan's explanations, he would go into personal understanding of the absolute truth. So I won't go into that analysis, I will do that another occasion and how Srila Prabhupada explained that.

#### Bhagavad Gita, initiation, beads
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vIdSB_kWFg4?t=974
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So Swami Prabhupada was a little amused and very happy to hear that."
**Summary:** The speaker describes how Srila Prabhupada read from his own Bhagavad Gita manuscript and announced the initiation ceremony for the day after Janmashtami, also mentioning the making of the beads.

There are very, very interesting stories around that, we'll keep it for another occasion. So Prabhupada would read from the Bhagavad Gita of Dr. Radhakrishnan and then he would explain. But then on that particular day, Prabhupada was actually working on his own Bhagavad Gita. He was typing the manuscripts, all that was also going on. So one of the young American students who was, we may not call him disciple at this point of time, they were not formal disciples, they were just eager to know and associate with Swamiji, they were coming, they were inquisitive, they were curious, they were very sincere, they really wanted to know what's all this about and they were coming to understand more. So they asked Swamiji, can you read from your manuscript? We have heard that you are preparing the Bhagavad Gita. So Swami Prabhupada was a little amused and very happy to hear that. And so he had one of the boys go up to the apartment, bring the bundle and they brought the bundle and then Prabhupada read for the next one hour translations and the purports that he had compiled for a long, for a certain number of, about an hour or so. And then at the end of the talk, Prabhupada said, and Prabhupada had announced already, the next day after Janmashtami, September 8th, 1966 was Janmashtami, September 9th was going to be the initiation day. Srila Prabhupada didn't tell what is significant about the next day of Janmashtami. We all know now today, that was the day Srila Prabhupada appeared. He didn't talk about that one, at least it looks like from the biographical information that I have read about. And it was supposed to be the next day of Krishna Janmashtami. So Srila, so everybody knew that next day was going to be the initiation, the beads were bought. And Srila Prabhupada said you should get beads from a store and told, some of the women who were coming, one of them was Jan, Mike's girlfriend, and also supposed to be, who have given their name for the first initiation. So Prabhupada said, or rather we will say, Swamiji said, women are patient, they have patience, and they should make the beads. And so Jan had taken it upon herself, maybe someone else also helped her, and she was going to make the beads for her. And the beads would have 108, the mala would have the 108 beads, beads bought from a store in America, some American store. And so it was not tulasi beads and all of those kind, you know where you don't have those kind of things at that time.

And Swamiji looked so effulgent and so confident and there is a certain kind of a, you know, you can trust Swamiji. So there was a kind of a confidence in Swamiji and so they would ask him those kind of questions that they would come across. #### Vaishnava tradition, servant of God, philosophy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vIdSB_kWFg4?t=1312
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "If someone says he is God, he is not God."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Srila Prabhupada's strong refutation of the idea that one can become God through meditation, emphasizing the Vaishnava understanding of being a servant of God.

and should be asked. Srila Prabhupada would strongly speak about this. This is a philosophy that's not explained in the Gita and not explained in the Vaishnava tradition. According to the Vaishnava tradition, according to the Bhagavad Gita, we are all part and parcel of Krishna. We are servant of Krishna. Krishna is the Supreme Master. Prabhupada often quoted verses from the Upanishads, Nithyo Nithyanam, Chetanas Chetananam, Eko Bahunam, Yo Vidha Dati Kamar, Katha Upanishad, where it says that the living entities are many and there is the Lord who is one, both of them are eternal and they are eternally the same. The Lord will eternally be the Lord and the living entity part and parcel will eternally be the part and parcel. The Lord is conscious, the living entity is conscious, Chetanas Chetananam, Nithyo Nithyanam, Chetanas Chetananam, eternal and eternal, Eko Bahunam, Yo Vidha Dati Kamar and that one is the provider of everything that the many conscious eternal entities want or desire. So one is the supplier, one is the receiver. The supplier, the receiver cannot become the supplier, he is always eternally the receiver. So these kind of ideas Srila Prabhupada had often talked about and then Prabhupada would often say. If somebody says, I am God, you know, it's an absurd statement. I realize God, I meditate on God and I become God. You know what God is? God controls the planetary movements. And I start controlling the planetary movements. What kind of an absurdity that is? Is the sun going to listen to me? Unless I am some insane guy thinking that I have become God, now I have God realized and I'm controlling all the stars and the planets and the moon and all of those kind of things. How absurd it is. So Prabhupada would strongly speak against this kind of an understanding and say, no, from the Bhagavad Gita, from the teachings of the Vedic literatures, we understand we are not God. We are servant of God. We are of the quality of God, but not God. God is God. We are always his part, amsa, mamai vamsho jiva loke, jivabhuta sanatana. This is Krishna's instruction in the Gita. Sanatana we are amsa, eternally we are amsa. And his amsa, he is the purna, he is the full. So this is the philosophy Prabhupada often would talk. And then sometimes, very often these questions would come about. Someone would come and declare that I have been practicing yoga for the last one year. And I have become God. You know, all those kind of things that these kind of people would come and declare. So Prabhupada would strongly say, if someone says he is God, he is not God. He is the opposite. Not G-O-D. He is D-O-G. You know, these kind of very, very strong themes that Prabhupada would talk about. In all of his classes. And so these young Americans, they would hear about all these things and then oh, Swamiji says that we are not God. We are actually servant of God and all of those kind of things. So it was one of the popular themes that Srila Prabhupada had repeatedly stressed in his classes, in his discussions and question answers and all of that.

they were looking at each other. Did you hear that? And they said, yes. Another one said, Swamiji just dropped a bomb. Another said, yes, it was mind blowing. And then they started discussing. important foundational principles. I must go forward quickly. So, Srila Prabhupada had described this as the initiation. The spiritual master accepts the disciple and agrees to deliver. Okay. What is the meaning of Deliver? That is also very nicely explained in the Acharyas which we sing every day in the morning in our temples. Yahara Prasade Bhai Yeh Bhava Toriyai Krishna Prapti Ho Yahahoy Te It is by the mercy of the spiritual master one can cross over the material world. Yahara Prasade Bhai Yeh Bhava Toriyai. Bhava means material world, the world of birth and death. Yeh Bhava Toriyai Krishna Prapti Ho Yahahoy Te. One can cross over the material world and he can deliver Krishna to the disciple. This is the meaning of Deliverance and it happens by the mercy of the spiritual master. This is what the spiritual master is agreeing. I will do that to you. I will deliver you from this material world and I will deliver Krishna to you. Provided what is that the disciple has to do? The disciple accepts the spiritual master and agrees to worship him as God or as good as God, as a representative of God with the same respect due to God.

Because he is a confidential servant of God. So this was the wonderful explanation Srila Prabhupada gave on that day and then the next morning and all these there were ten men and one woman. They all joined. It didn't happen in the storefront. It happened in the first floor of that house, the room, on the living room where Prabhupada was sitting was living there and there Prabhupada had created a mound of earth and then someone had brought orange crate you know wooden crates are there they had brought that one broken that up and that was supposed to be the wood you know in India when we do yagna we get special Samit wood there what can Swamiji get he could the best he could get was orange crate broken that up and then Swamiji had arranged and and there were some bananas and then some flowers and all the decoration and then Swamiji told all those everybody was sitting around each other and none of them were shaved up they were not even wearing dhoti kurta they were in their American attire and they sat there and then Swamiji said we will all chant and then they all chanted one round Hare Krishna mantra and then Swamiji said started chanting oh all of you repeat after me and they were all you know this young they were all there figuring it out. figuring out what's going on here. Then Swamiji said, Om, chant after me and everyone said Om. Apavitraha, everyone for first time hearing all these complex Sanskrit words. Om apavitraha pavitrova sarvavastho gatopiva yasmaret pundari kaksham bahyantara abhyantara sucihi sri vishnu. Made them chant all those kind of things. They were trying to figure out and then Swamiji said, now one by one can come and then he would give hand over the mala and then he would give them a name. This was another thing. And then Swamiji said, you will get a new name. And the name will end, it will be a name of Krishna and the name will end with Dasa or Dasi for men or women, which means you are a servant of Krishna.

#### first initiation, fire sacrifice, cleaning up
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vIdSB_kWFg4?t=2291
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Swamiji was alone to clean up the whole place."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the description of the first initiation ceremony, including the fire sacrifice, the devotees' concerns about the fire department, and Srila Prabhupada's explanation and subsequent cleanup.

This was all new for these young Americans. It must have been a wonderful experience. And then after that Prabhupada Swamiji gave names. Hovar became Hayagriva Dasa. Vali became Umapati Dasa. Mike Mike became Mukunda Dasa. Jan One, the lone American woman who was there, she became Janaki Devi Dasi. So like this all of them got the name and then still Srila Prabhupada, Swamiji lit them, lit the fire, sacrificial fire and then Swamiji would chant some mantras and then Swaha, then they would throw all those different grains into the fire and the whole ceremony and then everybody was wondering with billowing smoke. Oh my God, will the fire department show up now? Will the firemen, if they come and see, what are we going to tell them? And everybody was looking at each other and were worried. Swamiji was not concerned. He went on with his Swaha and the chanting and all of that and then it all ended. Everyone was, you know, it was like a new experience and they would call each other very self-consciously with their new names. They were trying to figure out and then all of them at the end of the ceremony then there it was and Prabhupada gave a small talk, Swamiji gave a talk and again he explained what it means to worship him, regard him as God and as good as God, respect due to God and all of those kind of things and not God, Guru is not God, the spiritual master is a servant of God, all those clarifications and then everybody left. Swamiji was alone to clean up the whole place. Just see the thing that Srila Prabhupada was doing and ten, eleven, few of them and then those devotees took initiation. There were many more who were just sitting and watching and all of that and Swamiji had to clean up everything. So this was the first initiation story.

**Key Quote:** "So that was a very disturbing thing for the women disciples who have taken."
**Summary:** The speaker transitions to the story of the first second initiation, explaining that it was initially only for men, which upset the women disciples.

Then we all know now there is first initiation and Srila Prabhupada called this the Harinam Diksha, Harinam initiation and after a few years, it was 1968, so this was the first, first initiation that happened. Now we will talk about the short story about the first second initiation. So once again it was 1968, now 66 to 68, two years have passed. Prabhupada, Swamiji has conducted many first initiations by now and there have been many, few temples. There is temple in New York, there is temple in Boston, there is temple in San Francisco, there is one temple in Montreal, Canada and I am not too sure, maybe Hamburg, Germany. So already Swamiji and his disciples have gone and started few temples. Now in 1968, Swamiji is in Boston and now he says he is going to introduce the second initiation and those who have taken first initiation they are going to take second initiation. Once again everyone is curious what is the second initiation and again Swamiji says I will tell you at the right time and they are all curious and then on a certain date and then Swamiji says the second initiation is only for men. So that was a very disturbing thing for the women disciples who have taken and they were thinking what is this, why is Swamiji doing this, this is a kind of a discrimination and they were really upset about it. They felt slighted, they felt discriminated and they were very upset that why is Swamiji doing this and there were actually only two women at that time in Boston. One was Govinda Devi Dasi another was Jadurani. Both of them very very serious, very committed, very devoted to Swamiji, ready to do anything for Swamiji and but Swamiji said it is only for men because that was the policy of the Gaviyamat and that was generally that is a tradition in India. The second initiation was called the Brahmana initiation and that is the initiation where you will get the Gayatri Mantra and it is very well established in India. Gayatri Mantra only men chant. What is this women chanting Gayatri? That is unheard of and that was the policy of Gaviyamat as far as I have understood. That is what I have understood and that is what Srila Prabhupada announced too.

#### second initiation, Brahmana initiation, Matthews
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vIdSB_kWFg4?t=2607
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You know, Prabhupada was doing all kinds of things just to get people to come and hear and they should hear about Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the second initiation ceremony in Boston, including the invitation of a student named Matthews as a special guest.

So on the day of second initiation it was happening in Boston. There was one student from Boston University, divinity school. He was studying religion and he was studying Indian religion. A young student, a young fellow, maybe he was doing a scholar, maybe he was doing his PhD or something like that. And then so Swamiji said he can be invited as the chief guest during the second. You know, Prabhupada was doing all kinds of things just to get people to come and hear and they should hear about Krishna. Maybe their hearts will change. Prabhupada was you know, he found different ways to get. So he called him Professor Matthews. He was not actually a professor. Prabhupada would call him Professor Matthews. And then he would say no, no, no Swamiji I am not yet a professor. And so Matthews was maybe he was a doctoral student or something. He was called as the special guest during the ceremony. And then once again a sacrificial fire and all that was conducted. And then Swamiji spoke about second initiation. And he said it was actually my spiritual master who introduced this. And my spiritual master would give the Brahmana diksha to even people who are not born in Brahminical families. Because this is actually the true Vedic tradition. And so anybody who has acquired some right guna and karma has undergone certain purification rites. And if he is serious in spiritual life can take second initiation and which is the Brahmana initiation will be given Gayathri mantra. So then Matthews asked Swamiji does that mean they have to take some special vows? Are there any special vows that these candidates who are taking the second initiation? Swamiji said no, there is no special vow. They have to chant the Gayathri mantra that will be given to them. And then do they have to fast for something? No, Swamiji said no, there is no special fasting or anything. They just have to chant the special mantras. And so Swamiji conducted the fire sacrifice once again. And again there was smoke and all this chanting going on. You know generally the neighbours of Iskand, we become very unpopular among the neighbours of our temples. And here too, the neighbour was a landlord and the landlord's wife and she was an alcoholic woman

#### second initiation, alcoholic neighbor, Govinda Dasi, Jadurani
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vIdSB_kWFg4?t=2771
**Verse References:** —

**Summary:** The speaker recounts the interruption of the ceremony by an alcoholic neighbor and the absence of Govinda Dasi and Jadurani, who were feeling left out, and Srila Prabhupada's gracious acknowledgement of them upon their return.

and then she would get very upset with the devotees and what they do. And now she is hearing, she is seeing some smoke coming and then hearing all these chants. And while this chanting is going on she comes rushing into the house, into the temple where the devotees are conducting this ceremony. She opens the door forcibly, walks in and then she looks, her hair is all dishevelled and her garment is also dishevelled and then she is looking at them. And then she is very upset and she is drunk obviously. And she is very upset and she looks at everybody there and then she is just, "God damn this house!" And then she turns around and goes back, slams the door and walks away. Swamiji is very peaceful. He looks at her, he hears her and then he asks them, what did she say? Did she say this is a house of God? Swamiji heard it as house of God. She said this God damn house or something, that's what she had said. Then the disciple said, no no Swamiji, she is a drunkard and she is a troublemaker like that. Anyway, and then this ceremony went on. And then Swamiji noticed that the boys are all there participating in the ceremony and the two girls, Govindadasi and Jadurani are not there. So they were actually so upset that they were not included for the ceremony. They stayed back in the apartment a certain distance away and they were crying. Govindadasi was crying that we are feeling left out. But then at some point they made up their mind, what's this? You know we are going to miss Swamiji's talk. And they decided, they kept aside and they came running back. And the ceremony was going on and Prabhupada was still talking. And then as they entered the room, so gracious of Srila Prabhupada, Swamiji looked at them and said, oh I was wondering, I was thinking about you. Where were you? And Krishna has sent you. You know so sweet of Srila Prabhupada to acknowledge those two young girls who were feeling hurt like that. And Srila Prabhupada said, oh Krishna has sent you. And so they sat down there and they watched the ceremony going on and the whole ceremony happened.

#### women, Gayatri Mantra, time place circumstance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vIdSB_kWFg4?t=2923
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "There is no difference spiritually. So let them also get."
**Summary:** The speaker describes how Srila Prabhupada, after considering American culture, decided to give Govinda Dasi and Jadurani the Gayatri Mantra, emphasizing that there is no spiritual difference between men and women.

And then the next day you know, see you can imagine, Srila Prabhupada must have thought over because those few days, few years, one or two years that went by Prabhupada used to ask these young, his disciples Govinda Dasi and few others understand about American culture, American society, American practices, how women do everything that men do. So Prabhupada had heard about all those kind of things. So the next day in the morning, Prabhupada calls Govinda Dasi and her husband Gaur Sundar and has a talk. And then he says, I have thought over in America, women can also get Gayatri Mantra and they can also chant the Gayatri Mantra but they will not have the thread. The previous day Prabhupada had given, draped a thread around each one of them and made them chant Gayatri, how to chant Gayatri, all that had happened. So Prabhupada said the next day he would conduct the ceremony and the next day in the night, evening they had another ceremony only for Govinda Dasi and Jadurani. And both of them, Prabhupada gave them Gayatri Mantra and he said in America women can also do as much as men do. There is no difference spiritually. So let them also get. These are all unprecedented very very unusual things that Srila Prabhupada was doing according to time, place and circumstances. There is a very beautiful verse in the Srimad Bhagavatam. I will read this verse. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Mantrane Mantra Mantrena Anena Devasya Kuryat Parvyamaya Mayim Budhaha Saparyam Vividhair Dravyaer Desha Kala Vibhagavit Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya This is the 12 syllable Mantra for worshipping Lord Krishna. One should install the physical form of the Lord and with the chanting of the Mantra, one should offer flowers and fruits and other varieties of foodstuffs exactly according to the rules and regulations prescribed by the authorities. But this should be done in consideration of place, time and attendant conveniences and inconveniences. And Prabhupada explains here now according to because it says Desha Kala Vibhagavit and Saparyam Vividhair Dravyaer Desha Kala Vibhagavit. The paraphernalia Dravya that you use should be according to Desha Kala, according to practical circumstances and all of those kind of things. So you can see how Srila Prabhupada according to time circumstances he was presenting, he was introducing all of these ancient Vedic practices in ISKCON. And so

#### transcendence, anger, absolute truth
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DgwYgDv6gTw?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Because he is absolute, both his anger and pleasure are the same."
**Summary:** This segment explains that while both Arjuna and Krishna appear angry, Krishna's anger is transcendental and not subject to material nature.

Both Arjuna and Lord Shri Krishna are described here in an angry mood. But Arjuna's eyes were like balls of red copper, whereas the eyes of the Lord were like lotuses. This means that the angry mood of Arjuna and that of the Lord are not on the same level. The Lord is Transcendence. Please note, Transcendence with a capital T. The Lord is Transcendence. I hope it's the same there, yeah. And thus he is absolute in any stage. His anger is not like the anger of a conditioned living being within the modes of qualitative material nature. Because he is absolute, both his anger and pleasure are the same. His anger is not exhibited in the three modes of material nature. It is only a sign of his bent of mind towards the cause of his devotee because that is his transcendental nature. Therefore, even if he is angry, the object of anger is blessed. He is unchanged in all circumstances.

Ripum, he is his enemy. Shibiraya Nishantam. And it is Arjuna's intent that he should bring him to the Shibira military camp. Praha, at that time, spoke. Krishna spoke. Praha, Arjuna. And to Arjuna, Prakupitaha. Praha, who is angry. Bhagavan, Ambujekshanaha, Lord, who has lotus eyes, spoke. Praha, because in the next verse we see Lord Krishna speaking. So Bhagavan, Ambujekshanaha, Krishna, who has lotus eyes, spoke. Prakupitaha, to angry Arjuna. And in the purport, Srila Prabhupada has expanded the understanding and he has said that not only Arjuna was angry, even Krishna was angry about the whole thing. If you see the next statement of Krishna, which you will see in your next class, he is telling Arjuna, kill him. Obviously, you can't be telling Krishna to kill him. You can't be telling Krishna to kill him in a very happy way. Kill him. So there is anger even in Krishna's voice. So even Krishna is angry. Arjuna is also angry. Kill him. So that's the kind of... It's not a simple, normal conversation that's going on. So it's full of anger. And so, in the purport, we see how Srila Prabhupada is taking action. And so, in the purport, we see how Srila Prabhupada is taking action. Srila Prabhupada is taking this as an opportunity to explain that Arjuna's anger was that his eyes were red. Krishna is also angry that his eyes are like lotus. That is the difference.

#### transcendental, material nature, Krishna's position
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DgwYgDv6gTw?t=249
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna's position is not within the Panchabhootas, not within the Kala, something that is beyond all of this."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes Krishna's transcendental position, contrasting it with the material world composed of Panchabhootas and influenced by Kala (time).

Srila Prabhupada takes every opportunity, Srila Prabhupada takes every opportunity, Srila Prabhupada takes every opportunity, Srila Prabhupada takes every opportunity the position of Krishna as a transcendental person. Krishna's position, Krishna's personality, Krishna's form, Krishna's nature, Krishna's name, Krishna's activities, all of these are transcendental. Transcendental meaning, it is something not of this material domain. In the material domain we are familiar with Panchabhootas, right, earth, water, fire, all our bodies, table, all of this is actually transformation of the Panchabhootas. And the Panchabhootas are always undergoing transformation, that is the nature of the Panchabhootas, and they are undergoing transformation under the influence of Kala. So in this material world we see material nature constituting of the Panchabhootas under the influence of Kala undergoing transformations. But Krishna's position is not within the Panchabhootas, not within the Kala, something that is beyond all of this. He is beyond. Whether it is the Rig Veda, whether it is the Upanishad, whether it is the Bhagavad Gita, all of them constantly repeat this point.

#### transcendental, material world, Bhagavad Gita

**Summary:** This segment explains Krishna's transcendental position as 'Tamasaha Parastaat,' beyond the material world, and introduces how Krishna establishes this in the fourth chapter of the Bhagavad Gita.

Krishna's position is Tamasaha Parastaat. Tamasaha refers to the material world, the Panchabhootas under the time. Tamasa also refers to ignorance, also refers to darkness. This nature of the material world is dark, and so Parastaat is beyond. Let's see how this material world is beyond. Let's see how Krishna establishes this, that his nature is very different and he is transcendental in the Bhagavad Gita. They say, there are many places, but in a very early part in the fourth chapter of the Gita, Krishna begins to establish this. Let's see how Krishna does this in a very interesting manner.

This avyaya yogam, this immutable, indestructible knowledge of yoga. imam vivasvate yogam proktavan prokta means I spoke. proktavan aham avyayam yogam aham proktan to whom? imam vivasvate yogam unto vivasvan. vivasvate yogam proktavan aham proktavan. avyaya yogam aham proktavan imam vivasvate unto vivasvaan. This imam avyayam yogam. Because in the second chapter of the Gita, Krishna spoke. He concluded about transcendental knowledge. Sorry in the third chapter. Krishna concluded that about thus knowing oneself to be transcendental to material senses, mind and intelligence, one should control the lower self by the higher self and thus by spiritual strength conquer this insatiable enemy known as lust. That's how the third chapter ends.

#### analogy, doubt, understanding
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DgwYgDv6gTw?t=670
**Verse References:** BG 4.4

**Key Quote:** "Prabhu, how could you give Bhagavad Gita to Jawaharlal Nehru, your junior? Jawaharlal Nehru, Gandhiji, they are very senior. How could you give?"
**Summary:** The speaker uses an analogy of distributing Bhagavad Gitas to Jawaharlal Nehru and Mahatma Gandhi to illustrate Arjuna's question about how Krishna could have taught Vivasvan, who was much older.

Just like, suppose I say, we are all here going to celebrate book distribution success, right? So, suppose I tell you this story. Three weeks back, I went to Delhi and I was aware there is some problem. There was a book marathon going on. And even I thought I should distribute some books to some important people. So I went to Delhi. I took with me a carton of books. And there was another devotee with me. We went to Red Fort in Delhi. And there was a big lawn beside the Red Fort. We went to the lawn in the Red Fort. And this devotee was with me. And this devotee was with me with a carton of Bhagavad Gitas. And there I saw, there was a gathering and there was some security men and went through all of that. And there, you know, Delhi means security. All these are big issues. So, went there. And had an opportunity to meet Jawaharlal Nehru. And I told the devotee, I said, you know what? And I told the devotee, bring the books. And he opened the carton. And I presented the book to Jawaharlal Nehru. And next to him was Mahatma Gandhi. And I presented, get another book. And he gave me another. I presented another book to Mahatma Gandhi. He looked through the book. And next to him was Dr. Radhakrishnan. Then I remembered, oh, Prabhupada has criticized Radhakrishnan. He didn't understand Gita. So let me give him. I took another book. Dr. Radhakrishnan, this is Bhagavad Gita. You must read. You will understand better. And they were all very happy. And then I said, thank you very much for receiving. And then me and the devotee with me, we were happy. We gave the books to the first prime minister of the country. And we came back. And I come this and come back to Bangalore. And I tell you all of that. This is what we did. Then you will ask the question. Prabhu, how could you give Bhagavad Gita to Jawaharlal Nehru, your junior? Jawaharlal Nehru, Gandhiji, they are very senior. How could you give? This is the question that Arjuna is asking. Just see. Aparambhavato janma param janmavivasvatah. How can I understand? You spoke this knowledge to Vivasvan. Vivasvan is param. He is much much senior to you. Katametadvijaniam tvamadau proktavaniti. You spoke. How can I understand? You distributed Gita to Jawaharlal Nehru.

AJO PISAN NAVYAYATMA BHUTHANAM ISHVARO PISAN PRAKRITIM SWAM ADHISHTAYA SAMBHAVAM YATMAMAYAYA this is my position AJA I am unborn ABYAYATMA I am inexhaustible BHUTHANAM ISHVARO PISAN all existence, all living entities, I am the controller my position is different, Arjuna PRAKRITIM SWAM ADHISHTAYA this is my position situated in my position PRAKRITIM SWAM ADHISHTAYA SWAM PRAKRITI my own nature this is my nature nature, transcendental nature, situated in my adhishthaya, svam prakritim adhishthaya, adhishthaya means being fixed, being situated, prakritim svam adhishthaya sambhavami, I appear in this world, so my appearance, my taking birth here in this world is not the same as you taking birth, it is different, I am Aja, Avyaya, Bhutanam, Ishwaraha, and sprakritim svam adhishthaya sambhavami, so Krishna is establishing that his position is transcendental,

Translation and purport by His Divine Grace A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada. When the Kumaras, although by far the fittest persons, were thus forbidden entrance by the two of them, the two chief doorkeepers of Sri Hari, while other divinities looked on, their eyes suddenly turned red because of anger, due to their great eagerness to see their most beloved master, Sri Hari, the personality of Godhead.

#### renunciation, anger, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/HdPMySPtJv4?t=103
**Verse References:** BG 9.29
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The difference between the anger of an ordinary person and that of a liberated person is that an ordinary person becomes angry because his sense desires are not being fulfilled, whereas a liberated person like the Kumaras becomes angry when restricted in the discharge of duties for serving the Supreme Personality of Godhead."
**Summary:** The speaker reads Srila Prabhupada's purport, explaining the role of a sanyasi in Vedic society, the importance of Krishna as the best friend, and the nature of anger in both conditioned and liberated souls, emphasizing that even liberated persons can experience anger when service to the Lord is obstructed.

Purport by Srila Prabhupada. According to the Vedic system, a sanyasi, a person in the renounced order of life, is dressed in saffron-colored garments. This saffron dress is practically a passport for the mendicant and sanyasi to go anywhere. The sanyasi's duty is to enlighten people in Krishna consciousness. Those in the renounced order of life have no other business but preaching the glories and supremacy of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Therefore, the Vedic sociological conception is that a sanyasi should not be restricted. He is allowed to go anywhere and everywhere he wants, and he is not refused any gift he might demand from a householder. The four Kumaras came to see the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Narayana. The word Suhrtama, best of all friends, is important. As Lord Krishna states in the Bhagavad Gita, He is the best friend of all living entities. suhrtam sarva-bhuta-anam. No one can be a greater well-wishing friend to any living entity than the Supreme Personality of Godhead. He is so kindly disposed towards everyone that in spite of our completely forgetting our relationship with the Supreme Lord, He comes Himself. Sometimes personally, as Lord Krishna appeared on this earth, and sometimes as His devotee, as did Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, and sometimes He sends His bona fide devotees to reclaim all the fallen souls. Therefore, He is the greatest well-wishing friend of everyone. And the Kumaras wanted to see Him. The doorkeepers should have known that the four sages had no other business, and therefore to restrict them from entering the palace was not apt. In this verse, it is figuratively stated that the younger brother of desire suddenly appeared in person when the sages were forbidden to see their most beloved personality of Godhead. The younger brother of desire is anger. If one's desire is not fulfilled, the younger brother anger follows. Here we can mark that even great saintly persons like the Kumaras were also angry, but they were not angry for their personal interests. They were angry because they were forbidden to enter the palace to see the personality of Godhead. Therefore, the theory that in the perfectional stage one should not have anger is not supported in this verse. Anger will continue even in the liberated stage.

#### liberation, sense control, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/HdPMySPtJv4?t=294
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Becoming liberated does not necessitate losing one's sensual activities. Sense activities continue even in the liberated stage. The difference is however that sense activities in liberation are accepted only in connection with Krishna Consciousness, whereas sense activities in the conditioned stage are enacted for personal sense gratification."
**Summary:** The speaker continues reading Srila Prabhupada's purport, clarifying that liberated persons like the Kumaras can still experience anger, but their anger is directed towards serving the Lord, not personal gratification. He emphasizes that liberation doesn't eliminate sensual activities but transforms them into Krishna consciousness.

These four mendicant brothers, the Kumaras, were considered liberated persons, but still they were angry because they were restricted in their service to the Lord. The difference between the anger of an ordinary person and that of a liberated person is that an ordinary person becomes angry because his sense desires are not being fulfilled, whereas a liberated person like the Kumaras becomes angry when restricted in the discharge of duties for serving the Supreme Personality of Godhead. In the previous verse, it has been clearly mentioned that the Kumaras were liberated persons. Vidit Atma Tatva means one who understands the truth of self-realization. One who does not understand the truth of self-realization is called ignorant. But one who understands the self, the super-self, their inter-relation and activities in self-realization is called Vidit Atma Tatva. Although the Kumaras were already liberated persons, they nevertheless became angry. This point is very important. Becoming liberated does not necessitate losing one's sensual activities. Sense activities continue even in the liberated stage. The difference is however that sense activities in liberation are accepted only in connection with Krishna Consciousness, whereas sense activities in the conditioned stage are enacted for personal sense gratification.

#### Vaikuntha, anger, desire
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/HdPMySPtJv4?t=402
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Their eyes became very agitated with the younger brother of desire. And so Prabhupada has said that figuratively it means the anger. Suddenly they became angry."
**Summary:** The speaker begins to elaborate on the verse, describing the sages' attempt to enter Vaikuntha and their subsequent anger at being stopped by the doorkeepers. He explains the meaning of terms like 'Suarhatama,' 'Nishidhyamanaha,' and 'Animesheshu Mishatsvu,' and how the sages' eyes became agitated with anger, which is figuratively the younger brother of desire.

So this verse is talking about. So we have been reading about the sages trying to enter Vaikuntha and the two doorkeepers stopping them from entering the Vaikuntha Loka. These sages were, it is described here, Suarhatama. They were very very qualified. They were not some unqualified persons trying to enter Vaikuntha. They were Suarhatama. And still Nishidhyamanaha, they were forbidden. And when they were forbidden, Animesheshu Mishatsvu, there were many other personalities of the Vaikuntha. Animesha, Animesha refers to unblinking eye. It refers to Vishnu and it refers to Vishnu's associates. So Animesheshu Mishatsvu, they were all other residents of Vaikuntha. They were also watching over this. Nishidhyamana Suhratamahyapi Hare Pratiharapabhyam, Hari's Pratiharapabhyam, His doorkeepers, His associates, Pratiharapabhyam, They stopped them. So the sages began to speak Uchuhu. From the next verse onwards Munaya Uchuhu, Sages will be speaking. So why were they and how did they speak? They were with angry words they spoke. Kamanujena sahasata upapluthakshaha, upapluthakshaha, Their eyes became very agitated. And with what? With the Kama Anujena, Anuja means a younger brother. Kama Anujena refers to a younger brother of desire. Kama. And so Prabhupada has said that figuratively it means the anger. Kamanujena sahasa, Suddenly they became angry.

**Key Quote:** "They were very eager to see their beloved master Lord Hari. And that was stopped by the two doorkeepers. And hence they became very disturbed."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the sages' qualifications and self-realization, highlighting that their anger stemmed from being prevented from seeing their beloved Lord Hari. He explains the terms 'didrikshita' (eagerness to see) and 'suhrthama' (beloved master), clarifying why the sages were disturbed.

And importantly why did they become angry? They were suarhatta maha, They were most qualified. And in the previous verse we saw viditaatma tatvam, They were self-realized. And why did they become angry? That is disclosed in this verse. Because suhrthama didrikshita bhanga, They were didrikshita, Very eager to see. They were eager to see whom? suhrthama, Their beloved master Lord Hari. And that was bhanga, that was stopped by the two doorkeepers. And hence they became very disturbed. When the Kumaras although by far the fittest persons, suarhatta maha api were thus forbidden entrance nishidhyamanaha by the two chief doorkeepers of Sri Hari Hare pratihara pabhyam while other divinities looked on animisheshu mishatsu Their eyes suddenly turned red upaplu thakshaha because of anger kamanujena due to their great eagerness didrikshita to see their most beloved master suhrthama didrikshita bhanga Sri Hari the personality of Godhead.

#### Vedic society, sadhus, spiritual knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/HdPMySPtJv4?t=676
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Why is it that the sages should be allowed to go anywhere and no one should stop them? Because the sages are carriers of the message of the Lord. They have no interest other than to share the information about the Supreme Lord, the purpose of life and what is that we should do in this human form of life."
**Summary:** The speaker transitions to discussing Prabhupada's points, emphasizing that sages should not be forbidden from entering places because they carry the message of the Lord and enlighten society. He explains the Vedic sociological conception and the important position of sadhus in sharing spiritual knowledge.

So Prabhupada has touched upon many important points. The first one is that the sages were not to be forbidden because it is so in the, it is the sanyasi or a mendicant or a sage is permitted to go to any place and no one shall stop them. That is the, what Prabhupada has said here. This is the Vedic sociological conception. How a Vedic society is organized, how the Vedic society conducts is what is being described. Why is it so? Why is it that the sages should be allowed to go anywhere and no one should stop them? Because the sages are carriers of the message of the Lord. They have no interest other than to share the information about the Supreme Lord, the purpose of life and what is that we should do in this human form of life. So they have, they are meant to enlighten the society and hence they should not be stopped. This is the important position of the sadhus in the Vedic sociological conception.

#### Vedic society, brahmanas, self-realization
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/HdPMySPtJv4?t=786
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Giving knowledge about self-realization is even far superior and more important than becoming a king and ruling over a kingdom."
**Summary:** The speaker illustrates the importance of saintly persons in Vedic society by narrating the story of King Vena, who was killed by brahmanas for being impious. He explains that the brahmanas did not become king themselves because they had more important duties related to self-realization and teaching others.

From this we see, the important position given to a saintly person in the Vedic society. He is discharging a very important duty of enlightening the people about the spiritual values of life. In another place, in the Bhagavatam, King Vena was a very impious king. And then, in the Vedic system, the king has to follow the directions of the brahmanas and rule and encourage and not only maintain the citizens in his kingdom, fulfilling their material needs, he also has an obligation to ensure that their spiritual needs are also fulfilled. But King Vena was not a good king. He was a sinful person and he didn't care for the brahmanas and he was not encouraging any spiritual activities in his kingdom. At that time, all the brahmanas in his kingdom come together and they discuss what is to be done. And they decide that they should kill King Vena. And they kill him. And then, it says, from Vena's body they created another person and made him the king. Now the question comes, if the brahmanas were so powerful that they could kill and dethrone a king, why couldn't they become the king themselves? After dethroning King Vena, because after all he was a bad king. And there Prabhupada explains, no, the brahmanas would not become the king because they had more important duties than becoming a king, which is, they were engaged in self-realization and they were engaged in teaching others in the society in the matter of self-realization. So you see from all of these kind of instructions, where Srila Prabhupada positions that activity of giving knowledge about self-realization is. It's even far superior and more important than becoming a king and ruling over a kingdom. So this is the kind of conception that Srila Prabhupada in all his teachings he brings about, about the role of a saintly person in the human society. And hence in the Vedic conception, such a saintly person should have free access to move around in the human society and give this knowledge to whoever was ready to receive this.

Just like we have in Bhagavad Gita the words guhyam, guhyatama means the most confidential knowledge. In the same way here, suhrutthama, suhrut, suhruttara and suhrutthama. Suhrut means friend. Suhruttara means greater friend. Suhrutthama means most well-wishing friend. So the superlative is used. So Krishna is such a friend of all living entities.
