#### ecstasy, devotion, Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pzMGkDdfoBE?t=65
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Haridasa listened intently to the pastime of his beloved Lord Krishna and he suddenly moved by ecstasy he fell unconscious to the ground."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates a story of Haridas Thakur experiencing ecstatic trance while watching a snake charmer perform and sing about Krishna.

Many many past times were there, of Haridars Chakur, miraculous past times, small small, different places. There is one mentioned here in Chaitanya. There's a story of Haridars Chakur, a snake charmer and a snake. One day, in the courtyard of a rich man's house, a snake charmer was performing a snake dance. He was chanting a special mantra accompanied by musicians playing the Mridanga and Manjira. Haridas was passing by and stood to one side watching the snake dance. Although the snake charmer was an ordinary human being, by the influence of his mantra he could dance as though the spirit of the king of the snakes Ananta Sesha possessed him. With an enchanting melody he sang about the pastime of Lord Krishna in the lake called Kaliadaha where the Lord had vanquished an evil serpent called Kalia. Haridasa listened intently to the pastime of his beloved Lord Krishna and he suddenly moved by ecstasy he fell unconscious to the ground when he regained consciousness. He jumped to his feet and rode like a lion, dancing in ecstasy as he babbled with joy. The snake charmer was impressed by the manifestation of ecstatic symptoms in Haridas Thakur's body and stood respectful to the sight. As the various symptoms of horripilation, crying and shivering traversed Haridas' body, he rolled in the dust, crying in love for Krishna. He remembered how his beloved Lord had mercifully saved the snake Kalia and as he floated in ecstatic trance, the people around him obliged by singing songs about the wonderful pastimes of the Lord. The snake charmer simply stood to the sight, overcome by awe and reverence for Haridas.

---
#### envy, devotion, punishment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pzMGkDdfoBE?t=195
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Not out of love for Krishna, but out of enviousness towards a pure devotee, the Brahmana threw himself on the ground in ecstasy."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the story, describing how a brahmana attempts to imitate Haridas's ecstasy out of envy and is punished by the snake charmer.

When his ecstasy subsided, Haridas left and the snake charmer resumed his performance. Everyone who witnessed the ecstasy of Haridas felt spiritually accelerated and they smeared their bodies with dust from his feet. However, one cunning and insightful Brahmana in the crowd decided to cheat the people. He also started dancing. He told himself, I can see that the people are not very intelligent. They are offering so much respect to this low-born Haridas just because he dances and cries. If I, a Brahmana, who also dance and cry, then they will certainly worship me as well. The Brahmana threw himself on the ground and feigned unconsciousness as though he had fallen into trance. However, as soon as he touched the ground, the dancing snake charmer became furious, picked up a stick and began beating the Brahmana relentlessly. He struck him repeatedly. He hit him on every part of his body until the Brahmana, howling in agony, got up and ran away just to save his life. The snake charmer then resumed his dance while the entire crowd stood dumbfounded. At last, a man with folded hands humbly requested the snake charmer to explain why he had stood respectfully to the side while Haridas danced, yet he had beaten the Brahmana for what seemed to be the same activity. The spirit of Ananda Sesha, who dwelt within the snake charmer, then spoke in glorification of Srila Das Thakura. The subject matter of your inquiry is very confidential. Although such a topic is rarely discussed, because of its highly esoteric nature, I shall nevertheless answer your question. When Srila Das Thakura was dancing in ecstatic trance, each one of us felt it. Our respect and affection for him spontaneously increased. However, the deceitful Brahmana was simply imitating Haridasa in order to get some cheap adoration from the crowd. Not out of love for Krishna, but out of enviousness towards a pure devotee, the Brahmana threw himself on the ground in ecstasy. Furthermore, he interrupted by dancing. Since he disturbed me and tried to imitate the pure devotee, I punished him severely. This arrogant and deceitful Brahmana has no love for Krishna. To receive the shelter of devotional service at the lotus feet of Lord Krishna, one has to become pure, free from deceit. By seeing Srila Haridas Thakura dance, one becomes liberated from material bondage.

---
#### glories of Haridas Thakur, purity, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pzMGkDdfoBE?t=372
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Even the Supreme Lord Krishna himself dances when he sees Srila Dasa Thakura dance."
**Summary:** The speaker continues to glorify Haridas Thakur, emphasizing his purity, his ability to purify others, and his appearance in a low-caste family to teach about the illusory nature of material designations.

Even the Supreme Lord Krishna himself dances when he sees Srila Dasa Thakura dance. The entire universe becomes cleansed of all sinful reactions just by seeing Srila Haridas dance. He is truly worthy of his name, Haridasa Thakura, the servant of the Supreme Lord, Hare Krishna, who shines eternally like a full moon in the heart of his devotees. Haridasa is affectionate and compassionate towards all living entities, and he descends as the eternal servant of the Supreme Lord in each of the Lord's incarnations. He is free from any fault or offense towards the Supreme Lord or his devotees. Even in his dreams, he never commits an offense. A moment's association with Haridasa can purify any living entity and assure him the eternal shelter of Lord Krishna's lotus feet. Even Lord Brahma and Lord Shiva constantly desire his association. Haridas appeared in the family of a lowly Muslim on the order of the Supreme Lord, just to teach human society that material designations like caste, creed, birth, status and family positions are all illusory and pursuing them is futile.

---
#### scriptural truth, devotion, liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pzMGkDdfoBE?t=463
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "From the moment a person speaks the name Srila Haridas Thakura, his ascension to the supreme abode of Lord Krishna is guaranteed."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that a pure devotee born in a low-class family is worthy of worship, citing examples from scripture, and emphasizes the power of seeing and glorifying Haridas Thakur.

A pure devotee of the Lord is born in a low-class family. He is no less worthy of worship. This is injunction of the scriptures. On the other hand, if someone is born in a sophisticated family but does not worship the lotus feet of Sri Krishna, then his high birth is useless, for he lives just like a resident of hell. Just to prove the truth of these scriptures, Srila Haridas took birth in a low-caste family. Prahlad Maharaj, one of the greatest devotees of the Lord, had previously appeared in a family of demons. And Hanuman, the greatest devotee of Lord Ramachandra, took birth in the monkey species. The demigod desired the touch of the pure and transcendental form of Srila Haridas Thakura. And Ganga Mata also prays that her water will be sanctified by his presence. However, one need not touch Srila Das Thakura. By seeing him, simply by seeing him, one is automatically freed from the eternal cycle of birth and death. Furthermore, one who sees a devotee who has taken shelter of the lotus feet of Srila Haridas Thakura is also granted freedom from material bondage. Neither a thousand mouths nor a thousand ears would be sufficient to describe and hear the wonderful qualities of Srila Haridas Thakura. You are a fortunate soul, for your inquiry has given me an opportunity to glorify the pure devotee and has given you the opportunity to hear the transcendental topic. From the moment a person speaks the name Srila Haridas Thakura, his ascension to the supreme abode of Lord Krishna is guaranteed.

#### association, devotion, humility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pzMGkDdfoBE?t=628
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "If one actually chants the holy name without offences, his ecstatic love for the lotus feet of Lord Krishna is awakened."
**Summary:** The speaker begins another story about Haridas Thakur, focusing on his association with Balaram Acharya and Raghunath Das, and his humility in the face of praise.

There is one more pastime in Chaitanya Charitamrita. Haridas Thakur walked until he came to the village of Chandapura, later known as Krishnapura, where he stayed at Balaram Acharya's house. Balaram Acharya was the family priest of Hiranya and Govardhana Majumda. Due to receiving his favour, Balaram Acharya was very attached to Haridas Thakur and thus, he provided all his needs with great care and attention. Haridas Thakur was given a cottage in a secluded place and he came daily to Balaram Acharya's house to accept prasad. At that time, Raghunath Das was a schoolboy and he used to come and see Haridas Thakur every day. Naturally, Haridas Thakur was very merciful towards him and as a result, Raghunath Das later on attained to his love. Haridas Thakur was immediately sheltered at the lotus feet of Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. Regular discourses were held at the residence of Hiranya and Govardhana. One day, Balaram Acharya very humbly invited Haridas Thakur to come there. Upon seeing Haridas Thakur arrive, the two Majumdaras immediately stood up and then fell at his lotus feet to offer obeisances. There was many learned scholars, brahmanas and respectable gentlemen in that assembly and Hiranya and Govardhana were also greatly learned. As the two brothers very respectfully offered Haridas Thakur a sitting place, everyone began to speak about his exalted qualities as if they each possessed five mouths. Hiranya and Govardhana were very pleased to hear this talk. When it was mentioned how Haridas Thakur chanted 300,000 holy names daily, the learned scholars began to discuss the glories of the holy name of the Lord. Some of them said, by chanting the holy name of the Lord, one is freed from all reactions to sinful activities. Others said, simply by chanting the holy name of the Lord, one becomes liberated from material bondage. Haridas Thakur protested. These two benedictions are not the true result of chanting the holy name of the Lord. If one actually chants the holy name without offences, his ecstatic love for the lotus feet of Lord Krishna is awakened.

---
#### chanting, liberation, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pzMGkDdfoBE?t=786
**Verse References:** SB 1.2.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Liberation and the destruction of sinful reactions are two concomitant byproducts of chanting the holy name of the Lord."
**Summary:** Haridas Thakur explains that the true result of chanting the holy name is ecstatic love for Krishna, with liberation and destruction of sinful reactions being byproducts.

In Srimad Bhagavatam, it is said, when a person is actually advanced and takes pleasure in chanting the holy name of the Lord, who is very dear to him, he becomes agitated, and thus sometimes laughs, sometimes cries, and sometimes chants loudly and dances without caring for outsiders. Liberation and the destruction of sinful reactions are two concomitant byproducts of chanting the holy name of the Lord. As the rising sun immediately dissipates all the world's darkness, so the holy name of the Lord, even if chanted only once without offences, dispels all of one's sinful reactions. Haridas Thakur questioned, O learned scholars, please explain the meaning of this verse. However, the audience responded, it would be better if you explain the meaning of this important verse. Haridas Thakur said, even before the sun rises in the morning, it dissipates the darkness of night with its dawning light, and thus all fear of thieves and ghosts immediately vanishes. Then, when the sun actually becomes visible, everything is clearly manifest and people begin performing their morning religious activities and other duties. Similarly, the first glimpse of offenceless chanting of the Lord's holy name immediately dissipates all reactions to sinful life. Then, when one is actually on the platform of offenceless chanting, there awakens a static loving devotional service to the lotus feet of Krishna. Liberation is thus the insignificant result derived from a mere glimpse of awakening of offenceless chanting. An example of such an effect of Nama Bhasa is seen in the life of Ajamila.

---
#### offence, liberation, humility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pzMGkDdfoBE?t=900
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "For the devotee who enjoys the transcendental bliss of devotional service, liberation is most insignificant, like the water containing within the hoof print of a calf."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the story, describing how Gopal Chakravarti challenges Haridas Thakur's statement about liberation through chanting, leading to a bold challenge from both of them.

Liberation, which is unacceptable to a pure devotee, is very easily offered by Lord Krishna. There was a person named Gopal Chakravarti staying at Hiranya and Govardhana's house. He was the chief tax collector, and it was his duty to collect 12 lakh coins and deposit them in the emperor's treasury. This caste brahmana had very handsome bodily features, and he was very learned and youthful. However, he could not tolerate hearing the statement that one can achieve liberation by glimpsing the awakening of pure chanting of the Lord's holy name. Becoming very angry, Gopal Chakravarti began criticizing Haridas Thakur as follows. O learned scholars, just hear the conclusion of this emotional devotee. After many millions of births, when one becomes complete in transcendental knowledge, one may still not attain liberation. Yet, this man says that it can be obtained simply by awakening a glimpse of the holy name of the Lord. Haridas Thakur replied, Why are you doubtful? Shastra clearly states that one can attain liberation simply by a slight glimpse of offenseless chanting. For the devotee who enjoys the transcendental bliss of devotional service, liberation is most insignificant, like the water containing within the hoof print of a calf. Gopal Chakravarti said, If one is not liberated by such namabasa chanting, then I shall certainly cut off your nose. Haridas Thakur accepted this challenge by saying, If by namabasa liberation is not available, then I shall certainly cut off my own nose.

---
#### consequences, devotee's mercy, Lord's intolerance of blasphemy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pzMGkDdfoBE?t=1014
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "It is a characteristic of a pure devotee that he excuses any offences committed by an ignorant rascal. However, the characteristics of Lord Krishna is that he cannot tolerate blasphemy of his devotees."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes the story, describing the consequences of Gopal Chakravarti's offense, Haridas Thakur's forgiveness, and the Lord's intolerance of blasphemy against his devotees.

After hearing Gopal Chakravarti's challenge, everyone gathered there became very agitated and while getting up from their seats, they made a tumultuous noise. Hiranya and Govardhana immediately went and began chastising Gopal Chakravarti and Balram Acharya told him, You are a foolish logician. What do you know about the devotional service of the Lord? Since you have insulted Haridas Thakur, you have put yourself in a very dangerous position and so you should not expect anything auspicious. The two Majumdaras immediately dismissed Gopal Chakravarti from their service. Then, as Haridas Thakur got up to leave, they went along with the other members of the assembly and fell at his lotus feet. While smiling, Haridas Thakur spoke to them as follows in a very sweet voice, None of you are at fault. Indeed, even this ignorant so-called brahmana is not to be blamed, for he is accustomed to dry speculation and logic. One cannot understand the glories of the holy name of the Lord simply by mundane logic and argument. Now, all of you please return home and do not be sorry that I have been insulted. May Lord Krishna bestow his blessings upon you all. After returning home, Hiranya and Govardhana ordered that Gopal Chakravarti never again be admitted into their house. Within three days, Gopal Chakravarti was attacked by a leprosy and as a result, his highly raised nose melted away and fell off. Although his toes and fingers were as beautiful as golden champaka buds, they gradually withered away and upon seeing Gopal Chakravarti's condition, everyone became highly astonished. While considering the wonderful influence of Haridas Thakur, everyone glorified him and offered him obeisances. Although Haridas Thakur did not take the brahmana's offences seriously, the Supreme Personality of God could not tolerate it and thus arranged for him to suffer the consequences. It is a characteristic of a pure devotee that he excuses any offences committed by an ignorant rascal. However, the characteristics of Lord Krishna is that he cannot tolerate blasphemy of his devotees. Haridas Thakur was unhappy to hear about how Gopal Chakravarti had been attacked by leprosy. After informing Balram Acharya, Haridas Thakur went to Advaita Acharya's house at Shantipur.

#### association of devotees, qualification, relationships
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pzMGkDdfoBE?t=1177
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Great devotees are like razor blades. They can save your life by a good surgery, but if we are not careful, we can get cut."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of being qualified to associate with pure devotees and acting appropriately in their presence, comparing them to razor blades.

So, this is the glory of great devotees like Haridas Thakur. Great devotees are like razor blades. They can save your life by a good surgery, but if we are not careful, we can get cut. So, it is said that we always, first before praying for the association of pure devotees, we first pray that we are qualified to associate with the pure devotees. If we are qualified to associate with pure devotees, then we can act in an appropriate way in front of a pure devotee. So, what does it speak of dealing with the Supreme Lord? So, this material life, Sadhu Sanga, is so important that we learn how we associate with devotees of three different stages, as Chaitanya Mahaprabhu said. Devotees who are senior to us, devotees who are equal to us, and devotees who are junior to us. So, to all three levels, we need to have an appropriate relationship, a right way of dealing.

---
#### humility, service, offenses
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pzMGkDdfoBE?t=1263
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Devotees don't take the offence directly, but when devotees are offended, the Lord, even though He does not take offence to Him seriously, He will take the offence to His devotees seriously."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the appropriate relationships with senior, equal, and junior devotees, emphasizing humility, service, and the importance of avoiding offenses.

We don't consider ourselves superior to the inferiors. Rather, we show mercy and teach them. To equals, we be very friendly and together we serve the Supreme Lord. And towards the seniors, we humbly serve them. It doesn't matter who is leading, because going towards Krishna is more important. Whether we are leading or we are led, it really doesn't matter. So, whenever there is this situation of seniors, so juniors and equals, we need a situation to deal with that. So, that is why Srila Prabhupada created this house full of devotees, different levels of devotees, devotees from different areas, different from all over the world. So, we may be knowingly or unknowingly committing so many offences. So, every morning we say, Devotees don't take the offence directly, but when devotees are offended, the Lord, even though He does not take offence to Him seriously, He will take the offence to His devotees seriously. So, we know the story of Ambarishma Raj. The story is here. Haridas Chakul, lowly born or highly born, educated, not educated, beautiful, ugly, black, white, doesn't matter. So, we need to pray to the Supreme Lord and the devotees to go past the externals of Ambarishma. One has to cross. Looking at the Vaishnavas materially.

#### Dham Seva, Vaishnava Seva, opportunity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pzMGkDdfoBE?t=1385
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "But those two weeks, if we spend with Vaishnavas, the whole experience is totally different."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by emphasizing the fortune of being in Haridas Thakur's birthplace, the importance of Dham Seva and Vaishnava Seva, and the value of spending time with Vaishnavas.

So, great lesson by the life of Haridas Chakul. We are very fortunate to be here. It is said that if you chant Haridas Chakul's name, your liberation is guaranteed. So, what to speak of being in Haridas Chakul's birthplace, being in the bhajanskali of Haridas Chakul, and that too the association of Vaishnavas. So, this is a great, great opportunity. We cannot forget the basic foundational teachings of devotional service. So, Dham Seva is very important, just like Nama Seva, Murti Seva, Vaishnava Seva, Bhagavata Seva, Dham Seva. So, one has to, in the middle of many, many services and important roles we play in different places, it is always good to take some time off to visit Dharams with Vaishnavas. We can alone go two weeks, go around and come, but those two weeks, if we spend with Vaishnavas, the whole experience is totally different. So, we are coming here for Dham Seva, not only Dham Seva, along with that Vaishnava Seva and the greatest of Vaishnavas, Haridas Chakul. Haribol.

### Harinaam Diksha 2025 | Ritvik Initiation Ceremony Lecture | His Grace Jai Chaitanya Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Jai Chaitanya Dasa

#### Diksha, Liberation, Parampara
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/C-m7shDkWHI?t=11
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Having come here, this is an opportunity for us to revive the Krishna Consciousness, which is as a Swaroopa of the spirit soul."
**Summary:** HG Jai Chaitanya Dasa welcomes candidates for initiation, explaining Diksha as a process involving Divya Gyan and Papakshaya, essential for returning to Krishna and reviving our original Krishna Consciousness within the Brahma Madhva Gaudiya Parampara.

Hare Krishna and welcome to all the candidates for the first and the second initiation ceremony. As per the system given by Srila Prabhupada, as per his last wish, Diksha, you may know already, comes from two words, sounds, Di and Ksha. Di represents Divya Gyan, and Ksha is Papakshaya. Two things which is primarily needed for returning home back to Krishna. All of you know that we are in a place which is unsuitable for us. Us meaning the spirit soul, not as the body, not as the mind, or not as the ego, what we have assumed. So, this is not the place for the living entities to be in, because he should be with Krishna. He should be associating with Krishna and his paraphernalia. But unfortunately, we are here in this material world. And the process of liberation has already started by us coming in touch with this Brahma Madhva Gaudiya Parampara. The most sacred, live, vibrant, practicing Parampara in the Vaishnava system. So, having come here, this is an opportunity for us to revive the Krishna Consciousness, which is as a Swaroopa of the spirit soul.

---
#### Knowledge, Karma, Constitutional Activity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/C-m7shDkWHI?t=119
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Knowledge is there, but the Vijnana, the realised knowledge. Realising the knowledge by practice. Making the knowledge a reality. That is Divya Gyan."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Divya Gyan as realized knowledge, contrasting it with superficial understanding, and explains how Karma, resulting from Papa, obstructs our constitutional activities, leading to conditional activities based on our current state.

So, to go back to our original position, the two most important aspect is the Gyan, Divya Gyan. Knowledge about our original position. Gyan does not mean just material or theoretical understanding, superficial understanding. All of us know Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead. All of us have heard, we are not the body. All of us know we have to go back to him, but we are not able to go back to him. So, knowledge is there, but the Vijnana, the realised knowledge. Realising the knowledge by practice. Making the knowledge a reality. That is Divya Gyan. Realised knowledge. Ten Brahma Hridayat, like Brahma, realised. So, this realisation happens in the heart, not in the memory. So, this Divya Gyan is one of the components required to go back to the original position. But for that, the stopping, what is stopping us is the Karma, which is due to Papa. So, this building up of this Papa is there, which is stopping us from knowing the Jnana, which is stopping us from performing the original constitutional activities. Now, we are performing conditional activities. Those activities which we are made to do in this condition. Sometimes we are in human condition, we do human activities. We are in animal condition, you do animal activities. So, these are conditional activities. Even the activities we may do in Varna and Ashram are also conditional activities. Brahmanas, Kshatriyas, Vaishyas, Shudras, Abramacharis, Sanyasis, these are all conditional. Constitutional activity, Nitya Seva. The constitutional activity is Krishna Consciousness. Krishna Consciousness, Bhakti, performance of devotional service to the Supreme Lord.

---
#### Diksha, Spiritual Master, Surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/C-m7shDkWHI?t=298
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So, by the process of proper Diksha, one is supposed to develop Divya Gyan, one is able to burn his Papa, all by the grace of the spiritual master, who is at the lotus feet of Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Diksha helps develop Divya Gyan and burn Papa through the grace of the spiritual master, emphasizing that it's a beginning, not an end, and requires determination to follow, serve, give up, and surrender.

So, that is the constitutional activity of the soul. And what is stopping that is our conditional situation, which is due to Papa. The covering of the soul, build up covering, subtle covering and the gross covering. So, by the process of proper Diksha, one is supposed to develop Divya Gyan, one is able to burn his Papa, all by the grace of the spiritual master, who is at the lotus feet of Krishna. So, taking shelter of such a spiritual master, taking shelter of this Acharya, who is dear to Krishna, who has come to us by good fortune and by the mercy of this personality. So, that is the opportunity we have in this decision you all have made by taking this determination. So, Diksha is a beginning process. It is not an end process. By Diksha, one gets opportunity for this purification. Yes, there are certain qualifications, qualifications needed. Some qualification needed to get Diksha. Just like if you want to be a doctor, you should have a pre-university degree. With your SSLC or a matriculation, you cannot enter a MBBS college. So, there is a pre-requirement of getting something to get into this college. So, this is like an admission, which guarantees that you will become a doctor. But by admission, you don't become a doctor. Similarly, by Diksha, you don't become pure. It is a pre-requisite to become pure. And for Diksha, there are also pre-requirements. So, having qualified for Diksha, which is what? Qualification of Diksha is the determination. Determination to follow. Determination to serve. Determination to give up. Determination to surrender. Saranagati.

#### Surrender, Spiritual Master, Personal Relationship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/C-m7shDkWHI?t=450
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Unless we are ready to give up our own requirements, we cannot surrender 100% to the spiritual master, and 100% dedicate our life to satisfy his wishes."
**Summary:** The speaker defines Saranagati as full surrender, emphasizing the need to give up personal requirements to fully surrender to the spiritual master and dedicate one's life to fulfilling his wishes, as Krishna desires devotees to come through pure devotees.

So, then what is Saranagati? Full surrender. Irrespective of any personal needs, personal requirements, care for person. So, a devotee who is taking Diksha, who is desiring to go back to Godhead, who is desiring to serve the spiritual master, has to first understand that he is ready to give up his own requirements. Unless we are ready to give up our own requirements, we cannot surrender 100% to the spiritual master, and 100% dedicate our life to satisfy his wishes. Why? Because the Supreme Lord has wished that anybody who comes back to him should come through his pure devotees. As Krishna has explained in Adi Purana, anybody who considers himself as my devotee, I don't consider him as a devotee, unless he considers himself as a devotee of my devotee. That is the process. That is the principle. So, to satisfy the devotee of the Supreme Lord, one surrenders to him. And it's a very personal philosophy. This is not a mechanical philosophy that okay, I studied five years in engineering college, I have to get a certificate if I pass. I have gone through five years of doctorate, I have to become doctorate. I have done my research, I have to get. But here I have done everything, read Bhagavatam ten thousand times, I may not get Krishna. That is the difference. Material knowledge and spiritual knowledge. Because spiritual knowledge is under the control of a personality. Material knowledge is a control of a system. You go through the system, it doesn't matter who you are.

---
#### Spiritual Knowledge, Krishna, Devotee
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/C-m7shDkWHI?t=575
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In spiritual system, one cannot get Krishna without pleasing the servant of Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts material and spiritual knowledge, highlighting that spiritual knowledge requires a personal connection with Krishna and pleasing His servant, which involves surrendering one's heart and actions, not just superficial gestures.

You are able to vomit everything that is in the book, you will get. But here, vomiting won't help. Here, proper personal connection with Krishna. You have a connection with professor or a dean, doesn't matter. He may say yes sir, and behind him he will say so many other things. It doesn't matter. It's not a personal connection. Even though in many of the tradition knowledge, personal connection is important. You take any of those Vaidika knowledge, Natya Shastra, or any of those things also, the blessing of the teacher, blessing of the guru, this is a system in the Vedic system, not in the modern system. But spiritual system is totally different. In spiritual system, one cannot get Krishna without pleasing the servant of Krishna. Now pleasing the servant of Krishna does not mean simply, shaking your head in front of him. Yes, yes, yes. It is not a tongue service. It is a service. It is surrender of one's heart. So one has to Manasa Vacha Karvana. By one's mind, by one's words, and by one's action, have to surrender to the personality. And not only surrender up to the personality, make sure that the personality is satisfied. That is a requirement to get Krishna. So there is a system, but there is a very, very deep personal connection. That personal connection makes us qualified to be accepted by Krishna.

---
#### Krishna, Surrender, Acceptance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/C-m7shDkWHI?t=694
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is an autocrat. Can you accept that? If we can accept Krishna is autocrat, and his wish goes, nothing but his wish, then we are ready."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes from the Upanishads, emphasizing that Krishna is a transcendental autocrat who allows Himself to be available, requiring complete acceptance of His wishes and everything associated with Him, like accepting a person's entire family when marrying them.

As Prabhupada quotes from Upanishad, many times, what is that verse? Naaham. Naaham labhyo pravachanena labhya. Naayam atma pravachanena labhya. Na medhaya bahudha suthena. I am not available. Even if you are a great intelligent person, you are a great orator, you are a great Vedic person, I am available only if I allow you to be. I allow myself to be available to you. So this is Krishna's demand. So he is called transcendental autocrat. And we are all against autocrats in this material world, right? Nobody likes autocrat. Everybody blames you, he is an autocrat. Krishna is an autocrat. Can you accept that? Yes. If we can accept Krishna is autocrat, and his wish goes, nothing but his wish, then we are ready. So this is a preparation to get that consciousness. Deeksha is a preparation to get that consciousness. Deeksha allows us to get that consciousness. Why? Because once we have the blessing of the spiritual master, then we are not fighting alone. Then we are not fighting alone. Then we are not fighting alone. The world is like a fight. The world is like a fight. Fighting against maya, fighting against the current. So if you are alone, it's difficult. We need to have equipments. We have to be properly armed when we are fighting, right? The same way, the arm, the armor, that's the blessing of the spiritual master. So a military kivgamesh said this last time, in the holy name of the Lord, the bhagavata, person bhagavata, and the philosophy bhagavatata, we are entering a very very personal arena, just like a girl wants to marry a person. I like you, I want to marry you. But I don't like your mother, I don't like your father, I don't like a dog, I don't like your house, I don't like. Impossible. he has to get out. But you cannot go to Krishna saying, Krishna, I like you but nothing around you. That is not possible. They're accepting the whole. We are not accepting one person because Krishna is that whole. You cannot say, I'll take Krishna but I don't like this, I don't like that. No. Block, stock and barrel you have to accept.

#### Time, Energy, Surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/C-m7shDkWHI?t=1078
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So we have to make sure that we give our time we give our energy these are the two things we can surrender to Krishna what does we have we have our time and we have our energy"
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that Diksha is a full-time commitment, requiring the surrender of time and energy, whether one is a brahmachari in the temple or a grihastha outside, with grihasthas proportionally surrendering their earnings after fulfilling their duties.

now now I am very free in my life and I am ready to take Diksha no it's not a free time work it's a full-time work so we have to make sure that we give our time we give our energy these are the two things we can surrender to Krishna what does we have we have our time and we have our energy so brahmachari is in the temple give their time and their energy for the cause of the ashram which is the house of the spiritual master and there are the grahastha who stay outside the temple but they also give their time and energy whatever is left after taking care of their first duty so they have the license to take care of the first duty brahmachari is in the temple automatically they go back to godhead what about the me sitting in my house you also have the opportunity to go back to godhead by the same process the people living in the temple so doesn't matter whether one is staying in the temple or staying outside the temple both have the to go back to Godhead by following the process but one has to surrender, be ready to give his time and give his energy. So Grihasthas they earn money, they spend their time in earning money and they earn money whether it's an employment, whether it's a business and they have the duty for their ashram, the Grihastha ashram duties which includes immediate requirement, which includes emergency requirement. And he parts proportionately to his surrender the balance to the spiritual master's cause. That is a surrender and he keeps aside time enough to serve the spiritual master. So giving one's time and giving one's energy is a symptom of surrender, not taking Diksha, change name or you have a white thread that doesn't make us qualified as a qualified surrendered soul. What makes us qualified surrendered soul is what quantum of time and energy we spend for the absolute cause.

---
#### Diksha, Decision, Shelter
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/C-m7shDkWHI?t=1247
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "According to Prabhupada, this is the most intelligent decision one takes."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that Diksha is a determined decision, comparing it to deciding to go to war, and highlights it as the most intelligent decision according to Prabhupada, as it leads one from a dirty path to the proper path, balancing material and spiritual requirements to attain Krishna's shelter.

So that all that begins with Diksha. And a person taking Diksha is determined to do these things. If anybody wants to think twice, this is the right time. When you go to war, they all assemble and say anybody who wants to come out of this war, this is the right time to decide. So same way, this is the right time to decide. Before the Agni, before the Vaishnavas, before the holy name, before the deities, we take a pledge to dedicate. But according to Prabhupada, this is the most intelligent decision one takes. The most intelligent decision. Why? When you come to know that I am walking through the drain and somebody says, this is drain, dirty place. You are walking through, this is the road. Having come to know the dirty road, he gets into the proper path. So now we are driving on the main road. Till now we were driving on the drainage. Most intelligent person. Why? He doesn't work like a donkey in this material world for material causes. So one has to balance his life, his material requirements and keep spiritual requirement as a priority and step by step, if you follow this process, Gropa is already guaranteed as shelter of Krishna. Why all these things? To get the ultimate shelter which gives us Satchitananda, the state of eternity, the state of shelter and permanent bliss, not material bliss, not temporary bliss.

---
#### Happiness, Relationship, Diksha
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/C-m7shDkWHI?t=1364
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "What is intelligent is understanding that I am in an unnecessary business and enter the real business, which gives real bliss, real happiness, real connection with our relations."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by describing the intelligent person as one who understands the futility of material pursuits and enters the real business of connecting with Krishna and His pure devotees, which begins with Diksha, and then opens the floor for questions.

Permanent happiness. So if you want to experience the happiness of the soul, Atma Ramaha, self-sufficiently blissful soul, satisfied in himself, in service of the Supreme Lord. So that is the position we are trying to get. That is why it is called intelligent. Rather than in this material world, somehow or the other trying to get happy. Despite of all the difficulties, crossed difficulties, and after all these things, the man thinks, he's a hero. I've made it in this material world. That is where Gropa says, this is like a camel chewing the thorn. Finally, he's only testing his blood. Same way the man who says he's successful in the material world is only testing his own blood without knowing. That is not intelligent. What is intelligent is understanding that I am in an unnecessary business and enter the real business, which gives real bliss, real happiness, real connection with our relations. All the relations in this material world, by the way, happen so. But the real relationship is with Krishna and the pure devotees. And that begins with diksha. Hence, it is called the most right decision. It is called the most intelligent decision. So thank you very much for taking this decision. So we will continue with the other process. Anybody has any questions? Anybody wants to go outside, talk to the wife and date, decide again. This is the time to do all those things. Make a phone call. otherwise we can proceed

These are the work of the great preachers, like we said in the morning, the first set of preachers after Chaitanya Mahaprabhu: three people, three personalities—Srinivas Acharya, Narottam Das Thakur, and Shyamananda Prabhu. These were the first three preachers with the Shakti of Gauranga. All of them were very sad that they were born just after Chaitanya Mahaprabhu; they couldn't see Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. So, they were the Shakti of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu to initiate the preaching. Chaitanya Mahaprabhu did a lot of preaching, but real organized preaching was done by these three personalities. You know the story, on a big bullock cart, the books were brought, and it was stolen. So, we'll be going day after tomorrow to the dacoit king's city called Vishnupur, a very beautiful place. Shyamananda did North Bengal, Srinivas Acharya did Bangladesh, Narottam Das Thakur. Srinivas Acharya went all the way to Tripura and also Manipur. The first temple was built by Narottam Das Thakur. The first Gopunima was done by him in Keturi ground. We'll see that where Janava Mata himself, herself, and Nityananda Prabhu's wife, she was present. So, today also, that is the same system we follow: go to your place, take a place, put, have the deities installed, and start preaching. So, this system is not something new; it is going on from Narottam Das Thakur's time onwards. So, with all our weaknesses, He is making us to preach, continue preaching this message of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. But these preachers were Shakti away by themselves, Shyamananda, Narottam, Srinivas. #### Lineage, Controversy, Guru
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/nQmRdF90Ljw?t=270
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It was proven that this Tilak was handed over by Radharani herself to Shyamananda, and he did not change anything."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the lineage and addresses a historical controversy regarding Shyamananda Prabhu's tilak and initiation, clarifying his relationship with his gurus and Jeeva Goswami.

And Narottam Das Thakur, Vishwanath Chakravarti. So, this is our line. However, as far as these are concerned, they are a little different, even though they are from Nityananda Prabhu's vamsha because of the tilak mark, right? There's a little different, but basically, we have the same parampara. So, there was a controversy long time back in Mindavan with this tilak. Dukhi Krishna, the disciple of Krita Chaitanya, when he went to Vrindavan, he had this connection with Lalitha Sakhi and Radharani, and he got this Tilak. All the people said that he took reinitiation from Jeeva Goswami, and Jeeva Goswami has changed his name from Dukhi Krishna to Shyamananda. This whole kind of controversy erupted in Vandavan. So, to solve this, his own Guru from Mayapur, from Ambika Kalna, from there, Gauridas Pandit's disciple Hridayachaitanya went all the way to Vandavan to settle this issue. But then finally, to make the story short, that it was proven that this Tilak was handed over by Radharani herself to Shyamananda, and he did not change anything. He considered Hridayachaitanya as his Guru, but Jeeva Goswami and Radharani did not change anything. He considered Hridayachaitanya as his Shiksa Guru for further preaching. So, these great personalities went to Jeeva Goswami and Radharani who was a disciple of Lokannath, all went to Jeeva Goswami friend for further preaching.

So, these great personalities all went to Jeeva Goswami friend. Narottam Dashtakul, who was a disciple of Lokannath, also went to Jeeva Goswami to learn. Shyamananda, a disciple of Hridayachaitanya, went to Jeeva Goswami to learn. And Srinivasacharya's disciple was Gopalabhadra Goswami. He also was given under the care of Jeeva Goswami. And Gopalabhadra Goswami had the guidance and guidance like I said. So, I am a disciple and the Hadith was. So, basically, Jiva Goswami is the real authority on Gaudiya Vaishnavism. Prabhupada said Jiva Goswami is the authority, especially because of the shakshandarva he wrote conclusive truth about Krishna, Krishna is the Bhagavan Swayam. And then Baladeva Dhyabhushan, today morning you went to his birthplace. So, these personalities actually fixed Gaudiya Vaishnavism as authorized parampara within the Madhya Gaudiya parampara. Especially the Ramanandis and the Ramanujacharyas, they were saying there is no authority to the Gaudiya Vaishnavism, where is your siddhanta coming from and where is your bhashya on the Brahma Sutras. Unless we have a bhashya on Brahma Sutras, we are not going to be able to understand Brahma Sutras. You are not considered an authorized line, because Sri Vaishnavas have their authorized bhashya, Madhya has their authorized bhashya. But what about Gaudiya Vaishnavism?

So, Prabhupada Goswami, Sanatana Goswami, Jeeva Goswami, Vishwanath Chakravarti Thakur, Baladeva Dhyabhushan. ### How Can I Overcome Envy Toward Specific Devotees? | Sri Jai Chaitanya Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Jai Chaitanya Dasa

#### envy, judgment, qualification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/wiA0mcCYCUI?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So you are saying that when unqualified people progress, I feel envious."
**Summary:** The speaker addresses the questioner's feelings of envy towards devotees perceived as unqualified who are progressing.

Hare Krishna, Prabhu Dhanwad Pranam. Prabhu, we see this feeling of enviousness is not for everybody. Suppose there are two devotees in ashram who are doing very nice things and they are achieving. So one devotee's nature is very good. He is submissive and humble and he treats everybody nicely. So when he is progressing, I feel good for him. I am not feeling enviousness. But on the other hand, some other devotees who are not respectful or who don't care for others, if he is progressing, then I feel kind of hatred or I would say jealousy. Why is it so that I am not having enviousness for everybody? Same. But for some particular kind of people only. So you are saying, you don't mind qualified people progressing. But you mind when unqualified people progress.

---
#### karma, detachment, judgment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/wiA0mcCYCUI?t=58
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So that itself is wrong from our side because we don't actually, but it is sometimes common, we don't, we should not, by seeing what he is getting, we should not judge whether he is qualified or not qualified."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that judging qualification based on visible achievements is flawed, as karma also plays a role.

First of all, how do you classify qualified or unqualified? So you are saying that when unqualified people progress, I feel envious. How can unqualified people progress? Not possible. Whatever achievements they are getting, it is by sanction of Krishna and Prabhupada only. So what you are looking, what you are saying is, you are talking about the facilities he is getting or the position he is getting. You are measuring qualified or not qualified in terms of the visible positions, visible things he is getting. So that itself is wrong from our side because we don't actually, but it is sometimes common, we don't, we should not, by seeing what he is getting, we should not judge whether he is qualified or not qualified. Because karma provides, not Krishna. Right? If some devotee is getting something, he is not really qualified, but he has got something. Karma is also playing.

---
#### maya, detachment, judgment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/wiA0mcCYCUI?t=120
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So our measure of who is qualified, who is not qualified is totally wrong."
**Summary:** The speaker cautions against judging devotees based on external appearances or current circumstances, as maya can be deceptive.

But don't think that, oh, Krishna has given, Krishna has blessed him. Or sometimes you find externally a devotee very, very humble, crawling down. Humble so that he crawls down. Paka sadhana, pakka externals. Ah, he got a PhD. I feel happy. But it could be the reverse. Maybe he is a duplicitous person. Right? Where bhakti can be, drama is possible. Sometime in life, maya will hit him. Maya is not going to spare anybody. But maybe now maya is favoring him. He has got some facility. So our measure of who is qualified, who is not qualified is totally wrong. Could be wrong.

---
#### detachment, Krishna consciousness, brotherhood
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/wiA0mcCYCUI?t=165
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Best thing is always feel that whether it is karma or not, Krishna has provided him."
**Summary:** The speaker advises against comparison and encourages seeing Krishna as the provider for all, fostering a sense of brotherhood.

So the best thing is not to compare. Best thing is always feel that whether it is karma or not. Krishna has provided him. We always conclude that Krishna has provided him. My father has provided him. My father will provide me also whenever I am ready. My father has provided him. So when we identify the Supreme Lord as a father, and all other living beings as brothers and sisters, then the same father is providing for him, same father is providing for me too. So when we try to compare and analyze, so when we try to compare and analyze too much, we get in the wrong path.

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of finding solace in spiritual philosophy and the Shastras, rather than mental justifications.

So it is not our position or our requirement just to console. You have to take spiritual philosophy as a consoling matter. Feel consoled by the Shastra. Don't feel consoled by mental justifications. And we should feel, what is Shastra saying? So by absorbing oneself in the philosophy, one should feel consoled. Yeah, sometimes it may pain.

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We should feel peaceful ourselves. We should feel full. What Shastras say."

Or maybe there was a mistake in the management also. Nobody is God. The management can also make mistake or this person should have got better, that person somehow got it. Nobody is God. Mistakes can happen. Depend on Krishna. So what you said is correct. Be happy for others. That's good. Be happy for others. That's a good qualification. But don't try to justify my happiness, my peace of mind, because of certain judgments I make. We should feel peaceful ourselves. We should feel full. What Shastras say. Based on that, we should feel full.

### Learning to Develop Divine Consciousness | Sri Jai Chaitanya Dasa | ISKCON Bangalore
**Speaker:** HG Jai Chaitanya Dasa

#### divine consciousness, duality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Uq_oxS2uPsw?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Unless you are in divine consciousness, it is not possible to cross over the dualities."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that without divine consciousness, one cannot overcome the dualities of material existence.

Unless we get divine consciousness, we cannot cross over the tribunals. We cannot cross over the dualities. Profit will affect us. Name and fame will affect us. Loss will disturb us. Facilities not provided will disturb us. Too much facilities will make us proud. Both these things are affecting. Dualities are there. Unless you are in divine consciousness, it is not possible.

---
#### devotional service, duty, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Uq_oxS2uPsw?t=22
**Verse References:** BG 2.47
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Your only work in this material world is to do your duty without looking for the results."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of performing one's duty without attachment to the results, citing the Supreme Lord's instructions.

A man engaged in devotional service rids himself of both good and bad actions. That is where the Supreme Lord is saying that your only work in this material world is to do your duty without looking for the results, whether it is profit or loss, success or failure.

---
#### consciousness, action, spiritual practice
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Uq_oxS2uPsw?t=38
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Easy to change outside, then inside automatically follows outside."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that acting without seeking results leads to fixed consciousness, and that in spiritual life, changing external actions can influence internal change.

When you act in such a way where we are not looking for the result, you will slowly, slowly get fixed in consciousness. So the body is you doing all sinful activities. Your mind will also be sinful. Other way round also. If the mind is always sinful, the body will also follow the mind. When there is acceptance, when the external body is acting, something is happening inside also. The ability to cross beyond dualities is happening inside. That is why we say external actions are important to change the insides. Many times we see psychologically, insides if you change, outside you can change. But in spiritual life, easy to change outside, then inside automatically follows outside.

### Legacy of Srila Narottama Dasa Thakura & Disciples | Sri Jai Chaitanya Dasa| Gaura Mandala Parikrama
**Speaker:** HG Jai Chaitanya Dasa

#### Narottama Das Thakur, Disappearance, Pastimes
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AN0Io1sU7_g?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In the year 1611 A.D., the bank of Bajirathi, where Narottama Das Thakur entered the river and vanished, just as milk dissolves in water, is now known as Duktha and is an exalted place of pilgrimage."
**Summary:** This segment describes the location and circumstances of Narottama Das Thakur's disappearance.

So, this is the spot where Narottam Das Thakur had his last Lila. Narottam Das Thakur was absorbed in singing the glories of Lord Gauranga and Nityananda, day and night. By the mercy of his lotus feet, several atheists, agnostics, offenders, demigod worshippers, dry logicians, speculators, etc. were all delivered. Hearing the terrible news of the disappearance of Sri Ramachandra Kaviraj, who was a very dear friend, Narottama plunged into an ocean of grief. Added to his grief, shortly, the spiritual master of Ramachandra, Sri Srinivasacharya, also left this planet. Arriving at Gambila, Narottam Das Thakur ordered his disciples, Sri Ramakrishna and Ganga Narayan Chakravarti, to arrange for his bathing in Padma. He entered the waters after repeatedly paying his obeisances. Standing there in the water, he then ordered his disciples to wipe his body. Then, as Ganga Narayan and Ramakrishna were cleansing the body of their spiritual master, Sri Narottama merged into the waters of Padma and vanished from worldly view. His disappearance was just as milk vanishes in water. It is believed that Thakur Mahasaya wrapped up his earthly Lila and manifested his disappearance.

---
#### Vaishnava philosophy, Lamentation, Devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AN0Io1sU7_g?t=119
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "One of his biggest contribution was Pratana, a lot of songs which very clearly explains the Gurdwaraya Vaishnava philosophy."
**Summary:** This segment discusses Narottama Das Thakur's contributions, his lamentation, and his dedication to Lord Gauranga.

In the year 1611 A.D., the bank of Bajirathi, where Narottama Das Thakur entered the river and vanished, just as milk dissolves in water, is now known as Duktha and is an exalted place of pilgrimage. So, that is the very spot we are sitting in, the greatest spot, the greatest devotee of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. One of his biggest contribution was Pratana, a lot of songs which very clearly explains the Gurdwaraya Vaishnava philosophy. And it is said that Gork laboratory mother would read no book but that one little chocolate continues today Tling tan philosophy is hidden in his songs. We will also read the same pastime from Narottama Vilasa, the biography of Narottama by Sri Narahari Chakravarti Thakur. One day after in the morning, Narottama Das Thakura began to helplessly shed tears while signing deeply. He lay down on the ground for some time and his lotus-like face became pale. With a choked-up voice, he sang this song in great lamentation. Swaroopa, Roopa, Sanatana, Raghunatha and the Bhattas, be kind to me. Acharya Srinivasa, whose servant is Ramachandra, can I have their association once again? Without seeing their face, my heart is pierced, just like a doe struck by a poisonous arrow. I kept my jewels hidden under my cloth. Who has stolen them? Why is Narottama suffering so much? After singing this, Narottama Das Thakur remained silent and everyone became aware of what would soon occur. Knowing the mentality of his dear disciples, Narottama Das Thakur consoled them. He then went to the courtyard of Lord Gauranga's temple and once again dedicated himself to the lotus feet of the Lord.

Afterwards he departed to Yudhuri with Govinda, Govinda Kaviraj and some other devotees. He stayed there for one day and Sri Govinda Chakravarti and others took the opportunity to meet him. After talking to them very sweetly, Narottama Das Thakur spent the rest of the day and night performing Sankirtana. From Yudhuri, Narottama Das went to Gamvila. arranged for the funeral at Gamvila. They then hastily went to Kethudi by way of Vidhuri. There, King Narsimha, Rupanarayana, Krishna Simha, Chandanaraya, Sri Gopiramana, Sri Govinda, King Santosharaya and others started preparation for a great festival. Due to fear of unnecessarily increasing the size of this book, I am restraining myself from giving an elaborate description of the festival. When the devotees started Sankirtana in the courtyard of Lord Gauranga's temple, Devi Dasa, Gauranga, Gokula, Govinda Kaviraj and others soon forgot their external identities and began to roll on the ground. They rolled like lions and soaked the ground with their tears. Suddenly, due to the favor of their guru, Krishna, and the Vaishnavas, everyone had a chance to witness Narottama Das dancing with them again. When he suddenly vanished, everyone lost their composure. And so Narottama Das Thakur once again appeared and consoled them all. Who can comprehend the transcendental qualities of Sri Thakura Mahasaya? In the hopes of gaining his favor, be attentive while hearing Narottama Vilas narrated by Narahari. Hi, Narottama Das Thakur! This place is also a famous place for some Lilas performed by his disciples. This is one disciple called Ganga Narayan Chakraborty. We will read about him also, because from here we will go to his temple that is the golden Gauranga deity of Bado Govinda Mahana, the foremost of Narottama Das Thakur's disciples. He appeared to be a great disciple. He was Śrī Rādhā Veda. He was really a great disciple.

here in Jiyaganj as the sacred place where Narottam Das Thakur merged into the Ganges and disappeared from this world. Jiyaganj was previously known as Gamvila. Shri Ganga Narayan was very rich and famous. He was a renowned scholar. He used to impart education unto hundreds of students daily. The great stalwart Shri Vishwanath Chakravati Thakur was also one of his dedicated students. When Shri Ganga Narayan Chakravati, a renowned brahmana, took initiation from Shri Narottam Das Thakura, the Smartha brahmanas became outraged. They complained to King Narasimha that the audacious Shudra, Narottam Das, had brazenly initiated a brahmana into the fold of Gaudiya Vaishnavism. The king, the Smarthas, and the Digvijaya Pandit were to challenge and conquer the upstart Narottam Das. Krishna's devotees peacefully tolerate all insults and abuses to themselves, but they strike like fire against offenders to their beloved Guru or worshipable Lord Shri Krishna. Ganga Narayan Chakravati and Ramachandra Kaviraj felt disturbed upon hearing of the imminent attack against their Gurudeva. Disguising themselves as a potter, they went to the Ganges. They were accompanied by a big pot of grass. They were also given a bucket full of sugar, a small bottle of water, and a cup of water. When they were taken to the well, there was a potter and a paanwala, they opened shops in a neighboring village. Upon arriving there, the Smarthas sent their students to buy a paan and a clay pot for cooking. Both the potter who was Ramachandra Kaviraj and the paanwala who was Ganga Narayan spoke pure Sanskrit with the students. Soon an argument stirred the ethers. The Smartha king Narasimha, the only one who was the… he take to the school… Then the students got flowers. The students left. The students got flowers. The Smarthas

#### King Narasimha, Initiation, Golden Gauranga Deity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AN0Io1sU7_g?t=593
**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** This segment tells how King Narasimha and the Dikvijaya Pandit took initiation from Narottam Das Thakur and introduces the Golden Gauranga Deity.

King Narasimha and Sri Rupa Narayana, the Dikvijaya Pandit, joined the furious debate. The two Walas, that is the Paanwala and the Potwala, quickly silenced the opposition. When he understood they were Narottam Das' disciples, the King told the Smartha Pandits, if you can't even defeat these two common ordinary disciples of Narottam Das Thakur, how will you even defeat Narottam Das himself? Humiliated, the Pandits headed home. That night, in a dream, Durga Devi told King Narasimha and the Dikvijaya Pandit, take initiation from Narottam Das Thakur, or else I will cut off your heads. The next day, they surrendered and received Radhakrishna Mantra, Diksha, from Narottam Das Thakura. The Samadhi of Ganga Narayan Chakravarti is in Vrindavan, Radha Gokul and the temple. Next, Bada Govinda Bari, the Golden Gauranga Deity. At Bada Govinda Bari Temple, rests today the enchanting Golden Gauranga Deity, that was once worshipped by Narottam Das Thakura. There is a very interesting pastime regarding how Narottam Das Thakur discovered this Gauranga

---
#### Gauranga Deity, Narottama Das Thakur, Devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AN0Io1sU7_g?t=676
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So when Narottam Das Thakura arrived at the villa at the residence of Ganga Narayan for the final time before he merged with the Ganges, he had carried this golden Gauravishniya Deity along with him from Keturi."
**Summary:** This segment narrates the story of how Narottama Das Thakur discovered the Gauranga Deity and how it came to reside at Bada Govinda Bari.

Deity. In the province of Gopalpur, there lived a pious Brahmana. His name was Vipra Dasa. Mahaprabhu revealed to Narottam Das Thakura in his dream one night that his beloved Gauravishnupriya Deities were presently resting at the house of this Brahmana. These Deities lay hidden in his huge container of paddy. When Mahaprabhu left Bengal for Puri, after accepting his sannyas initiation, he manifested these enchanting Deities and hid them inside his paddy. In the course of time, they landed up in the house of this Brahmana. But this Brahmana was completely unaware of this. So when Narottam Das Thakur arrived at the house of this Brahmana the next day and wanted to look into his huge mount of paddy, the Brahmana warned him that a huge venomous snake lived in it, and it was not safe to go near it. Regardless, Shri Narottama entered the paddy container. He was very happy. He entered the paddy container, and immediately the huge snake appeared and walked out of the mount. All present were very filled with wonder. Soon Thakura Mahasaya discovered his beloved Gauravishnupriya Deities from the paddy and grabbed them dearly. The onlookers began loudly glorifying Narottam Das and shouted, Haribol! Haribol! Haribol! Haribol! Wherever Narottam Das Thakur went, he carried his beloved Gauravishnupriya Deity to the mountain. Haribol! Haribol! Haribol! He took his beloved Gauravishnupriya Deity and took him along with him. Narottam Das was mad in ecstatic love with his beloved Gauravishnupriya Deity. So when Narottam Das Thakura arrived at the villa at the residence of Ganga Narayan for the final time before he merged with the Ganges, he had carried this golden Gauravishniya Deity along with him from Keturi. This beautiful Deity has remained at Badok Govindabhari ever since.

### Lord Chaitanya transforms the heart of Chand Kazi | Sri Sampati Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Jai Chaitanya Dasa

#### Sankirtana, Transformation, Shravanam
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-Lq-nkt6nws?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The process of Shravanam is so powerful, it can immediately awaken Krishna Prema within our hearts."
**Summary:** This segment narrates the story of Lord Chaitanya's civil disobedience movement against Chand Kazi, highlighting the transformative power of hearing (Shravanam) from the Lord.

Nadia district, where Navadvip is located, was under the jurisdiction of Chand Kazi, who worked under Nawab Hussain Shah. Under the Kazi's order, his soldiers disrupted Sankirtana, congregational chanting of the Lord's holy names, on the streets of Navadvip. Interestingly, Lord Chaitanya then conducted the very first civil disobedience movement, attended by thousands of devotees, to protest the order of the Kazi and the actions of his soldiers. By seeing that big gathering, Chand Kazi got scared, and he hid in the home. Somehow, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu convinced Chand Kazi to come out and had a debate with him and transformed his heart. In this way, Chand Kazi became a great Vaishnava. It is because it is a result of hearing from Lord Chaitanya. Shravanam, the process of Shravanam, is so powerful. Krishna Raj Kaviraj Goswami says this Shravanam process can immediately awaken Krishna Prema within our hearts.

### Never Judge a Vaishnava | Sri Jai Chaitanya Dasa | Sri Gaura Mandala Parikrama 2024
**Speaker:** HG Jai Chaitanya Dasa

#### mercy, acceptance, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/RqnNLQtdXlI?t=0
**Verse References:** SB 3.2.23
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord accepts the least qualification of the living entity and awards him the highest reward; that is a standard of his character."
**Summary:** This segment focuses on the extreme mercy of the Lord, exemplified by his acceptance of Putana, and emphasizes that even small acts of devotion are greatly rewarded.

So, we read from Srimad Bhagavatam, 3rd Canto, Chapter 2, Text 23, the matter of remembrance of Lord Krishna. Here is an example of the extreme mercy of the Lord, even to his enemy. It is said that a noble man accepts the good qualities of a person of doubtful character, just as one accepts nectar from a stalk of poison. In his babyhood, he was administered deadly poison by Putana, a Shri demon who tried to kill the wonderful baby. And because she was a demon, it was impossible for her to know that the Supreme Lord, even though playing the part of a baby, was no one else than the same Supreme Person. His value as the Supreme Lord did not diminish upon his becoming a baby to please his devotee, Yashoda. The Lord may assume the form of a baby or a shape other than that of a human being, but it doesn't make the slightest difference. He is always the same Supreme. A living creature, however powerful he may become by dint of severe penance, can never become equal to the Supreme Lord. The Lord accepted the motherhood of Putana because she pretended to be an affectionate mother, allowing Krishna to suck her breast. The Lord accepts the least qualification of the living entity and awards him the highest reward; that is a standard of his character. Therefore, who but the Lord can be the ultimate shelter? Spiritual life done alone, parikrama done alone, these are all not as powerful as doing it in the company of a sadhu sanga. So sadhu sanga, that is the most important thing.

---
#### surrender, offenses, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/RqnNLQtdXlI?t=139
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord does not take our limitations, but the Lord does consider offenses done to another Vaishnava."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of sadhu sanga, avoiding offenses to Vaishnavas, and the value of surrender and dedication to the Lord, even amidst limitations and responsibilities.

As bhakti, we know said he decides to do this along with the sanga of maestro. So we are very fortunate that we get this opportunity to do this, and we pray that we do this parikrama with the least dhamma parath, the least ways from a parath, the least mama parath. We are all coming to light conditioned souls. So there are chances that we think differently. So we pray that we don't offend the Dham, the Vaishnavas, we go with. The Lord accepts Putana, demoness, because she had that few minutes of affection, at least to show affection. And the Lord has granted her such a high position as his mother. So the Lord does not take our limitations, but the Lord does consider offenses done to another Vaishnava. Offenses done because that shows that we don't recognize his creation, all the living entities. So keeping away from these offenses, respectful to all the Vaishnavas, doing Sankirtan is very important. The Lord will recognize, even though we have limitations, he will recognize that moments of surrender we do to him. So this act of 20 days dedicating to him itself is a surrender. Keeping aside so many things, so many important work would be there, but we keep that aside. So many family responsibilities are there, so many business responsibilities are there. We keep them all aside, and we come and dedicate. That is such a big surrender. The Lord will recognize these things.

#### endeavor, dedication, inspiration
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/RqnNLQtdXlI?t=260
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "That dedication we have for the Supreme Lord is what all that matters."
**Summary:** This segment highlights that even small endeavors are recognized by the Lord, and that dedication to the Supreme Lord is what truly matters, regardless of one's position or service.

The difficulties we may go through, the difficulties to catch up with the team, right, to be together, so all these are different surrender, different different austerities of the mind, austerities of the body. So it's an opportunity to do all these things. So the Lord will recognize that. So here this verse is also apt. With all the limitations, Putana gets a high position. So imagine a small endeavor from our side, how much the Lord would provide, how much the Lord would give credit. Ultimately, that is the most important thing, irrespective of what we do, what service we do, what positions we have, what ashram we are in, what order of life we are in, that dedication we have for the Supreme Lord is what all that matters, right? So let us do this sankalpa and continue this parikrama, and this verse is very much connected to our parikrama also. This verse was one of the verses which inspired Pundarika Vidyanidhi, whose place we will be visiting, which will be the last of the parikrama in Chattogram, and he shifted from here to Navadvip to take company of the Supreme Lord, and he in Krishna lila, he is considered as Radharani's father, Vishwa Bhanu, king and elderly, very rich, very opulent, very charming personality with a lot of servants, a lot of perfumes, a lot of wealth. So Gadadhar Pandit had a tinge of doubt, you see a devotee at all, right, who was walking such opulent way.

#### ecstasy, offenses, shelter
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/RqnNLQtdXlI?t=370
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Seeing that he could understand what a great personality he is just by hearing this mercy of the Supreme Lord to Putana he has gone into this ecstatic position."
**Summary:** This segment narrates the story of Gadadhar Pandit's initial doubt about Pundarika Vidyanidhi, which was dispelled when he witnessed Pundarika's ecstatic reaction to hearing about the Lord's mercy to Putana, leading Gadadhar to take shelter of him.

But then who is that Govinda Mukunda who took Gadadhara to Pundarika Vidyanidhi to meet a great devotee? So Gadadhar Pandit saw his house so opulent, and he is chewing pan always, red mouth, big couch sitting there. But then I think it is Mukunda who sang this verse. The moment this verse was chanted, Pundarika went into the rapture of ecstasy, and he was like mad man. So seeing that he could understand what a great personality he is just by hearing this mercy of the Supreme Lord to Putana he has gone into this ecstatic position. He kind of threw everything, broke everything in that room. Nobody could control him. He was so mad in love of Krishna. So that is why Gadadhar said, I offended him. Now I should take shelter of him. I should take and he took initiation from Pundarika Vidyanidhi. So Gadadhar Pandita, an expansion of Radharani, Pundarika, an expansion of Vishwabanna, so they connect, they get connected in Gaur Lila in that form, right? So it's an important verse for us and good words to remember, Pundarika Vidyanidhi. So all Vaishnavas, let us pay once again obeisance to each other. We were being pray for an ordinary life as God Almighty gave us an ultimate role and the same does for us. So let us pray for an eternal life. Ablution we ask that you also be kind to all human beings. Let us pray for an eternal life. So God Almighty take care of us, Lord of all the souls in this world we ask that you vegans and all of us may all be well and may all of us live in peace. May all of us live in peace. May all of us live in peace. May all of us live in peace. Pati tana pavanibhyo vaishnavibhyo namo namah. Ananta koti vaishravandhaki ghoro mandal paritrama bhaktavrindhaki inathai ghor prema.

### Ramachandra Khan's Plot Against Haridasa | Sri Jai Chaitanya Dasa | Sri Gaura Mandala Parikrama 2024
**Speaker:** HG Jai Chaitanya Dasa

#### Haridas Thakur, Benapol, Bhajana
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/8SVAJN1DuAo?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Srila Haridas Thakura's name and fame soon began spreading far and wide."
**Summary:** This segment introduces Benapol as the place where Haridas Thakur stayed after leaving his home, established his bhajana kutira, planted tulasi, and chanted 300,000 holy names daily, leading to his widespread fame.

Glorious pastimes of Benapol. Soon after leaving his home at Boron, Srila Haridas Thakur spent a few days here at Benapol, which is located in Jesho district of Bangladesh. The place, in fact, is very near to the international border post between India and Bangladesh. Let me offer my respectful obeisances unto Haridas Thakura and his master Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, who danced with the body of Haridas Thakur on his lap. Choosing a solitary forest for his stay over here at Benapol, Haridas Thakur set up a cottage and established his bhajana kutira. He planted tulasi, and in front of tulasi devi, he used to chant 300,000 holy names of Lord Krishna daily. He chanted through the day and night. In order to sustain his body, he used to beg for food from a brahmana who lived nearby. Srila Haridas Thakura's name and fame soon began spreading far and wide.
