#### Envy, Plot, Temptation, Haridas Thakur
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/8SVAJN1DuAo?t=111
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He found it difficult to tolerate that Haridas Thakur was being offered such respect."
**Summary:** This segment describes Ramachandra Khan's envy of Haridas Thakur's fame, his plot to defame him using a prostitute named Hira Bai, and Hira Bai's initial attempt to tempt Haridas, which was unsuccessful as he asked her to wait until he finished his chanting.

Everyone began respecting and revering him. However, there lived an envious landlord by the name of Ramachandra Khar. He was envious of Vaishnavas and a great atheist. He found it difficult to tolerate that Haridas Thakur was being offered such respect. Thus, he began plotting ways and means to defame and dishonour Srila Haridas. Being unable to find any fault, he started practicing dharma. He refused to do any good to anyone. With him, he conspired with the prostitute and asked them to lure Haridas and deviate him from his vow of austerity. Among those prostitutes, one young and very attractive girl named Hira Bai was selected. Hira was very confident and assured Ramachandra Khan that she would be able to accomplish her job within three days. Ramachandra wanted to send a constable with Hira so that he could arrest Haridas as soon as he would try to get anywhere close to the girl. However, Hira Bai wanted to get intimate with Haridas Chakur at least once before Haridas was arrested. So, she requested Ramachandra Khan to send a constable when she would unite with Haridas for the second time. So, as planned, Hira Bai approached the cottage of Haridas Chakur in the middle of the night. She dressed herself very well and stood there exposing a part of her body to his view. She flattered Haridas Chakur, saying how beautiful he looked and how desperate she was to enjoy him. She explained that it would not be possible for her to continue living if she does not get to unite with Srila Haridas. Tumara Sangam Alagi Lubdhamo Ramana Tumana Paile Pranana Yaya Dharana. Chaitanya Chaita Amrita Antya 3.11.3. Haridas Chakur listened to the girl carefully and assured that he would definitely accept her. However, he requested her to wait until he had finished chanting his spiritual number of rounds. He said that he would fulfill her desires after that. So, Hira sat there and waited for Haridas to finish his chanting. When the light of the morning appeared, Hira stood up and left disappointed. She went and updated her mind. She said,

#### Transformation, Surrender, Forgiveness, Lakshahira
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/8SVAJN1DuAo?t=259
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The night gradually passed and the chanting of the holy name slowly began to have its effect on the consciousness of the prostitute."
**Summary:** This segment narrates Hira Bai's transformation through Haridas Thakur's influence, her confession, repentance, acceptance of Haridas as her spiritual master, and her subsequent life of devotion, leading her to be known as Lakshahira.

She was not able to enjoy with her, but he assured her that he would definitely complete his rounds the next day, and then they could unite as agreed. He left only to return the following night. The next day he arrived by evening and waited eagerly for Haridas to complete his songs. The night gradually passed and the chanting of the holy name slowly began to have its effect on the consciousness of the prostitute. She had a change of heart and soon surrendered at the lotus feet of Haridas. She confessed her guilt and revealed how Ramachandra Khan had appointed her to defame and pollute the takura. She begged forgiveness and expressed how, being engaged in the profession of a prostitute, she had committed countless offenses in her life. She accepted Haridas as her spiritual master and sought his guidance. Haridas replied that he knew everything about the conspiracy of Ramachandra Khan, whom he then addressed as an ignorant fool. He added that he could have left Benapol on the very day Ramachandra Khan was planning all of this, but he had only stayed back to deliver Hirabai. Haridas Taku then instructed Hira to immediately return home and distribute whatever property she possessed to the brahmanas. After that, she was ordered to return to that very place and chant the holy name of Krishna in front of tulasi devi for the rest of her life. Instructing thus, he stood up and left. Hira did accordingly, and she went on to become a great vaishnavi. She shaved off her head and resided in that cottage wearing only one cloth. She chanted three lakh holy names daily and ate whatever little food she obtained by begging. When she would obtain no arm, she would fast day and night. She chanted and served tulasi devi with utmost devotion. Gradually, she conquered her senses, and the symptoms of love of God began manifesting within her body. She continued to worship the holy name of Krishna, and she became a great vaishnavi.

#### Karma, Consequences, Haridas Thakur, Departure

**Summary:** This segment concludes the story of Ramachandra Khan with his arrest and the destruction of his village, emphasizes the consequences of offending devotees, and briefly describes Haridas Thakur's later life and departure.

Ramachandra Khan's business was questionable, and he avoided paying taxes to the government. Soon the Muslim ministers found this out, and they arrested Ramachandra Khan along with his wife and sons. They stayed at his Durga Mantapa and, having killed cow, cooked it over there for three continuous days. They plundered the village, and as a result, it remained deserted for many years. This is what happens when offenses are committed. Wherever an exalted devotee of the Lord is insulted, even if it's a single person's doing, the entire town or village ends up suffering the terrible reaction. Mahantera Pamanaye Deshak Grama Haya Eka Janara Dose Saba Desha Ujjadhaya. Haridas Thakur, life after living in Benapur. After living in Benapur, he had a dream. After living in Benapur, Haridas Thakur shifted to Adi Sabta Grama, and then he stayed for a few days at Puliya near Shantipur. Over there, he had enacted some amazing pastimes like surviving even after being whipped at 22 marketplaces, initiating Maya Devi, etc. Thereafter, he shifted to Jagannath Puri along with Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, and he subsequently put an end to his manifested pastimes there. **Summary:** This segment describes the holy places associated with Haridas Thakur and Lakshahira Devi in Benapol, including the Madhavi tree, Bhajan Kutira, and Tamala tree, emphasizing the importance of these places for remembrance and devotion.

So here we have the sacred Madhavi tree. We have the Bhajan Kutira of Lakshah Hira Devi and the same compound. Lord Chaitanya and Advaita Acharya had previously met. And there stands a Tamala tree where they sat and discussed. Advaita Acharya had planted this around 500 years ago. One is Haridas' Madhavi tree where he chanted. Other one is Advaita planted a sacred Tamala. This is Hira Devi. Haridas Thakur stayed few days only, and this became the Bhajan Kutira of Lakshah Hira. From his village he came here. When is not given. Date is not given. He wanted to go away from the people and the town and the village. So he came here and chanted. From here he went. It is right across the river. You can cross the river and access many of those places in Navadvimya. Namacharya Haridas Thakur Ki Lakshah Hira Devi Ki. Go to heaven.

### Sri Balaram Jayanti Festival Discourse 2025 | His Grace Jai Chaitanya Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Jai Chaitanya Dasa

#### Balarama's importance, Purification, Krishna's enjoyment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/OcQ46N3pDaM?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Unless he comes and purifies, Krishna cannot come there."
**Summary:** This segment introduces Balarama as Krishna's elder brother and first expansion, emphasizing his role in purification, enabling Krishna's appearance and enjoyment, and highlighting that Balarama is the true God of Liberation.

Welcome to all of you on this occasion of Balaram Purnima, Balaram Jayanti. Balaram Jayanti comes always before Krishna Jayanti, and Balaram appeared as the elder brother of Krishna, elder brother of God. Then the elder brother should be the God? But no, our God is Krishna, the Supreme Personality of God. Sarvakarana Karanam. He is Raso Vaisaha. He is full of Rasa. His existence is full of Rasa, interactive enjoyment. Purpose of life is enjoyment, but we do not know how to enjoy because we try to catch all diaramas for enjoyment, and diaramas don't give enjoyment. Only real life gives enjoyment. So in the spiritual world, there is only enjoyment, Satchik, Ananda, eternity, knowledge, and bliss. This is the existence in the spiritual world. So to this material world, the Supreme Lord comes in different, different forms. In Goloka Vrindavan, where Krishna, the Supreme Lord, is always enjoying, he has his brother, elder brother, because he wanted an elder brother. Because he wanted elder brother, who can become his elder brother? He wanted a friend. Who can be his friend? Only he himself can. So, the first expansion, the first incarnation in one sense, the first expansion is Balaram, Baladeva. So whenever a person says, Balaram is the true God of Liberation. Whenever Krishna appears, before that Balarama appears. Just like in the womb of Devaki also, Balarama appeared first, just before Krishna. However, it was considered as a miscarriage. Why? Because he was attracted by the womb of Rohini. Hence, Sankarsana, he took birth as Rohini Nandan rather than Devaki Nandan. Krishna is Devaki Nandan, Krishna is Ashodan Nandan. Balarama is Rohini Nandan, another wife of Vasudeva. But he appears. Why does he appear? Because unless he comes and purifies, Krishna cannot come there.

---
#### Spiritualization, Guru, Spiritual Strength
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/OcQ46N3pDaM?t=244
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "God cannot accept anything material. He can only accept anything spiritual. And only Balarama can spiritualize things."
**Summary:** This segment explains that only Balarama can spiritualize material things for Krishna's worship. It also establishes Balarama as the Adiguru Nithyananda, emphasizing the necessity of his mercy to attain Krishna and highlighting that Balarama provides spiritual strength (Bala).

Only when Balarama comes and purifies, then the seat is worth to invite Krishna. So, even in our heart, unless Balarama appears first, Krishna cannot come. And in Chaitanya Leela, Krishna appeared as Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and Balarama appeared as Nithyananda Prabhu. So, if you want to get the mercy of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, we have to get the mercy of Nithyananda, who is Balarama. All the paraphernalia we use to worship Krishna is Balarama. All the pots, all the aarati, all the items we use to worship Krishna, because no material things can be used to worship Krishna. God cannot accept anything material. He can only accept anything spiritual. And only Balarama can spiritualize things. So, Pujaris, before they worship, they spiritualize everything with the Pancharatra mantras and come to the level of Krishna's spirituality to worship him with all the spiritual things, which represents Balarama. His bedding is Balarama. His pillow is Balarama. Burdanga is Balarama. Kartal is Balarama. So, for a devotee, everything is to be respectfully used. The asana, the vigraha, all the asanas you make for the Supreme Lord, the altar you make for the Supreme Lord, that is also Sankarsana. So, the Lord, Lord Vishnu only sleeps on Sankarsana. You can provide him any pillow, goose chest pillow, he will not accept. He will only lie down on Sankarsana. So, Sankarsana, Balarama, is the most important personality in all of our spiritual life because with his blessing, with his mercy, we can get Krishna. We can know Krishna. So, the person who takes us to Krishna, that is Guru. Hence, Balarama is Adiguru Nithyananda. He is Adiguru. Unless we get the blessing of Guru, Shri Guru, we cannot get the Supreme Lord. So, and Guru, what is Guru? Guru is a mercy incarnation of the Supreme Lord. Hence, Balarama is a merciful incarnation of Krishna who shows the way. So, Balarama also gives Bala, not bodybuilding Bala, but spiritual Bala. Because we need spiritual Bala to get Krishna, not physical Bala. Physical Bala will help you to do Krishna Seva better, but won't give Krishna. That Bala comes from Balarama.

---
#### Krishna's Satisfaction, Relationship with God, Spiritual Spark
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/OcQ46N3pDaM?t=382
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord always wants to associate with the relationship, and that is our Swaroopa, relationship with the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment describes Balarama's subservient role to Krishna, always seeking Krishna's satisfaction. It emphasizes that Krishna is happiest when addressed in relation to his devotees and that our true nature (Swaroopa) is to have a loving relationship with the Lord, like a spark to a fire.

So, in Vrindavan, Krishna and Balarama, even though Balarama is elder to Krishna, he will only say yes to Krishna, anything he wants. In the house, if our elder brothers stay like this, we will not be able to get Krishna. If our elder brothers stay like this, how good it could have been. But elder brothers are not like that. But Balarama, the elder brother, his only wish is to satisfy Krishna, because he was also put into confusion at the time of Mahabharata Yuta, which side will Krishna have into Pandavas. I like Duryodhana, but I can't fight against Krishna. So, he went for Tirta Yatra. So, he never does anything against the Vishnu of Krishna. In Dwaraka, he protects Dwaraka. He and him and Krita Verma, under him, the entire Narayaniya, Vanakshahuni soldiers, army of Balarama. The entire army was given to Kauravas, under Krita Verma. That's all the complexities of warfare. But Balarama always wants the satisfaction of his younger brother. And Krishna also does not like to be called Krishna. See Krishna, not very happy. You call him Balabhadra Anuja, he is very happy. Krishna Purvaja, that is Balarama. You call him Partha Sarathi, he is happy. You call him Rukmini, Rukripyatma, he is happy. You call him Devaki Nandan, he is happy. Krishna is happy. So, the Lord always wants to associate with the relationship, and that is our Swaroopa, relationship with the Lord. It is not that God is a big boss and we are all servants like in the material world, no. Yes, we are servants, but we are part and parcel of the Supreme Lord, and as a part of Him, we too enjoy in His association. Just like the spark is always full of bliss. When the spark is in the fire, as soon as the spark gets out of the fire, it dies away. In the same way, we, the spiritual spark, Amsha of the Supreme Lord Krishna, we too are satisfied only when that connection with Krishna comes back. And that connection with Krishna, if we want, then Balarama.

---
#### Vaishnava Sampradaya, Balarama's Mercy, Spiritual Purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/OcQ46N3pDaM?t=558
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Balarama's mercy is most important."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes Balarama's importance in the Bhagavata and Gaudiya Vaishnava Sampradayas, even more than Krishna, similar to how the Guru is more important than the Lord. It highlights that Balarama's mercy is essential for spiritual purification and establishing a relationship with Krishna.

That is the very most important personality. Hence, in the Bhagavata Sampradaya, Gaudiya Sampradaya, the Vaishnava Sampradaya, which we follow, Balarama is very important, more important than Krishna, just like Guru is more important than the Lord. So Balarama's mercy is most important, and He always sees that only those purified souls get relationship with the Supreme Lord. So for our spiritual purification, He plays a very important role. In the Hare Krishna Mahamantra also, there are three levels of chanting the Hare Krishna Mahamantra. The second level is Krishna and Balarama. Sopa says Balarama provides the strength, and not only strength, Rama also means pleasure potency. So the spiritual pleasure, spiritual bliss, spiritual strength is given by Balarama. Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Rama. And the final stage is Radha Krishna. Rama means Radha Krishna. Jubala Jodi. So he plays a very important role in the purification of the spirit soul in receiving Krishna's mercy. That is the final goal, to receive Krishna. That is not possible unless Balarama is satisfied.

Vaishnavas play a very important role because Guru, Sri Guru appears, instructs the disciple in different ways. It is not that one overnight we get spiritual knowledge. Spiritual knowledge comes from Seva, purifying the senses by doing Seva. That is why Krishna Seva is given so much importance. Rishikena, Rishikesha, Saivana, Bhakti Rucha. Rishikena, Rishikesha, Rishikesha is Supreme Lord Krishna. Rishika is the senses. So when the senses are utilized in serving the Supreme Lord, Rishikesha, then the senses get purified. When the senses get purified, mind gets purified. Intelligence gets purified. The entire existence becomes purified. In that purified existence, chanting Hare Krishna, that makes a connection with the Supreme Lord. It's always Seva. That is why Prabhupada, Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada, our Acharya, made these temples all over the world. He said these are the places, these are all spiritual hubs where you can come and purify. So purification happens in this place. So that is why here in, near Ananagar, he opened this branch. Small place, but any Seva done here with pure mind reaches the Supreme, Goloka Vrindavan. There is no difference between where it is. You may have some, you may have some inconveniences here, but if you do Seva humbly to satisfy Guru and Krishna, it reaches. And Balaram notices that.

#### Liberation, Material World, Intelligence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/OcQ46N3pDaM?t=878
**Verse References:** SB 1.2.6
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "This is not a good place to be in."
**Summary:** This segment explains that Balarama, represented by the spiritual master, aims to liberate living entities from the undesirable material world. It uses the analogy of a meat-eater enjoying sinful activities and the camel eating thorns to illustrate different levels of intelligence regarding the recognition of suffering in the material world.

When Balaram notices that, he gives. So, Balaram, represented here by the spiritual master. They are all watching because the whole idea of spiritual master, Balaram, is to see that the living entities get liberation from this material world. Why should we get liberated from the material world? Because this is not a good place to be in. If you want to know this is not a good place to be in, you should have some negative experiences in this material world. Otherwise, you think this material world is very nice. Like, there is a story. The Western world. Tell a meat eater, Westerner who is meat eater, all kinds of sin he does. He says, don't do sin. Why? I feel good doing all these things. Smoking, meat eating, humanizing, intoxicating. I know good. I feel this enjoyment. No, you will go to hell. No, my family, everybody does this. All of them will go to hell. Oh, then hell is a good place. I will go to hell. All my family members are there. So, if you do not know what is good and what is bad, you will think this is the right enjoyment. But this is not the right enjoyment. We can hear from the Shastras that Krishna himself declares Dukhalayam Asasratam. Don't try to be happy in this world. This is Dukhalayam. This is the place of Dukha. We may hear, we will not understand. So, we commit some activities in the material world and face Dukha. Or then we say, oh, this is Dukha. Some people, even after experiencing Dukha, will continue like a camel eating their thorn, tasting the blood. So, sometimes people like to taste their own blood without knowing. So, this is called third-class intelligence. You taste this dirty world as good.

#### Intelligence, Material Happiness, Purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/OcQ46N3pDaM?t=1026
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Material world cannot give you permanent happiness."
**Summary:** This segment differentiates between second-class and first-class intelligence. Second-class intelligence recognizes the suffering in the material world after experiencing it, while first-class intelligence understands it from the Shastras and spiritual master. It emphasizes that the material world cannot provide lasting happiness due to its inherent limitations.

Second-class intelligence means that you know what is good and what is bad. So, you know what is good and what is bad. So, second-class intelligence is when you get a beating, this place is not good. Let us go back and see what is good. And first-class intelligence is when you hear from the spiritual master, when you hear from the sastras, you already experience yes, material world is not good. Why? Because material world cannot give you permanent happiness. And we all want happiness. Everybody wants a house. Everybody wants a job. Everybody wants a job. Everybody wants a vehicle. Everybody wants a fridge. Everybody wants a TV. Everybody wants a flat. Why? To be happy. But are you happy? No. Why? Because once you get one thing, then there are ten problems connected to that. I want mobile phone, iPhone, earphone, Apple phone, orange phone, but no connectivity. Your house is in a guha. There is no connection. What is this? So, for every desire, there are ten obstructions in this material world. How much can we enjoy with our senses? How much can you eat? So, there is limitation in this material world. But the soul is unlimited. The soul is not to be limited by all these temporary things. So, to free the soul, layer by layer, we need to purify ourselves. Come to the level of Krishna Consciousness where the real bliss comes within the heart.

#### Temples, Seva, Krishna is Bhagawan
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/OcQ46N3pDaM?t=1137
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is to Bhagawan Swayam."
**Summary:** This segment explains the purpose of temples as places for purification through Seva and emphasizes the conclusion that Krishna is Bhagawan Swayam (the Supreme Personality of Godhead), as stated in the Bhagavatam, even though Vishnu is often considered supreme in South India.

So, this is why these temples are made all over the world, where you all can come, come together and do seva, satisfy the Lord and purify ourselves. And gradually, gradually, gradually, come to the conclusion that Krishna, Krishna is to Bhagawan Swayam. As Bhagavatam says, Krishna is to Bhagawan Swayam. In South India, most popular is Vishnu is considered as the Supreme Bhagawan, right? All the incarnations come through Vishnu in this material world. But in Bhagavatam, it is said, and all the South Indians read Bhagavatam. Why? People think that only Aishwarya that is the position of the Supreme Lord. No. The position of the Supreme Lord is also Aishwarya, very official. Aham taham sarva paap ye bhyo moksha. So, the Lord is there for us to serve Him as Vishnu.

---
#### Relationship, Balarama's Mercy, Sacredness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/OcQ46N3pDaM?t=1230
**Verse References:** BG 2.47
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "To achieve him, to get him, we need the mercy of Balarama."
**Summary:** This segment uses the analogy of knowing a person through different relationships (official, friendship, marriage) to illustrate the depth of knowing the Lord. It concludes by reiterating the importance of Balarama's mercy in attaining Krishna and emphasizing the sacredness of Balarama Purnima.

He is standing in Tirupati waiting for all of us, accepting our services. That's official. If you want to know a person, is official identity enough? No. That is only one part of that of friendship, like Arjuna had. Dasi has one thing and there is friendship. You know the person more than his friend, but you will not know him as much as his wife knows him. So, the parakhya rasa, the madhurya rasa, that is the parakhya rasa. It is only one part of the parakhya. This is the Parakhya rasa. The parakhya is the true one of the parakhya. So, now it is to achieve him, to get him, we need the mercy of Balarama. So Balarama, Purnima, the appearance of Balarama is very, very, very sacred, very, very important, even though public do not recognize, they recognize Janmashtami. But Baladeva, Balarama, Purnima is one of the very important functions. So we are here today celebrating that. May the Lord bless all of us to get his mercy, to go back to Supreme Lord Krishna.

### Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu’s Pastimes in Puri | Sri Jai Chaitanya Dasa | Gaura Mandala Parikrama 2024
**Speaker:** HG Jai Chaitanya Dasa

#### devotion, surrender, divine plan
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KLChvudsdSM?t=22
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu made his residence in Jagannath Puri."
**Summary:** This segment narrates how Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, after taking Sanyas and desiring to go to Vrindavan, was guided by Nithyananda Prabhu and Advaita Acharya to Shantipur, where he met his mother, Sachi Mata, who requested him to reside in Jagannath Puri.

Chaitanya Mahaprabhu came from Navadip to Puri. He came to Puri because his mother requested him not to go to Vrindavan. After taking Sanyas, he wanted to go to Vrindavan. So, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu wanted to go to Vrindavan after Sanyas, but then Nithyananda Prabhu tricked him. Instead of going to Vrindavan, he showed the way towards Shantipur, which is Advaita Acharya's house. So, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu was in ecstasy. He has no idea where he is going. He wanted to go to Vrindavan, but Nithyananda Prabhu brought him to Shantipur, Advaita Acharya's house. And Advaita Acharya called Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's mother, Sachi Mata, because now he has become a mendicant, he has become a Sanyasi. He will go away to Vrindavan or other places. Mother will not be able to see Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. Already the mother is in great pain. So, Advaita Acharya brought Sachi Mata to Shantipur so Chaitanya Mahaprabhu could meet her. So, one request Sachi Mata had was that he doesn't go to Vrindavan. He make his headquarters in Jagannath Puri, because in Jagannath Puri and Calcutta, always devotees come and go. So, she said I will get some information about you. I will hear about you from people who come from Jagannath Puri. So, please make your residence in Jagannath Puri. So, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu made his residence in Jagannath Puri. That is how he came from Navadip to Jagannath Puri and made this his headquarters.

#### renunciation, divine residence, separation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KLChvudsdSM?t=131

**Summary:** This segment describes Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's travels around India and his permanent residence in Jagannath Puri for 18 years, including 12 years spent in Gambhira, a secluded place for deep contemplation and experiencing separation from Krishna.

Of course, he went around India twice. He went to Vrindavan also, but Jagannath Puri was his permanent place for 18 years, out of which 12 years he was in Gambhira. We visited Gambhira before going to Jagannath Temple, a small house. Gambhira means deep inside the house. The most innermost portion of the house is called Gambhira, and that is where all the treasure is kept. In any house, they don't keep your treasure in the outer rooms. The most inner room, that is where treasure is kept. So, the treasure called Chaitanya was also residing. So, that's why it is called Gambhira. He resided there for 12 years totally immersed in separation with Krishna.

So, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu when he came to Puri, he in ecstasy, he knew where Tota Gopinath Deity was hidden or submerged. They say most of Puri was, it was a hill, Nilachala Parvata. Where is the Parvata now? So, there is no more Parvata. They say this whole place has been flattened by the sand of the beach before it was a hill. So, probably the Deity was submerged. Chaitanya Mahaprabhu knew where this was hidden. He knew where this Deity of Tota Gopinath was and he dug with his hand. So, he himself lifted this Deity from earth, Tota Gopinath, installed there and every day he would visit. So, we visited Gambhira. That is the place Chaitanya Mahaprabhu stayed last 12 years.

But top portion all the green leaves are still there. And then Tota Gopinath. So, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu wound up his pastime after 48 years, 24 years as Grahastha and 24 years as Sanyasi. He wound up his pastime at Tota Gopinath by merging into the Deity of Tota Gopinath. After that Gadadhar Pandita made his house in Tota Gopinath. And Gadadhar Prabhu, he merged into the Deity of Radharani in Tota Gopinath. So, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and Gadadhar both merged into the temple of Tota Gopinath to Deity of Tota Gopinath and Radharani. So, very important place for Gaudiya Vaishnava.

Sri Ramachandra Kaviraj is an eternal associate of Lord Chaitanya and served as Karna Manjiri in Krishna's eternal pastime. Ramachandra Kaviraj went on to become great friends with another stalwart Vaishnava, Sri Narottam Das Thakur, and they relished each other's association. Hence, in his song Gauranga Dutipada, Narottam Das explains, 'Ramachandra sangha mage Narottam Das.' Ramachandra actively participated in the festivals of Keturi, which took place at Narottam Das Sri Par.

#### meditation, pastimes, spiritual vision
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xFSqT_lWKR4?t=71
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees
**Key Quote:** "Srinivasa Charya who was the incarnation of Mani Manjari could see that Radhanani was so absorbed in her play that she did not notice her nose ring falling off her nose into the waters."
**Summary:** This segment narrates a pastime of Srinivasa Acharya in deep meditation, where he witnesses Radha and Krishna's eternal pastimes in Yamuna and observes Radharani losing her nose ring.

There is a very nice pastime of Ramachandra Kaviraj and Srinivasa Acharya. Basically, this is Srinivasa Acharya's. Of course, Ramachandra Kaviraj is Srinivasa Acharya's disciple. Once while residing at Vishnupur, Sri Srinivasa Acharya became suddenly engrossed in deep meditation. He became absorbed watching and relishing the eternal pastimes of Sri Radha and Krishna in Yamuna. This is one of the characteristics of exalted devotees. They are qualified to relish the eternal pastimes of Sri Radha and Krishna in Yamuna. Srinivasa Charya, who was the incarnation of Mani Manjari, could see that Radhanani was so absorbed in her play that she did not notice her nose ring falling off her nose into the waters. Sri Rupa Manjari informed Gunam Manjari of the nose ring's loss, who in turn asked Mani Manjari to find it in the Yamuna. All the other gopis returned home. And who is Mani Manjari? Srinivasa Acharya.

---
#### devotion, spiritual power, guru-disciple relationship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xFSqT_lWKR4?t=154
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees
**Key Quote:** "Ramachandra Kaviraj, who was an incarnation of Karuna Manjari, could see his worshipable Gurudeva was with him."
**Summary:** This segment describes how Ramachandra Kaviraj, as a disciple of Srinivasa Acharya, enters into meditation to help his guru and retrieves Radharani's nose ring from the Yamuna in the spiritual realm.

So Mani Manjari, Srinivasa Acharya, began looking for the ring, but she could not find it. Meanwhile, when Srinivasa's wife noticed that their husband was absorbed in meditation for an unusually long period of time, they became very anxious and alarmed. This is in meditation. The whole day passed and yet he did not come out of his trance. The news spread far and wide and even the king of Vishnupur, who was a disciple of Srinivasa, arrived at the scene. When they saw that Srinivasa's meditation was unbroken, even at noon the next day, they called for Ramachandra Kaviraj, who was then the disciple of Srinivasa. He was riding at Telliya Budhari, near Bhagavangola. Ramachandra arrived and examined his Gurudeva and assured all that he would soon regain external consciousness. Saying this, he sat down and entered into a deep meditation. Ramachandra Kaviraj, who was an incarnation of Karuna Manjari, could see his worshipable Gurudeva was with him. Within the waters of Yamuna, in the spiritual Brindavan, Karuna Manjari entered into the waters as well and found Radhika's nose ring from under the leaf inside the water. Finding the nose ring, the two little girls went and handed it over to Guna Manjari, who in turn handed it to Rupa Manjari, who placed it upon Radhika's nose. All present began glorifying Radhika's astounding beauty. Guna Manjari was very pleased and Ramachandra Kaviraj handed over her chewed betel nut remnants as his reward.

---
#### spiritual reality, transcendental experience, Vibhuti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xFSqT_lWKR4?t=275
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So these personalities can, while being in this material world, be in the spiritual world also."
**Summary:** This segment explains how exalted devotees like Ramachandra Kaviraj and Srinivasa Acharya can simultaneously exist in both the material and spiritual worlds, participating in the Lord's pastimes.

After relishing this wonderful sight, Ramachandra and Srinivasa's meditation broke and they regained their external consciousness. Srinivasa embraced Ramachandra Kaviraj, who offered his respectful obeisances onto the lotus feet. Ramachandra still had with him the betel nut remnants of Radharani in his hands, and he thereafter distributed the Mahaprasadam onto all present. Being relieved, all the devotees glorified the two of them and blissfully chanted the Lord's holy name. So these personalities are called Vibhuti Patis. They are Lords in both the Vibhuti Patis. In both the Vibhuti of the Lord. The material world is one Vibhuti of the Lord, a small portion. The spiritual world is the main Vibhuti of the Lord. So these personalities can, while being in this material world, be in the spiritual world also. Take part in the Leelas in the spiritual world also. So there is Ramachandra Kaviraj, the disciple of Srinivasa Acharya, who helped Srinivasa Acharya in his transcendental meditation in the spiritual world, while being in the material world.

---
#### Guru, initiation, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XQGJ1TDYew8?t=65
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The whole story reveals how one can find one's guru through sincere prayers."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates the story of Hrishikananda Prabhu meeting his guru, Shamananda Prabhu, highlighting the importance of sincere prayers and the divine guidance in finding a spiritual master.

So we will just read, I have one or two paragraphs about Hrishikananda. So Hrishikananda Prabhu, the disciple of Shamananda Prabhu. Sri Hrishikananda Prabhu was born in 1590 in the village of Rayani, in the Medinipura district. His father's name was Raja Achuthananda, a rich zamindar, and his mother, Bhavani Devi. Medinapur district used to be part of Orissa kingdom. Hrishikananda was also known by the name Hrishikamurari. Hrishikamurari meets Shamananda Prabhu. One day, Hrishikamurari was meditating in a solitary place. He was anxious to find a spiritual master who could give him spiritual direction. He had entered into a very deep meditation when he heard a disembodied voice say, 'Murari, you need not be anxious any longer. Your guru is Shamananda Prabhu and you will meet him here very shortly. Take shelter of him and your life will be successful.' Upon hearing this divine message, Murari began chanting the name of Shamananda on his beads with joyful enthusiasm. He spent the entire night crying out of his desire to meet his guru. At dawn, Hrishikamurari was on the lookout for his guru when he saw the tall figure of Shamananda approaching him, as effulgent as the sun, surrounded by his disciples. He started dancing and chanting the name of Lord Nityananda. Having waited anxiously for so long to encounter his guru, Hrishikamurari immediately fell at his feet. Shamananda affectionately lifted him up and embraced him and later initiated him with the Radha Krishna mantra. The whole story reveals how one can find one's guru through sincere prayers.

---
#### Guru, preaching, spiritual power
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XQGJ1TDYew8?t=193
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "With the spiritual powers invested in him by his guru, Hrishikamurari was able to convert many criminals, atheists, Muslims and other fallen spirit souls to the path of devotion, bestowing the jewel of Prema upon them all."
**Summary:** The speaker describes Hrishikananda's devotion to his guru and his powerful preaching, highlighting his ability to convert even the most fallen individuals to the path of devotion through his spiritual power.

Hrishikamurari's Guru. Shamananda was a great devotee of the Guru, fully committing himself in body, mind and soul. So much so that in a short time he was recognized as Shamananda's chief disciple and was a very powerful preacher. With the spiritual powers invested in him by his guru, Hrishikamurari was able to convert many criminals, atheists, Muslims and other fallen spirit souls to the path of devotion, bestowing the jewel of Prema upon them all. On one occasion, a wicked Muslim tried to silence Hrishikamurari by having him attacked by an intoxicated elephant. But Hrishikamurari was able to transform even the elephant into a disciple and engage him in the service of Vishnu and the Vaishnavas.

---
#### legacy, disappearance, temple
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XQGJ1TDYew8?t=247
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Suddenly Hrishikananda Prabhu disappeared and merged into the Gopinath deity."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes the story of Hrishikananda Prabhu, mentioning the handing over of his personal deity, his literary works, his disappearance into the Gopinath deity, and a mention of the death of Shamananda Prabhu.

All who witnessed this amazing event were overwhelmed with astonishment at Hrishikamurari's spiritual power. Later, Shri Shamananda Pandit handed over his personal go in the temple. His personal go in the deity to Hrishikananda as it is still being worshipped at Gopi Vallapura. The Raja of Mayur Banj, Shri Vaidyanath Banj from Orissa was also attracted to the transcendental power of Hrishikananda and became his disciple. Hrishikananda Prabhu wrote several works including Shamananda Sataka, Bhakta, Bhagavata, and Kunjakeli Dvadasakha. It is said that in 1652, in the month of Magh, when Hrishikananda Prabhu was in Dramuna, while doing powerful Kretan in the courtyard of Shirachora Gopinath temple, suddenly Hrishikananda Prabhu disappeared and merged into the Gopinath deity. It is said that in 1652, when Hrishikananda Prabhu was in Dramuna, Shamananda Prabhu, Shrinitha Prabhu, and Shrila Prabhupada were all killed. The story of the death of Shri Shamananda Prabhu is told in the Gopinath temple.

Sri Raghava Bhavan, there is Raghava Pandit Samadhi here, Raghava Pandit's worshipable Sri Radha Madana Mohan Deities, Sri Raghunath Das Goswami's worshipable Sri Sri Radha Raman Deity, these are in this ghat. We know the story of Raghunath Das Goswami and Nityananda Prabhu which happened here and the subsequent Panihatti festival. Raghava Bhavan, the residence of Sri Raghava Pandita and his sister Damayanti is renowned all over the three worlds.

---
#### Raghava Bhavan, Lord Chaitanya, Raghavera Jali
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/4-fRAHSXhCc?t=50
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "These bags of delicious food items came to be subsequently known as Raghavera Jali, the bags of Raghavendra Pandita."
**Summary:** This segment describes Raghava Bhavan as the eternal residence of Sri Gaurahari, detailing how Lord Chaitanya accepted prasad from Raghava Pandita and how Damayanti Devi would cook and pack food for Mahaprabhu, which became known as Raghavera Jali.

As the eternal residence of Sri Gaurahari, Lord Gaurah Nityananda have manifested their innumerable heart-rendering pastimes over here at Raghava Bhavan 500 years ago, located on the bank of Ganges at Panihatti. Lord Chaitanya would always be immersed in the mood of Srimati Radharani and would lovingly accept the prasad brought to him by Raghava Pandita. In Chaitanya Charitamrita, it has been revealed that Sri Raghava Pandita was an intimate associate of Lord Chaitanya. After accepting his sannyas, Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu moved to reside at Jagannath Puri. Every year during the Rathayatra festival, almost all the devotees from Bengal would travel to Puri to unite with Lord Gaurasundara once again. Sri Mati Damayanti Devi would cook innumerable items for Mahaprabhu with her love and affection and devotion and thereafter pack these in separate bags. Raghava Pandita would then carry these bags to Puri during his annual visit and offer them to Lord Chaitanya. These bags of delicious food items came to be subsequently known as Raghavera Jali, the bags of Raghavendra Pandita. And they find a very special mention in Chaitanya Bhagavata and Sri Chaitanya Charitamrita.

---
#### Raghavera Jali, Devotion, Nityananda Prabhu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/4-fRAHSXhCc?t=158
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

Sri Krishnadas Kaviraj conceded in Chaitanya Charitamrita that it was impossible in one's lifetime to list down all the wonderful eatables contained in Chaitanya Chaitanya Bhagavata's food items. It was impossible in one's lifetime to list down all the wonderful eatables contained in Chaitanya Charitamrita. It was impossible in one's lifetime to list down all the wonderful eatables contained in Raghavera Jali. It was the love and devotion of both brother and sister that made these foodstuffs even more tasty. Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu would honor and relish them for a continuous one year until Raghava Pandita brought fresh food items. The ecstatic pastimes at the following four places, the house of Mother Sachi, that is Satchira Mandira, wherever Nithyananda Prabhu dances, that is Nithyananda Narthena, then during Sankirtana at the house of Shiva's Pandit, Shiva's Angan and the residence of Raghava Pandit, Raghava Bhavan. Whenever Nithyananda Prabhu and his associate used to return to Bengal from Puri, they used to stay at Raghava Bhavan. Nithyananda Prabhu has manifested many awe-inspiring pastimes here.

#### Panihatti, Raghunath Das Goswami, Nityananda Prabhu's Mercy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/4-fRAHSXhCc?t=240

It was here at Panihatti on the bank of Ganges that Raghunath Das Goswami, the son of the earth's white wealthy landlord of Saptagram, Sri Govardhan Majumdar, had received the costless mercy of Sri Nithyananda Prabhu. It was Nithyananda Prabhu's mercy that enabled Sri Raghunatha to later obtain the shelter and service at the feet of Lord Chaitanya. Raghunatha went on to become one of the six Goswamis of Vrindavana and is renowned all over the three worlds for his renunciation and love for the Supreme. So that is Panihatti. So we know about the Panihatti festival. Nithyananda Prabhu was there and Raghunatha Goswami, Raghunatha was ordered to feed all the brahmanas, all the devotees and every year at Panihatti this festival is commemorated.

### The Glories of Sri Advaita Acharya | Sri Jai Chaitanya Dasa | Sri Gaura Mandala Parikrama 2024
**Speaker:** HG Jai Chaitanya Dasa

#### Advaita Acharya, lineage, divine appearance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EAQRc5pezvo?t=114
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A few days later, the potency of Shri Advaita entered the womb of Mother Navaha."
**Summary:** This segment describes the birth story of Advaita Acharya, including his parents' devotion, a divine prophecy, and his conception.

Kubera Pandita was such that he cared for nothing apart from the lotus feet of Shree Krishna. Nabha Devi was Kubera Pandita's wife. She is our most worshipable as she is the mother of Advaita Acharya. He bore Shri Advaita Acharya, the combined incarnation of Sadashiva and Mahavishnu, as his son. Kubera Pandita and Navahadevi had six sons and a daughter, but they passed away soon after they were born. Feeling dejected, they visited Shantipur in West Bengal, where they bathed in the holy Ganges, distributed wealth and charity, and engaged in reciting Bhagavad Gita and Srimad Bhagavatam. They heard a divine voice echoing in the sky, which assured Kubera Pandita and Navahadevi that Mahavishnu would soon appear in their house. A few days later, the potency of Shri Advaita entered the womb of Mother Navaha. All of these pastimes took place while Shri Kubera and his wife were still in Shantipur. Shortly thereafter, Kubera Pandita and Navahadevi returned to the temple, and they were brought to the temple of Mother Navaha. In due course of time, Shri Advaita Acharya appeared in the womb of Navahadevi.

---
#### miracle, devotion, holy rivers
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EAQRc5pezvo?t=224
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Shri Advaita assured his mother and vowed to summon all the holy rivers to Lauda, so that she does not have to travel to bathe in the Ganges."
**Summary:** This segment recounts the miracle of Advaita Acharya bringing the holy rivers to Lauda for his mother, Navahadevi, demonstrating his divine power and devotion.

Waves of divine ecstasy flooded the people of Navahadevi. On the advice of learned astrologers, Shri Kubera Pandita and Navahadevi named their son Kamalaksha. Navahadevi once desired to take a dip in the holy Ganges. She saw a dream one night in which her son Kamalaksha revealed himself as four-handed Lord Vishnu. Beholding the Supreme Lord before her, she immediately offered her prayers in glorification. Shri Advaita assured his mother and vowed to summon all the holy rivers to Lauda, so that she does not have to travel to bathe in the Ganges. Upon waking up, Navahadevi seemed quite confused. Although she was initially reluctant to disclose her dream, Navahadevi finally revealed it to her son on his insistence. A seven-year-old Advaita then shone again and he would bring all the holy Tirthas to Lauda to fulfill the desires of his dear mother. Thereafter, during the night, Shri Advaita entered into a deep meditation and summoned the holy rivers. He then ordered them to reside at Lauda during the Madhu Krishna Trayodashi Tithi so that his mother can bathe in them. Accordingly, Ganga, Jamuna, Narmada, Kaveri, Saraswati, Udavari, etc. reached and gathered near the mountains of Lauda. The next morning, Advaita Acharya took his mother so that she could experience the wonder with her own eyes. As soon as Advaita chanted the names of the Supreme Lord and blew upon his conch shell, the holy rivers started pouring down from the mountains. Some of them were reddish, some were bluish, some were white, while some were yellow. Shri Kamalaksha introduced each of the rivers to his mother. This exalted dham came to be subsequently known as Paana Tirtha, as it was Advaita Acharya's vow, Paana in Bengali means vow, that had caused the holy river to descend to this place. This newly created water body came to be known as Jadukatha river as it was shaped by Advaita's magic. This is coming from the mountains of Meghalaya which is this way about 2 to 3 kilometers.

---
#### purification, divine identity, devotion to Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EAQRc5pezvo?t=375
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Goddess Kali could not tolerate receiving obeisances from Shri Advaita, the combined incarnation of Mahavishnu and Sadashiva, her most worshipable lord."
**Summary:** This segment describes Nabha Devi bathing in the holy waters, the significance of Paana Tirtha, and an incident where Advaita Acharya's refusal to bow to Kali reveals his divine identity.

You can see all the mountains, that's Indian state of Meghalaya. And this river comes from those mountains. It is also called Surmi river. Nabha Devi then bathed in these holy waters purifying her body, mind and soul. Beholding these wondrous feet, the demigods showered flowers from the sky. Even today, thousands and lakhs of pilgrims gather at Paana Tirtha during the Madhu Krishna Trayodashi Tithi to bathe and purify their consciousness. It is believed that anyone who bathes in this river on this auspicious occasion is relieved of his sins and obtains the pure love of God. On the bank of this Jadukatha river stands a beautiful temple renowned as Advaita Mahari. The occasion of Kali Puja was celebrated with great pomp and show at Lauda. On one such year, King Divya Raya had made all the necessary arrangements. The Pandits and Ramanas and all the residents of Nabha Gram were invited to the royal palace to attend this grand festival. Shri Advaita had also come to attend the occasion along with Kubera Pandita. When the king noticed that Shri Advaita did not pay obeisances unto the deity of Goddess Kali, he requested him to do the same. Shri Advaita however refused, reasoning that any person who worships other demigods or demigoddesses instead of worshipping the Supreme Lord Shri Krishna is cast into bewilderment and material misery. Hearing his son's statement, Shri Kubera Pandita countered Advaita and urged him to pay his obeisances unto Maa Kali to not disrespect her. Shri Advaita consented to fulfill his father's desire. But as soon as Advaita Acharya entered the temple and paid his prostrated obeisances, the deity of Kali broke into pieces. The king came to tears witnessing the broken deity. Goddess Kali could not tolerate receiving obeisances from Shri Advaita, the combined incarnation of Mahavishnu and Sadashiva, her most worshipable lord. Shri Advaita did not wish to reveal his divine identity to anyone just yet. Hence, after this pastime, he moved over to Shantipur where he occupied himself with studying devotional scriptures and propagating the glories of Shri Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

From the tender age of 14, he began worshipping the Supreme Lord on the bank of the Ganges at Shantipur, calling for the Lord to descend and deliver the word. However, every year he visited Panatirtha on the Madhukrishna Dayavadashi Tithi. We also sing this song every day: Dayakaro Sitaapati, Dvaita Gosai, Bhava Kripa Avalepai Chetan Nenithaya. We worship the Lord as feet of our spiritual master. So we have here the river which we will be crossing to go to his temple. This is the temple, Advaita's birthplace. Crossing the river, you also have Madhavendra Puri's birthplace. It is called Brahman Gaon, which practically does not exist. Some dilapidated buildings are there, we may not go there, it is quite interior.

#### Lineage, Birth, Early Life, Dukkhi
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YuoTYPFkCV8?t=175
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Shri Shamananda was known in this world as Hridayaananda's dear disciple."
**Summary:** This segment describes Shamananda Prabhu's lineage, birth details, and the reason for his initial name, Dukhi, due to the loss of previous children.

Shri Shamananda was known in this world as Hridayaananda's dear disciple. He was the grand disciple of Shubhala Saka, the most dear friend of the Supreme Lord. He was the crest jewel of the enjoyers of sacred rapture. May he appear day and night in my mind bringing an appreciation for the essence of the joys Beloved of Shri Radha, Shamananda Prabhu was born on the full moon day of Chaitra in 1534 in the town of Dharendra, Bahudarpura, which is near the Kargapura railway station in Medinipura. His father was Shri Krishna Mandala and his mother Durika. Prior to Shamananda's birth, his parents had lost several children during childbirth and they vowed to surrender their next child to Vishnu if he survived. Having suffered so much grief in the loss of their previous children, they first named Shamananda as Dukhi or Unhappy to ward off further distress.

---
#### Devotion, Diksha, Krishna Das
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YuoTYPFkCV8?t=253
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Dukhi told his parents that he wished to take Diksha from Hrudaya Chaitanya, and his parents happily permitted him."
**Summary:** This segment recounts Shamananda's early devotion, his desire to take Diksha from Hrudaya Chaitanya, and his initiation as Krishna Das.

For Shamananda's parents, Krishna and Krishna's devotees were very dear and everything to them. Shamananda used to carefully listen to the glories of Gauranga and Nityananda from devotees. When listening to the activities of Gauranithai or those of Radha and Krishna, tears would flow in waves from his eyes. Dukhi told his parents that he wished to take Diksha from Hrudaya Chaitanya, who was the disciple of Gauridas Pandit, and his parents happily permitted him. When Dukhi arrived in Amitabha, Hrudaya Chaitanya happily gave him Krishna Mantra and named him Krishna Das. From then on, Dukhi was known as Dukhi Krishna Das.

#### Vrindavan, Jiva Goswami, Shabana, Preaching Mission
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YuoTYPFkCV8?t=303
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In Vrindavan, Dukhi Krishna Das studied the Vaishnava scriptures under Shri Jivagoswami, who was the leading scholar of the Sampradaya."
**Summary:** This segment describes Shamananda's time in Vrindavan, his studies under Jiva Goswami, his new name Shabana, and his assignment to return to Bengal to spread Mahaprabhu's teachings.

Hrudaya Chaitanya ordered him to go to Vrindavan and engage in Vajana. In Vrindavan, Dukhi Krishna Das studied the Vaishnava scriptures under Shri Jivagoswami, who was the leading scholar of the Sampradaya. While in Vrindavan, he was given the name Shabana. Shabana was known as Shamananda because he brought great joy to Shamsundar. When Jiva saw his charming activities, he kept him nearby and instructed him in the Vaishnava scriptures. This is as per Bhakti Ratnakara. In 1532, Shri Jivagoswami sent Srinivasa Acharya, Narottam Das Thakur, and Shamananda back to Bengal with the Vaishnava literature, to spread the teachings of Mahaprabhu.

---
#### Preaching, Radharani's Mercy, Nupura Tilaka
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YuoTYPFkCV8?t=351
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Suddenly, by Radharani's transcendental mercy, he found her ankle bracelet torn."
**Summary:** This segment details Shamananda's preaching areas, Radharani's special mercy upon him, and the origin of the Nupura Tilaka.

Throughout Bengal and Orissa, Narottama went to northern Bengal and Shamananda went to Orissa. Radharani's special mercy on Shamananda. Even though Shamananda Prabhu was Hrudaya Chaitanya's initiated disciple, his guru had entrusted him to the care of Jivagoswami Prabhu. Through the association of Jiva and service to him, Shamananda developed a taste for serving Radha and Krishna. In the conjugal mood, an extraordinary incident which took place in Vrindavan prior to his being ordered by Jiva to return to Orissa, demonstrates how dear Shamananda was to Radharani. One day, Shamananda Prabhu was sweeping the Rasa Mandala in Vrindavan, absorbed in an ecstatic trance. Suddenly, by Radharani's transcendental mercy, he found her ankle bracelet torn. He saw the ankle bracelet lying on the ground. In his excitement, he touched the ankle bracelet to his forehead, where it left a mark preserved to this day as the Tilak marking of the disciple's descendant of Shamananda. It is known as Nupura Tilaka. You must have noticed the Tilaka that Babaji is wearing is the shape of the footprint of Radharani or Nupura. There are two stories, one says it is Radharani's seat, one says it is Nupura. So it is called Nupura Tilaka.

---
#### Samanandis, Preaching, Kirtan, Disciples, Resika Murari
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YuoTYPFkCV8?t=446
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Narottama Das Thakur and Shamananda primarily preached the message of Mahaprabhu through Kirtan."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the Samanandis, Shamananda's preaching style through Kirtan, his prominent disciples including Resika Murari, and his continued preaching in Orissa.

So they are called the Samanandis today. They have that parampara and they have that paramparic Tilaka. Shamananda Prabhu's preaching Narottama Das Thakur and Shamananda primarily preached the message of Mahaprabhu through Kirtan. Srinivas sang Kirtan in a style called Manoharasahi. Manoharasahi in Karanahati and Shamananda in Renithi, these are the three styles of Kirtan, which was popularized by these three personalities. He would enchant the listeners with his heartfelt singing of Kirtan. As a result of his preaching in Orissa, many Muslims also became Shamananda's disciples. The most important of his innumerable disciples was Resika Murari. Resikananda was the son of Achutananda, the Jamindar of Rohini village. He had another name Murari and was thus most commonly known as Resika Murari. He was a very powerful preacher and his fame is still widespread through the village of Orissa. A list of some of Shamananda's prominent disciples and related pastimes is given in Bhakti Ratnakara. Shamananda Prabhu lived the last part of his life in Narasinghapura in Orissa, where he continued preaching Vaishnavism.

### The Glories of Srila Narottama Dasa Thakura | Sri Jai Chaitanya Dasa | Sri Gaura Mandala Parikrama
**Speaker:** HG Jai Chaitanya Dasa

#### Narottama Das Thakura, Keturi, Temple History
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/MXeWQsRmnqs?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The first temple, in the sense, an organized form of preaching took place from here."
**Summary:** This segment describes the birthplace of Narottama Das Thakura, the temple he built, and its subsequent destruction in 1971.

So, this is the place of Narottam Das Chako's birth site. He was born in a very, very kingly home. Fathers, all many kings of this place. Huge houses, you can just imagine this place, how it could have been. And the temple was made by Narottam Das Chako. The first temple, in the sense, an organized form of preaching took place from here. After Srinivas Acharya, Shamananda Pandit and Narottam Das Chako came back from Vrindavan, they all came, went to different places as we read the other day. Shamananda Pandit went to Odisha, Srinivas Acharya to Bengal and Narottam Das Chako came to this part of Bengal. And he built the first temple, his family, rich family, they built the first temple. And he inaugurated the first temple with Gornithai Deity. And the first Gopunima was conducted here. Thank you. Called the K2D festival, the first festival, which was attended by Janama Mata, wife of Nithyananda Prabhu, Srinivas Acharya, all the Vaishnumas of that time congregated here in this part. That temple was existing here, but recently as 1971, everything was demolished here. The temple is gone, Deity is gone, everything is gone. But they could retrieve some Shaligrams, some Vigraha, and they again rebuild what we see here. So unfortunately, in 1971, everything was destroyed. But the place is still here. That is more important. We get the vibration of this place. So Narottama. Narottama.

#### Predictions, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, Narottama's Appearance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/MXeWQsRmnqs?t=135
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It seems that this Narottama is extremely devious. Perhaps he is about to make his appearance."
**Summary:** This segment narrates the story of how Chaitanya Mahaprabhu predicted the arrival of Narottama Das Thakura and the reactions of the devotees.

Even before the birth of Narottam Das Chako, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu predicted his arrival. And while he was in Ramkeli, while coming back from Kanena Shala, he was walking the other side of the river. And he looked at, actually Ramkeli is just the other side. We went about 100 kilometers, came about. But it's the other side. So Chaitanya Mahaprabhu was looking at this direction and went on calling the name Narottama, Narottama, Narottama. So all the Vaishnumas standing there could know that some great personality is going to come. And Chaitanya Mahaprabhu was looking at this direction. Same thing happened in Orissa when he was calling Srinivasa's name before the arrival of Srinivasa Thakur. So in your small book there is a prayer to Narottama Das Thakur. We can chant that first. Shri Krishna Nama Amrita Varshi Vaktra Chandraprabha Dvashtha Tamo Bharaya Gauranga Deva Aucharaya Tasmai Gauranga Deva Aucharaya Tasmai Gauranga Deva Aucharaya Tasmai Gauranga Deva Aucharaya Tasmai Gauranga Deva Aucharaya Tasmai Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu stayed at Ramkeli for a few days, constantly surrounded by an ocean of ecstatic devotees who were eager to bathe in the purifying waters of the Lord's transcendental association. Not only was the general mass of people affected by the Lord, but even the Mohammedan king had a change of heart due to Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's presence. One day, as Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu was dancing with his associates during Sankirtana, he suddenly looked in the direction of Shri Keturi village. A peculiar look appeared on his face. Tears came to his eyes and he cried out Narottama, Narottama again and again. The ocean of mercy, Nithyananda Raya, shouted with joy and Haridas, Vakreshwara and others were inexplicably filled with unbounded happiness. Witnessing this unusual behavior of the Lord, the devotees began talking amongst themselves as follows. Prabhu is repeatedly calling out the name of someone called Narottama. It seems that this Narottama is extremely devious. Perhaps he is about to make his appearance. Oh, who is that fortunate man who will have such a son! The woman who bears him within her womb certainly is the most fortunate lady in this world. Obviously, the Lord has some plan for this Narottama to fulfill his missions. In this way, rumors of Narottama's impending appearance spread far and wide. Oh, who is this Narottama? The wheel of time turned until finally during the sixth hour of the full moon day in the month of Magha,

---
#### Birth of Narottama, Divine Signs, Vishnu Prasad
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/MXeWQsRmnqs?t=359
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Don't worry, this boy will not accept food unless it was first offered to Lord Vishnu."
**Summary:** This segment describes the auspicious birth of Narottama Das Thakura, the divine signs surrounding his birth, and the incident where he refused to eat until the food was offered to Lord Vishnu.

when all auspicious signs became visible, Narottamadas was born to fulfill the mission of the Lord. His mother, radiant with beauty, wept joyfully as she lovingly gazed at the charming face of her son. A divine effulgence illuminated the delivery room and happiness swelled within the hearts of everyone present. The delighted Dattamahasaya warmly welcomed everyone without taking his eyes off the face of his son for a second. Krishnananda's exalted father profusely distributed charity to the singers, musicians and other present for the protection of his grandson. So his father is Dattamahasaya, grandfather is Krishnananda. There was no woman in the world as fortunate as Narottamada's mother. Narayana. By the grace of Lord, she was granted the divine vision to see Gauranga, Nityananda and Advaita dancing ecstatically in her house. Narayana jubilantly watched as his son continued to grow like the waxing moon. Similarly, Krishnananda's pleasure grew no bounds as he constantly stared at the jewel-like face of his son. Being grateful because of his great fortune, he fed the local brahmanas. Daily, on an auspicious day, Krishnananda arranged for the ceremony of offering rice to his son. The astrologer present on that joyful occasion noted all the auspicious signs present in the child's body. He predicted that the boy would become an illustrious person, saying, This baby will grow up to be the greatest of men. Therefore, he should be called Narottama. The festivities went on happily until Krishnananda tried to feed his son some rice. To everyone's dismay, the child refused to eat. Although they coaxed him again and again, the boy repeatedly turned his head away, refusing to eat. The festive atmosphere was thus replaced with anxiety as everyone nervously looked at one another, not knowing what to do. The thoughtful astrologer then replied, Don't worry, this boy will not accept food unless it was first offered to Lord Vishnu. The rice was then offered to Lord Vishnu and once again given to the child. To everyone's relief, the baby happily devoured it. From that day onwards, realizing the value of prasad, Krishnananda ordered, Only food that has been offered to Krishna should be given to my son.

---
#### Prema Vilasa, Padmavati River, Nithyananda Prabhu, Prema
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/MXeWQsRmnqs?t=528
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "O Padmavati, take my love and keep it here. When Narottama comes and bathes here, give it to him."
**Summary:** This segment recounts the story from Prema Vilasa about Mahaprabhu's visit to the Padmavati river, his request to the river to hold his love for Narottama, and Narottama receiving that love when he bathed in the river as a young boy.

According to Prema Vilasa, When Mahaprabhu passed through Kanena Shala, he was dancing ecstatically in Kirtan. He began to call out the name Narottama, Narottama. When Nithyananda asked him why he was calling out this name, Mahaprabhu answered, There will be one exalted devotee by the name Narottama, will soon be born here. Mahaprabhu then went on to the Padmavati river on the place known as Kutuppur, where he bathed and sang and danced in ecstasy. He then called out to the river, O Padmavati, take my love and keep it here. When Narottama comes and bathes here, give it to him. The Padmavati inquired, How will I recognize him? Mahaprabhu answered, You will know it is Narottama, for when he enters your water, you will overflow. The place where Mahaprabhu placed Prema for Narottama's sake, was later given the name Premtali. When Narottama was twelve years old, he had a dream in which Nithyananda Prabhu told him to bathe in the Padmavati and take the Prema which had been stored there for him. He went the next day to the Padma and as soon as he put his foot in the water, the river started to overflow. The Padmavati then remembered Mahaprabhu's words and gave Narottama the Prema which she had been safeguarding for him.

---
#### Keturi Festival, Deity Installation, Vaishnava Gathering
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/MXeWQsRmnqs?t=621
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The dust of the feet of such exalted Vaishnavas transformed Keturi into an exalted place of pilgrimage."
**Summary:** This segment describes Narottama Das Thakur's plan to inaugurate his six deities at Keturi, the grand festival organized for the occasion, and the gathering of prominent Vaishnavas from all over India.

After this, Narottama leaves home and goes to Vrindavan. Narottama Das Thakur wished to inaugurate his six deities on the auspicious occasion of Gaurapurnima at his hometown in Keturi. He decided to arrange a grand festival at Keturi and celebrate this occasion with great pomp and show. Narottama Das Thakur's brother, Sri Santosh Dutta, who was also a devout Vaishnava, had inherited the kingdom from his father, Sri Krishnananda. Accordingly, as per the instructions of his brother, Santosh began making all the necessary arrangements. He ordered the construction of temples at Keturi, where these six deities would subsequently be installed. Sri Narottama Das Thakur personally invited Srimati Janavamata, Sri Srinivasa Acharya, Ramachandra Kaviraj, and several other deities. Narottama Das Thakur was the first stalwart Vaishnavas to attend the festival at Keturi. During this grand festival that took place 500 years ago, Gaudiya Vaishnavas from all over India had come to participate. Sri Shamananda Pandita from Narasimha Pura, Srimati Janava Devi from Kardaha, Sri Parameshwari Das Thakura, Srinivasa Acharya along with his disciples, Krishna Das Sarkhel, Madhavacharya, Raghupati Vaidya, Manikandan Ramdas, Murari Chaitanya, Jnana Das, Mahi Das, Sri Shankara, Kamalakara, all these people. King Santoshadatta arranged for innumerable boats to transport devotees to the other side of river Padma. Numerous bullock carts and palanquins were arranged to ensure that the devotees faced no problems reaching Keturi. The whole atmosphere was surcharged with a mood of love and surrenderance. Narasimha Das Thakur, Santoshadatta and Srinivasa Acharya personally welcomed the guests with chandana and garland. Rooms and servants were arranged for each of the guests. The dust of the feet of such exalted Vaishnavas transformed Keturi into an exalted place of pilgrimage. Loud reverberations of Harinam Sankirtan were heard and the sound of hridanga and kartas enraptured the sky. Not only the temple but each and every house of the town was explicitly decorated with banana leaves, auspicious pots, etc. Huge quantity of ghee, milk, vegetables and puja paraphernalia were arranged. Sri Gauranga, Vallabhikanta, Brajamohan, Shri Krishna, Shri Radhakanta, Shri Radharamana. In 1970,

### The Munificent Nature of Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu | ISKCON Bangalore
**Speaker:** HG Jai Chaitanya Dasa

#### mercy, purification, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1zPYtCmsmkk?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Actually, Mahaprabhu bestows His mercy upon the devotees in three ways."
**Summary:** HG Jai Chaitanya Dasa describes the three ways in which Chaitanya Mahaprabhu bestows His mercy: destruction of material miseries, reconciliation of scriptural contradictions, and inspiration for devotional service.

If somebody achieves the mercy of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, how will he receive it? Actually, Mahaprabhu bestows His mercy upon the devotees in three ways. The first way a devotee gets the mercy of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu is very easily, all the material miseries are destroyed. And the person becomes purified, the transcendental ecstasy is awakened in his heart, a bliss is awakened in his heart, which covers up all the material miseries, and also all the material pleasures. The second manifestation of the mercy of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu is, all the contradictions in the scriptures get reconciled. And you are able to understand what is the essence of the scriptures. Krishna is the Supreme Personality of Godhead and firmly gets established in your heart. And not only that, what happens is, the transcendental mellows get revealed to us. The third aspect of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's mercy, He establishes inspiration to do devotional service with ecstasy and the appreciation of the conjugal pastimes of Krishna. This is the way Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's mercy is bestowed.

#### Family life, Nityananda Prabhu, Kardaha, Incarnations
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Uk4_F24Vz70?t=80
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Lord Chaitanya had entrusted the preaching of Bengal under the leadership of merciful Nithai."
**Summary:** The speaker describes how Nityananda Prabhu, on the orders of Lord Chaitanya, returned to Bengal, got married, and settled in Kardaha, begetting a son and a daughter. The speaker also identifies the incarnations of his wives and children.

On the orders of Lord Chaitanya, Nityananda Prabhu had returned to Bengal. He was inspired by the Lord Nityananda Prabhu and instructed to get married and accept grihastha ashram. Lord Chaitanya had entrusted the preaching of Bengal under the leadership of merciful Nithai in order to fulfill the orders of the Lord. He soon got married to the two daughters of Suryadasa Sarkhel, namely Vasudha and Janava, and settled down at Kunjavatika in the town of Kardaha. In the course of time, He begot one son and one daughter here, Shri Bhirachandraprabhu and Srimati Ganga Devi. Shri Gaurav Ganesha Deepika reveals that Srimati Vasudha and Janava were actually the incarnations of the eternal concerts of Lord Balaram, namely Srimati Varuni and Revati Devi respectively. Shri Bhirachandraprabhu was none other than Shirodakshayi Vishnu, who manifests as the super soul in all of our hearts, while Mother Ganges descended as Ganga Devi, the daughter of Lord Nithyananda.

---
#### Pastimes, Nityananda Prabhu, Kardaha, Miracle
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Uk4_F24Vz70?t=161
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "A huge mass of land suddenly appeared from the water exactly at the place where the burning piece of straw had hit it."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates the pastime of how Nityananda Prabhu acquired the land for his residence in Kardaha by miraculously creating land in the Ganges after being rebuked by a local zamindar.

So we saw the house where they were all staying after the lunch today afternoon. According to the hearsay, after his marriage, Lord Nithyananda had requested the local Zavindar over here in Kardaha to kindly allot him a piece of land where he and his family could reside. The Zavindar, being unaware of Lord Nithyananda's divinity, rebuked him for abandoning his sanyasa and accepting a wife later on. The Zavindar, at the time of conversation, was fishing along with his friends. On the way, Lord Nithyananda had requested the local Zavindar to kindly allot him a piece of land where he and his family could reside. Ganges. He sarcastically replied to Nithyananda that if he really needed a piece of land to stay then he would have to create it out of water. Saying this, the zamindar threw a piece of burning straw into the Ganges in order to mark the place on water where the zamindar decided lord Nithyananda to set up his residence, making fun of lord Nithyananda Prabhu in front of his friends. Nithyananda Prabhu who always remained very humble and without any anger did not mind the zamindar's harsh words and assured the zamindar that whatever he had wished though sarcastically would surely come to pass. What happened next stunned all present. A huge mass of land suddenly appeared from the water. A huge mass of land suddenly appeared from the water exactly at the place where the burning piece of straw had hit it. Then this newly created island then got connected with the mainland. This place subsequently came to be known as Kardaha. Kar means straw in Bengali.

#### Pastimes, Nityananda Prabhu, Abhirama Thakur, Bhirachandraprabhu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Uk4_F24Vz70?t=275
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Though Lord Nithya was an expansion of the Supreme Lord, yet he played his part like a normal father and therefore expressed his concern and anguish."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates the pastime of Abhirama Gopala Thakur's arrival in Kardaha and Nityananda Prabhu's concern for his newborn son, Bhirachandraprabhu, due to Abhirama Thakur's powerful nature.

Lord Nithai set up his residence on this piece of land and began residing with his family. His transcendental house is presently renovated all over the three worlds as Kunjavatika. There is a very sweet pastime that has been narrated in Abhirama Leelamrita which talks of Shri Abhirama Gopala Thakura's arrival in Kardaha. Shri Abhirama Gopal Thakur, one of the most prominent of the twelve cowherd boys, Dwaadasha Gopala, had a very powerful personality. He was a great friend of an intimate associate of Shri Nithyananda Prabhu. Shri Nithyananda Prabhu had a very powerful personality. He was a great friend of an intimate associate of Shri Nithyananda Prabhu. Shri Nithyananda Prabhu had a very powerful personality. He was a great friend of an intimate associate of Shri Nithyananda Prabhu. If Abhirama Thakur bowed down to any person who was not the lord or empowered by the lord then that person would die immediately. Similarly if he bowed down before any stone that was not a Saligramshila then that stone would immediately crack. Several deities in Bengal had cracked just because Shri Abhirama had paid them his obeisances. All the previous seven sons of Nithyananda Prabhu had died because Sri Abhirama Thakur had paid them his dhandavats. However, when Sri Bhirachand Prabhu appeared in this world, Lord Nithya was very careful not to invite Sri Abhirama Gopal Thakur to attend his childbirth ceremony. He feared that Abhirama would again bow down and cause harm to the child. Though Lord Nithya was an expansion of the Supreme Lord, yet he played his part like a normal father and therefore expressed his concern and anguish. Nithyananda Prabhu did not want to take any risk. He feared that Abhirama would still arrive uninvited.
