#### background mind, japa, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/txX01AjDsTQ?t=1644
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Background mind should always be in the context of japa."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains that a background mind focused on problems can make chanting mechanical, and emphasizes the importance of keeping the background mind in the context of Japa, such as remembering the purpose or offering feelings of love.

If your background mind is solving some problem and thinking of this problem, feeling some problem, and you are chanting in the foreground mind, then the chanting will become mechanical. So background mind should always be in the context of japa. Whether it is Shraddha, whether it is remembering the purpose of japa, whether you are remembering the golden command, or whether you want to offer feelings of priti for each bead in reciprocation to realizing how much priti he has in coming to me, descending on my tongue, or it can be Aghlada, feeling great elation that God is before me. Feeling so grateful that Lord has come before me from the depth of the heart, cry out to him with gratitude. You have learnt so many things from Prabhupada's books. What is surrender? God has come before you. Practice that now.

Each chanting of the Hare Krishna, you should think you have earned the pleasure of Krishna. Utsaha means how to earn more and more, how to become more and more wealthy, how to become spiritually richer and richer, one more pleasure giving to Nama Prabhu, one more pleasure giving to Nama Prabhu. This bhavana in the background, with this bhavana in the background, you do the tasking. Tasking means building up the number of sevas. You should know that not only you are counting the number of japa, Krishna is also counting your number of japa. As you finish one mala, he is waiting for the next mala. Okay, one he has finished, take one more.

So all these are, these cue cards that have been made are meant for two things. One is for us to read that, other than japa time. Meditate on the glory of the Lord. Every card is a relationship with the holy name. And, these cards are first a thought. Let's say somebody doesn't know who is Krishna. So if you tell him Krishna is supreme personality of God, it's only a thought. He hears many many many times. Just like we have heard so many times. This fact that Krishna is supreme personality of Godhead. So it has become a feeling. When we say Krishna is supreme personality of Godhead, it becomes a feeling. Bhavana. #### feelings, Nama Prabhu, concentration
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/txX01AjDsTQ?t=2111
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Good japa is chanting, hearing, feeling the Nama Prabhu."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains that good Japa involves feeling the relationship with Nama Prabhu, offering feelings to Nama Prabhu, and feeling the feelings that Nama Prabhu has towards us, emphasizing the importance of synchronized foreground and background minds for concentration.

Just like an angry person, behind the anger there is a thought that causes the anger. There is a reason, there is a thought that causes the anger. But when you are angry, you don't remember the thought, you don't think the thought. You feel the thought. So in the same way, good japa is thinking, sorry, chanting, hearing, feeling the Nama Prabhu. Feeling the relationship with Nama Prabhu. Feeling about Nama Prabhu. Feelings offered to Nama Prabhu. Feeling the feelings that Nama Prabhu has towards us. So if these feelings are in the background, you will connect it to Nama. Who is in the foreground? Foreground. This feeling is about whom? Feeling is about the Nama Avatara who is appearing on each bead. It is not that foreground mind is going one direction and background mind is feeling. No. Background and foreground mind works in a synchronized manner. Just like now you are listening to me, your foreground mind, background mind is synchronized. When it is synchronized, that is concentration. Then that is giving full attention. Artificially try to bring attention back, bring attention back, bring attention back. How long you will go? It is very tiring for the mind. So if you do groundwork for it, if you have support of your background mind, chanting becomes beautiful. Chanting will never become mechanical.

That is soulful japa. So we have devised this method of practicing this. For one year, two years you practice, rest of the life you take your first mala, first you lock your attention through anusarana, golden command, feel that I should seva by seva, distinct seva, feel saviour on each bead. All these things, the first mala you, and then you set the, you lock the attention through sankalpa, that I will hear, and vikalpa that I will not, I will not think. So the first mala locks the attention on chanting, hearing, chanting, hearing, chanting, hearing. How it gets locked? The shakti to lock. What is the, what, the mind gets dragged away by so many things. So you have to control the mind to lock. But we only struggle first round, deliberately, with faith that the shakti to control the mind is in the nama. Divya cheshta, what you are doing is a divya cheshta, is a divine effort. So divine effort, you get divya cheshta shakti from nama.

And this can be really experienced. How beautifully the attention gets locked to chanting, hearing, chanting, hearing, chanting, hearing. No, nothing else comes in the mind. If divya cheshta shakti is flowing into our heart, and it flows into our heart, we will feel no struggle, in doing the japa, we will feel that ruchi, that sense of ease with which we chant Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. If you move your finger in some paint, or in honey, like this, you will find lot of resistance, no? You move your finger in the air, no resistance. When you get divya cheshta shakti, your chanting and hearing will be like this, in the air. Smoothly, mind will not disturb. Otherwise mind will drag. So mala one, anusvarna arthavat vidivat. Anusvarna means obedience to golden command. Arthavat means purposeful. Whenever any activity we do with a, we load the purpose in the background, then there will be focus. Uninterrupted means uninterrupted by any other doing. Japa time means japa doing, not any other doing. You can kirtaniya sadahari, you can chant, you are cooking, you chant while driving, all that you can do, extra. But you have to chant sixteen rounds, without doing anything else. Without solving, trying to engage your mind in solving this problem, that problem, thinking about what I should do today, this, that, all these things. That is something else. This exclusively doing for Krishna. So that is a vidhi.

## HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### Every Devotee’s Journey is Unique, Avoid Comparison | Sri Madhu Pandit Dasa | SB 3.22.7 | 31.07.2024
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

#### oral reception, Krishna consciousness, spiritual master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/b4SP84ASur8?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna consciousness, ultimately, is a path of direct reception of the words that emanate from the transcendental platform."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of oral reception from an authorized source for spiritual growth and cleansing the heart, highlighting Krishna consciousness as a path of direct reception from the transcendental platform.

OM NAMO BHAGWATE VASUDEVAYA. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 3, Chapter 22, Text 7. I have fortunately been instructed by you and thus great favor has been bestowed upon me. I thank God that I have listened with open ears to your pure words. Srila Rupa Goswami has given directions in his Bhakti Rasamita Sindhu on how to accept a bona fide spiritual master and how to deal with him. First, the desiring candidate must find a bona fide spiritual master and then he must very eagerly receive instructions from him and execute them. This is reciprocal service. A bona fide spiritual master or saintly person always desires to elevate a common man who comes to him because everyone is under the delusion. Because everyone is under the delusion of Maya and is forgetful of his prime duty, Krishna Consciousness, a saintly person always desires that everyone become a saintly person. It is the function of a saintly person to invoke Krishna Consciousness in every forgetful common man. Manu said that since he was advised and instructed by Kardamma Muni, he was very much favored. He considered himself lucky to receive the message by oral reception. It is especially mentioned here that one should be very inquisitive to hear with open ears from the authorized source of the bona fide spiritual master. How is one to receive? One should receive the transcendental message by oral reception. The word, Karnarandhraja, means through the holes of the ears. The favor of the spiritual master is not received through any other part of the body but the ears. This does not mean, however, that the spiritual master gives a particular type of mantra through the ears in exchange for some dollars and if the man meditates on that, he achieves perfection and becomes God within six months. Such reception through the ears is bogus. The real fact is that the bona fide spiritual master knows the nature of a particular man and what sort of duties he can perform in Krishna Consciousness and he instructs him in that way. He instructs him through the ear not privately but publicly, you are fit for such and such work in Krishna Consciousness, you can act in this way. One person is advised to act in Krishna Consciousness by working in the deity's room. Another is advised to act in Krishna Consciousness by performing editorial work. Another is advised to do preaching work. And another is advised to carry out Krishna Consciousness in the cooking department. There are different departments of activity in Krishna Consciousness and a spiritual master, knowing the particular ability of a particular man, trains him in such a way that by his tendency to act, he becomes perfect. Bhagavad Gita makes it clear that one can attain the highest perfection of spiritual life simply by offering service according to his ability, just as Arjuna served Krishna by his ability in the military art. Arjuna offered his service fully as a military man and he became perfect. Similarly, an artist can attain perfection simply by performing artistic work under the direction of the spiritual master. If a man is a literary man, he can write articles and poetry for the service of the Lord under the direction of the spiritual master. One has to receive the message of the spiritual master in order to be perfect. Spiritual master regarding one's capacity for the spiritual master is expert in giving such instructions. This combination, the instruction of the spiritual master and the faithful execution of the instruction by the disciple makes the entire process perfect. Srila Vishwanath Chakravarti Thakur describes in his explanation of the verse in Bhagavad Gita, that one who wants to be certain to achieve spiritual success must take the instruction from the spiritual master as to what his particular function is. He should faithfully try to execute that particular instruction and should consider that his life and soul. The faithful execution of the instruction by the disciple is the best way to achieve spiritual success. The faithful execution of the instruction which he receives from the spiritual master is the only duty of a disciple and that will bring him perfection. One should be very careful to receive the message from the spiritual master through the ears and execute it faithfully. That will make one's life successful. That ends the bhaktivedanta. So the manu is expressing his gratitude. I thank God that I have listened with open ears to your pure words. And he rejoicing in his good fortune. The favor he received by listening to instructions from Haridavamuni. So this is a process of spiritual elevation. This is a process of spiritual growth. This is the process of liberation from material energy. What is the process, the process is oral reception. You have to hear from authorized source. When you hear from the authorized source, submissive oral reception, that will cleanse our heart of all the lower gunas. Tama guna, raja guna, and all the non-guna and eventually even sattva guna and put us in pure vishuddha sattva, transcendental consciousness, just by hearing. It is not the path of Krishna consciousness. Krishna consciousness, ultimately, is a path of direct reception of the words that emanate from the transcendental platform.

#### transcendental sound, material intellect, faith
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/b4SP84ASur8?t=455
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The material speculative intelligence is like a shutter. It has to be opened up."
**Summary:** This segment uses the analogy of a camera shutter to explain how the material intellect can block the reception of transcendental sound, emphasizing the need for faith to open the 'shutter' and allow purification through hearing.

Krishna consciousness, ultimately, is a path of direct reception of the words that emanate from the transcendental platform. Direct reception, without the interference by our material intellect. The material intellect is like a shutter. It opens. It opens that shutter. This material intellect, a shutter always stops light, isn't it? In a camera, a shutter stops light. As far as spiritual bhakti is concerned, when you compare it with the process of receiving transcendental words from the spiritual master, receiving transcendental sound, the material speculative intelligence is like a shutter. It has to be opened up. What does that mean? First, one should have faith that the shabda, that the words that emanate from a pure devotee, has God, the knowledge is God, is contained within that. The intelligence to understand those words are also contained within that. You only have to receive transcendental intelligence. Your material intelligence gets purified by transcendental intelligence. Material intelligence cannot lead us to transcendental intelligence. Only oral reception can lead us to transcendental intelligence. Intelligence. By receiving oral, by oral reception of transcendental facts and transcendental intelligence, which is contained in those words, the Shakti is there in those, the transcendental knowledge Shakti or Vidya Shakti is there in that transcendental words. This is a very mystical process. We don't understand, we don't try to understand Bhaktivedanta Parpat. Like a college professor tries to understand, like a philosophy professor tries to understand Prabhupada's books. This is not the way we understand, we read. Okay?

#### purification, submissive hearing, transcendental energy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/b4SP84ASur8?t=635
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Moment you have submissive hearing, thousand times you hear Prabhupada saying, you are servant of Krishna, each time is bliss. Each time it gives energy."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the power of repeated, submissive hearing to purify the heart and mind, contrasting it with intellectual analysis that can hinder spiritual progress. It highlights the transcendental energy contained within the sound vibration.

It simply purifies one. By hearing, hearing, hearing, it simply purifies one. The moment the intellectual shatter comes, then everything becomes stale. The moment the intellectual shatter, you take Bhagavad Gita as it is, go on reading, parpatu parpatu, go on reading. I used to do this, first when I got Bhagavad Gita. It was so nice. Intellect stopped working. Page after page, what took, going on, went on reading, reading, and reading, and reading. Wanted to read the parpats and not trying to memorize, not trying to assimilate, not nothing. Going on, hearing, hearing, hearing, hearing, surrender to Krishna, you are servant of Krishna, surrender to Krishna, you are servant of Krishna. Suppose the intellect had come in between. Arrey, this I already know, he already told once. I am servant of Krishna, he has told once, I already know. Again he is saying, I am servant of Krishna, I already know. See, intellect means it ends there. Moment you know it ends there. But moment you have submissive hearing, thousand times you hear Prabhupada saying, you are servant of Krishna, each time is bliss. Each time it gives energy. Thousand times you hear Krishna is supreme personality of Godhead, each time it gives you energy. But as far as fact is concerned, it is only one bit of fact. What is that? Krishna is supreme personality of Godhead. But each time you read, read, read, every time you hear, you get transcendental. This is a very big secret for spiritual advancement. You have to understand that your, it is not, your real advancement doesn't come from your material buddhi. Sometimes we are all very overconfident about our material intelligence, our rational intelligence. It is very limited. It is a very limited. It is a very limited chapter. It is a way of growth. It is a starting way of growth of spiritual life. Because we are stuck in our intelligence, we have to purify our intelligence. We have to try to understand with our material logic, material intelligence, we have to try to understand. But you should understand further stages. You should understand that there is another way of simple repeated oral reception. This shabda has got, it's not, this shabda does not only have intellectual energy. It has got the ability to understand. This shabda has got the ability to understand. This shabda has got the ability to understand. This shabda has got the ability to speak. It has got the ability to understand. There is little energy there. The transcendental sound has all the energies that is required to drive away all the lower gunas.

#### repeated hearing, spiritual knowledge, subconscious mind
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/b4SP84ASur8?t=790
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "No spiritual knowledge will stay with you if you have a sense of ownership."
**Summary:** This segment advises repeated reading of Prabhupada's books, emphasizing that spiritual knowledge should be served, not owned. It explains how transcendental sound purifies the subconscious mind, where wrong feelings and habits are stored.

So that is the secret. By simply, repeatedly hearing, that is why we say you should read, read Prabhupada's book, read Prabhupada's book. Something you don't intellectually understand, leave it aside. Go on reading. Leave it for now. One day it will become apparent. It will become clear. Try a little to understand. Beyond that you don't try. Then it becomes speculation. Smoothly it should come. Understanding should come smoothly. No spiritual knowledge can be actually captured. No spiritual knowledge can actually be controlled. No spiritual knowledge will stay with you if you have a sense of ownership. Vidya will run away if you have a sense of ownership. You should have a sense of serving that knowledge. By giving out that knowledge. Hearing repeatedly that knowledge. What is the meaning of sense of ownership of knowledge? Moment in the idea comes. Okay, now I know this. It's mine. I am a knowledgeable person. I have it. But you know in one second your knowledge can go. It can go into forgetfulness. It is just like Karmis think. I am rich. I got lot of money. In one moment money can go anytime. He can become a pauper. In the same way transcendental knowledge also. You cannot have a sense of possession. You cannot have a sense of possession towards a transcendental knowledge. There should be a sense of reception. All reception. Welcoming and hearing the sound. That comes from a transcendental source. And that sound will expand transcendental knowledge into our heart. Realization into our heart. Transcendental feelings into our heart. And unless transcendental feelings come into our heart. Nothing will get purified. Because all our impurities are in the form of feelings. Wrong feelings. And where are these wrong feelings? These wrong feelings are all stored in our subconscious mind. That is why habits and all don't change. Intellectually you are convinced but your habits don't change. Why? Because they are all stored and packed and tied together with certain feelings in your subconscious. So unless the subconscious gets purified. Subconscious means you are not conscious.

#### submissive hearing, feelings, long-term memory
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/b4SP84ASur8?t=973
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Submissive hearing means even the feeling of the speaker will go inside."
**Summary:** This segment explains that submissive hearing allows the speaker's feelings to penetrate the listener's subconscious, leading to lasting change. It contrasts short-term memory (chemical state) with long-term memory (structural change in the brain) to illustrate the importance of deep absorption.

It has to get purified. Means the right feelings have to go inside as we discussed yesterday. Submissive hearing. Submissive hearing means even the feeling of the speaker will go inside. The feeling has to go inside because the contamination is inside. Deep inside. Unless it enters. Simply it remains at an intellectual conviction at your conscious level. That is not enough. You will get temporary benefit. 2-3 days you will be fired up. After hearing something. You hear a japa seminar 2-3 years. 2-3 days your chanting will be very nice. Afterwards it is gone. Why? 2-3 days it is in your short term memory it is there. Scientists have found out that in short term memory means it is only a chemical state. Today you hear something in your neurons. The chemical you know chemical adjustment. Long term memory means that chemical adjustment goes and changes your neurons structure. There is a structural change in your brain. Just other day I was reading this article about neuroplasticity of brain. Brain is such a wonderful thing. Neuroplasticity. Your change can happen by difference in chemical concentrations in the mind. In the brain. Let us say now you learn one sloka. In your short term memory it remains. Immediately in the brain there are certain chemical levels which is corresponding to this learning sloka. And it remains there. But in 2-3 days it will go away. It will go away. It will go away. But in 2-3 days that chemical will go to normal. You have forgotten this sloka. You cannot repeat that sloka. But if it goes into long term memory. That goes into a structure. Just like we go on saying you know. Say every day we start with Bhagavatam by chanting you know. Nityam Bhagavata Seveya Bhagavati Uttama Slokye Bhaktir Bhavati Naishitiki. Where is this? You are not consciously saying. It has become a structural change in your mind. Let us say first time when you learn that sloka. First 2-3 days. It has not yet become. You will forget it. Now you will not forget it. Because it will just come. Because it has become a structural change in your mind. So anyway. So that is about simple memory thing. So these things have to go deep inside through feelings. Then there has to be a submissive oral reception. That oral reception is so important. That it is such a big thing. That it is such a big factor. The word used is Karna Randrehe. Means through the holes of the ears. The favour of the spiritual master is not received through any part of the body but the ears.

It proposes that the institutional system he established is non-different from his direct instruction, requiring faith to perceive Prabhupada operating through temple presidents and other leaders.

Now next Prabhupada is touching upon another very very very very important point. One is about ok. Hearing the transcendental knowledge. The rest of the purport Prabhupada is talking about hearing instructions for your service. Whole purport is about it. The rest of the purport is about it. In earlier days. The Guru used to take only few disciples. Earlier days means just even before the movement. Before Bhaktivinoda Thakur. Even during Bhaktivinoda Thakur also. How many disciples he had? Bhakti Siddhanta Sarva Thakur. How many disciples? May be. Bhakti Siddhanta Sarva Thakur. More disciples in Gaudiya Mathura. First time Bhakti Siddhanta. Otherwise as per this tradition. Spiritual master has accepted some disciples. See so many things Prabhupada is saying here. Ok. The real fact is that spiritual master knows the nature of a particular man. And what sort of duties he can perform in Krishna consciousness. And he instructs him in that way. Now the big question comes. When Prabhupada started this Krishna consciousness movement. He built an institution. Temple presidents. GBC. Temple presidents. And all these things he set it up. Out of thousands and thousands of disciples. Srila Prabhupada would have directly instructed. What sort of duty. Probably you know a dozen or twenty or thirty or fifty disciples. Or even hundred disciples. He would have instructed. Therefore we have to understand that Srila Prabhupada. Instruction is non different from. Prabhupada giving direct instruction is non different from. From. The institutional system he set up for receiving instruction.

### Focused Hearing Drives Out All Thought-Processing | Sri Madhu Pandit Dasa | Soulful Japa Training
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

#### japa, chanting, attention
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/N1mRycUGyCc?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Japa means chanting the holy name, hearing that very name that you chant, and tasking to repeat and increase the number of chantings and hearings without any break in attention on the sound."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the concept of japa as chanting and hearing the holy name with focused attention.

As we have discussed many times, japa means chanting the holy name, hearing that very name that you chant, and tasking to repeat and increase the number of chantings and hearings without any break in attention on the sound.

---
#### mind, tasking, thinking
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/N1mRycUGyCc?t=35
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Mind is never quiet; it is always tasking something."
**Summary:** This segment explains that the mind is always engaged in tasking, either thinking or doing.

So, the mind is always tasking something, either tasking or doing. Suppose I tell you, get me a glass of water. It's a task that your mind will guide your senses. You'll go where the water is and bring the water to me. Till then, the mind is engaged in tasking. Do it. Tasking can be done. A tasking can be a doing. A tasking can be a problem-solving. Tasking can be a thinking. The mind involves in tasking either a doing or a thinking. For instance, you have a problem. You encounter a problem. Then your mind engages in tasking to find a solution for that. So, the mind is either always tasking. Mind is always tasking. Either thinking tasking or doing tasking. Mind is never quiet. It is always tasking something.

---
#### japa, Shabda Avatara, holy name
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/N1mRycUGyCc?t=110
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The name of the absolute Lord, therefore, the name is Lord himself."
**Summary:** This segment explains that chanting the holy names is meditation on the sound, which is the Lord himself appearing as Shabda Avatara.

So, in japa, in nama japa, chanting, chanting the holy names of the Lord, it's japa meditation. Meditation on the sound because it is not an ordinary song. The name of the absolute Lord, therefore, the name is Lord himself. Lord is before you as a sound incarnation, is before you as a Shabda Avatara. As a Shabda Avatara, Avatara means the Lord comes in a very accessible way to our senses. So, Shabda Avatara means the Lord comes, enters a sound in order to give us association. This is called incarnation. So, this sound, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Ram, Hare Ram, Hare Hare, is not an ordinary sound. It is a sound in which the Lord has entered. It becomes a Lord's sound body. Touching this sound is equal to touching the Lord. The Lord is all pure, all knowledge, all bliss, all love.

When you touch the Lord, then just like iron put in a fire becomes red hot, transformed. The same way by association with the sound through hearing, our consciousness becomes blissful, becomes enlightened as to who we are, that we are eternal beings within this temporary body. We are eternal servants of Krishna. This enlightenment comes in the heart of a person who continuously touches the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra through hearing. And one gets spiritual strength to control the mind, control the senses, by simply associating with this Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare. So, japa is tasking of chanting, hearing, chanting, hearing, chanting, hearing towards a particular goal. Number one, mala 108 times, just like today we are doing 108 into 108. In the same day, if you are doing 16 rounds, then your task is complete 16 rounds. In one session, you are going to, you have sat and you have decided, I am going to do four rounds, then you have to task. Your task is four rounds. What is this tasking? During japa, what is this task? During japa, it is a doing task and not a thinking task. Our mind is constantly thinking and keeping us away from ourselves. It is keeping us away from God.

Don't neglect. Don't be disrespectful. Giving attention is a first respect. It is usually between the two mantras that your mind goes away somewhere else. So that is why I say you have to do inter-mantra tasking, there mind has to be careful, now next theory, no other thinking, next theory, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, so you should be consciously tasked mantra to mantra, it is called inter-mantra tasking. And within the mantra, Hare Vavara, Krishna, Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, that is called intra-mantra, within the mantra you are tasking, so that you hear mantra. So today you please practice this tasking, today's another 8 rounds, whatever I told, this aspect module about tasking, what is tasking, tasking means moving your attention within the mantra and between the mantras, putting an effort to see that nothing else comes inside. And then like that you simply do, it is not very complicated, it is not concentration, it is concentration on the task. It is not concentration on Hare, it is not concentration on Krishna, it is not concentration on Rama, no, you cannot concentrate, that kind of concentration not to ask, hearing me is over, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare.

That means doing, why hearing is a doing also? Because you have to handle your attention. Your attention is under your control. Not only that. Now you have decided to listen to what I am speaking. That means you have controlled your attention to hear what I am saying. So attention is in your control. Usually we, the mind takes our attention, wherever the mind takes my attention, I am also going, but actually attention is your property. You can decide. You can tell the mind. No, not here, here. Attention is a carrier. Mind is a carrier of attention. Be here now. Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Krishna, string over, then Hare Rama string, Hare Krishna over, Hare Rama, one bead over, one bead over, one bead over, one bead over, one bead over. God is so merciful. Our soul is like iron. Krishna is so merciful, so kind that he descends through our ears, into our heart. Fire is coming to you. The fire of Satya and Ananda is coming to you. I want your association by hearing you. So the Lord comes as a sound incarnation, you have to welcome and embrace him by hearing. Hearing is equal to embracing Krishna's sound body. There's no difference between. There's no difference between Krishna's sound body and Krishna himself. So such is the power, purifying power of the Hare Krishna, hearing the Hare Krishna Mahamantra. ## HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### Gaura Purnima 2026 | 1st Anniversary of Arunodaya Kirtan | Sri Madhu Pandit Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

#### Arunodaya Kirtan, Congregational Chanting, Lord Chaitanya's Grace
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TLxtrwKrtV0?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Today, by Lord Chaitanya's grace, it has become a vibrant spiritual activity touching thousands of lives."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa celebrates the first anniversary of Arunodaya Kirtan in Bangalore, highlighting its impact and the blessings of various deities and Srila Prabhupada.

Hare Krishna, happy Gopurnima to all of you. Today we are very happy to share a wonderful milestone by the mercy of Shri Shri Radha Krishna Chandra, Krishna Balaram, Thayagauranga, Srinivasa Govinda, Pralana Shingadev and the blessings of Vishwaguru Shilaprabhu Paan. We have achieved the milestone of completing one full year of Arunodaya Kirtan in Bangalore city. One year ago, this initiative began as a humble offering, a sincere desire to bring devotees together from the early hours of the morning in congregational chanting of the holy names of the Lord in the streets of Bangalore. Today, by Lord Chaitanya's grace, it has become a vibrant spiritual activity touching thousands of lives.

---
#### Nama Sankirtana, Love of God, Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TLxtrwKrtV0?t=53
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Because our dormant love of God already exists within our heart and it can be awakened simply by chanting Hare Krishna Mahamantra daily with sincerity and enthusiasm."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of congregational chanting as the mission of Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, explaining that it awakens dormant love of God without any pre-qualifications.

When we perform congregational chanting, we are directly connected to the mission of Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. Shilaprabhupada explains that Lord Chaitanya, the greatest proponent of love of God and the father of congregational chanting of the holy name, personally inaugurated the religion of this age, Nama Sankirtana. Mahaprabhu declared, "I shall personally inaugurate the religion of this age, Nama Sankirtana, the congregation chanting of the holy names." He said, "I will make the world dance in ecstasy." And what is this process? Shilaprabhupada beautifully explains that there is no pre-qualification required, no material condition to be fulfilled, because our dormant love of God already exists within our heart and it can be awakened simply by chanting Hare Krishna Mahamantra daily with sincerity and enthusiasm. Through this joyful process of chanting and dancing, one gradually develops genuine love for the Supreme Lord. This is the great mercy of Lord in this age.

---
#### Accessibility of Devotion, Arunodaya Kirtan Results, Prasadam Distribution
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TLxtrwKrtV0?t=125
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "And over the past year, we have witnessed the results practically."
**Summary:** The speaker highlights the accessibility of devotional service through chanting, sharing the practical results of Arunodaya Kirtan in various settings and the abundant distribution of prasadam.

There is no requirement for high birth, no requirement for scholarship and no complicated ritual. Simply chant, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. And over the past year, we have witnessed the results practically. Arunodaya Kirtan has been conducted in so many places, in temples, in homes, during festivals, in the community gatherings. During Janmashtami, Ram Navami, Nithyananda Trodeshi, Narsimha Jayanti, Ratha Yatra and many more occasions, thousands have participated in Bangalore city in chanting and dancing. Along with the Kirtan, Prasadam has been distributed abundantly, following the instructions of Srimad Bhagavatam.

---
#### Sankirtana Yajna, World Peace, Unity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TLxtrwKrtV0?t=179
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "In such a disturbed climate, congregational chanting of the holy names offer a unifying spiritual platform."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that in this age, intelligent persons worship the Supreme Lord by the sacrifice of Sankirtana, and that congregational chanting offers a unifying spiritual platform in a disturbed world.

In this age, intelligent persons worship the Supreme Lord by the sacrifice of Sankirtana. Srila Prabhupada describes how even the wild animals in the forest of Jarikanda joined Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's Sankirtana. Today the world is disturbed socially, politically, economically and in so many other ways. There is visible strife between individuals, divisions between communities, tensions among nations. So much mistrust, conflict and anxiety. They have become common feature of modern life. In such a disturbed climate, congregational chanting of the holy names offer a unifying spiritual platform. By bringing people together, beyond the differences of background, status and ideology, we are helping them reconnect with the common foundation of love of God. Through Nama Sankirtana, our hearts are purified, relationships are harmonized and genuine unity is achieved among all.

---
#### Encouragement, Home Kirtan, Following Srila Prabhupada
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TLxtrwKrtV0?t=255
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Let every home become a center for Kirtan."
**Summary:** The speaker encourages everyone to continue the mission of Sankirtana with enthusiasm, suggesting that every home become a center for Kirtan and to follow in the footsteps of Srila Prabhupada.

The more we perform this Sankirtana Yajna, the more we advance in love of God. Therefore, on this important occasion of completing one year of Arunodaya Kirtan, I would like to encourage all of you, let us continue with our mission with even more enthusiasm. Let every home become a center for Kirtan. If you have the facility, invite devotees to your home, distribute prasadam, hold Kirtan for a few hours, at least once a week. Gather your friends, your family, your neighbors. Just as the rising sun dissipates darkness, congregational chanting dissipates the darkness of ignorance from the heart. Lord Chaitanya predicted that this Sankirtana movement would spread every town and village of the world. Srila Prabhupada established ISKCON with the purpose of teaching this very same process. Let us follow in the footsteps of our Gurudev, Srila Prabhupada.

---
#### Arunodaya Kirtan Invitation, Commitment, Spiritual Benefits
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TLxtrwKrtV0?t=323
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Simply chant with faith and enthusiasm. Distribute prasadam with joy. There is no loss. There is only gain, spiritual happiness, purification, unity, and ultimately, love of God."
**Summary:** The speaker invites everyone to participate in the Arunodaya Kirtan on Gaurapurnima, emphasizing the importance of making a firm commitment and highlighting the spiritual benefits of chanting and distributing prasadam.

I would also like to invite all devotees to participate for the Arunodaya Kirtan on 3rd March, Gaurapurnima, the most auspicious day of Lord Chaitanya's appearance, who is the inaugurator of the Sankirtana movement 500 years back. This year, it falls on a weekday. You may have office commitments, important meetings, or responsibility for preparing your children for school. Yet, in your schedule, rise a little earlier if needed. Plan your day in advance and make it a firm commitment not to miss this Arunodaya Kirtan of 3rd March, 2026. Let us assemble in large numbers, in thousands, and offer our collective chanting as our heartful tribute to Lord Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. On this sacred morning, let us begin the day with the holy names and receive His special mercy. Let us take this Arunodaya Kirtan to more areas, more communities, more homes. Let thousands become lakhs. Let lakhs become millions. Simply chant with faith and enthusiasm. Distribute prasadam with joy. There is no loss. There is only gain, spiritual happiness, purification, unity, and ultimately, love of God. This is Krishna Sankirtanam. Sisi Go Nithaya Ki Jai. Arunodaya Sankirtan Ki Jai. Vishwaguru Sheela Prabhupada Ki Jai.

---

#### attention in chanting, sat-chit-ananda, Krishna's pleasure
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7xTYvkfhG4o?t=811
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is very pleased if we chant and hear. Pleased for us."
**Summary:** This segment uses the analogy of iron in fire to explain how attention during chanting allows us to connect with Krishna's sat-chit-ananda, and how Krishna is pleased when we chant attentively because it helps us become liberated.

Devotee, he is chanting mechanically, he is not hearing, thinking of so many other things while the tongue is chanting. Still the Lord is there, but He is saying, 'Oh, how foolish my devotee is. I am before him. I am available as a sound for him to embrace with his attention, but his attention is going somewhere else.' If he embraces me, then there can be flow of sat, chit, ananda, shakti into him, to awaken him, his consciousness, because Krishna is the Surya of sat, chit, ananda. Just like Prabhupada says, a piece of iron put in the fire, slowly it becomes hot, hotter, hotter, red hot. Red hot means fire like fire. Red hot means fire like fire. Red hot means fire like fire. In the same way, Krishna is thinking, he is chanting, 'I am there, the fire of sat, chit, ananda,' but he is not coming into the fire of sat, chit, ananda. Only when you give attention, then you are in the fire. So Krishna is very pleased if we chant and hear. Pleased for us, but we don't get anything. He gets our happiness. My Jeeva who is suffering will get liberated.

You are doing japa, but you are neglecting to hear. You are neglecting to give your mind to the nama of the Lord. You are neglecting to give your mind to the avatara who is before us and all these different stages of good quality chanting, good quality japa has to be practiced in a systematic way. You are neglecting to give your mind to the avatara who is before us.

For several months, probably I would say at least two months, you completely focus on mindful japa by reading the cards 1.1 to 1.6, 2.1 to 2.6, 3.1 to 3.6. By understanding what is in the card, which gives us, which will load into your consciousness, into your background mind, into your memory, what is, why you are doing japa, what is proper japa, who is the holy name, who is the chanter, what should be your consciousness. ### Hear Submissively to Change Old Perceptions | Sri Madhu Pandit Dasa | SB 3.22.10 | 07.08.2024
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

#### hearing from authority, faith, spiritual experience
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/INEV6vpZpUE?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Hearing from an authority is a better experience than gaining personal understanding."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the principle that hearing from a spiritual authority provides a better experience than personal understanding, challenging common sense and emphasizing the need for faith.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, 10-3, Chapter 22, entitled The Marriage of Kardamamuni and Devahuti, Verse No. 10: YADATU BHAVATASILA SHRUTA RUPA VAYO DUNAN ASHRANON NARADHA DESHA TVAYAYA SITRITANISHAYA. Translation in purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada: The moment she heard from the sage Narada of your noble character, learning, beautiful appearance, youth and other virtues, she fixed her mind upon you. Purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada: The girl Devahuti did not personally see Kardamamuni, nor did she personally experience. She did not personally see Kardamamuni, his character or qualities, since there was no social intercourse by which she could gain such understanding. But she heard about Tridamamuni from the authority of Narada Muni. Hearing from an authority is a better experience than gaining personal understanding. She heard from Narada Muni that Tridamamuni was just fit to be her husband, therefore she became fixed in her heart that she would marry him, and she expressed her desire to her father who therefore brought her before him. Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta Parvat. Devahuti, the daughter of Swayambhuva Manu, came to fix her mind upon desiring Kardamamuni, is being explained by Manu, and it is specifically explained in the purport by Prabhupada that she knew about Kardamamuni only by hearing from Narada Muni. And by hearing from Narada Muni about Kardamamuni, she got a better experience than even gaining personal understanding. So this principle, hearing from an authority is better experience than gaining personal understanding, it is a very heavy principle being made here. Thank you. Generally, this does not appeal to common sense. One will think that first I should hear and then I should experience. First I should hear and then experience. In spiritual life also we have a tendency, because that is how our mind works. Now I am reading these things, one day I will directly experience. But that is completely being, that process is completely being countered by the statement of Prabhupada. Hearing from an authority is a better experience than gaining personal understanding. We have to believe it. It does not appeal to common sense. It is a very heavy principle. It does not appeal to common sense.

**Summary:** This segment explains that hearing about Krishna from a pure source is superior to seeing Krishna with impure senses, emphasizing the importance of a pure source and submissive hearing in spiritual life.

This is relative. Which is better? Just like which is better, hearing about Krishna or seeing Krishna? According to this, if you hear from an authority about Krishna, it is better than seeing Krishna. Because if you happen to see Krishna, if you are not pure, you are not able to understand Krishna. If you are not pure then you will think oh maybe somebody from some drama party has come before me even if he is genuine Krishna. Now hearing Krishna purely, there are different ways of hearing about Krishna, the source should be pure. Now let's go into this hearing experience number one source should be pure. Here because Narada spoke, the hearer should be submissive, because only when you are submissive then the perception will go into your subconscious. At the conscious level your hearing is always being analyzed and judged. Real hearing is not analyzing and judging. Submissive hearing means allowing that sound to enter you and that sound will expand that perception expand the meaning of the shabda. One should have faith that this shabda transcendental of course Prabhupada is talking about transcendental hearing which is you know in our normal world what is it's the other way around no, you hear something and then you hear about America and go and see America, that is correct relative world it is correct, but in the spiritual world it's not like that not spiritual hearing nor during our sadhana stage, it is not like that. Anything to do with spiritual shabda is not that. One has to have faith that the spiritual shabda is impregnated with the meaning of that shabda. What multi-dimensional meaning that are all contained in it. If I use a word, if I say pen, in that word pen, in the material world, pen is not there. In the spiritual shabda, say Hare or Krishna or Rama, in that shabda, Hare is there, Krishna is there, Rama is there. There is no difference between shabda and the personality of that shabda. Because everything is personality. Prabhupada gives example in the spiritual world also. Water, water, you say. You have water.

Whether your thirst should be quenched or not, that water should mercifully decide. Here you put water on the tongue, there is no question of not getting quenched. Because the water here is just matter. It has to. But in the spiritual world, water is a person. So you say water, water, water. That is why it is said, Atta, Ashi, Krishna, Nama, Naba, Naba, Ved, Gra. Mahim, Indriya. Anything in the spiritual world you cannot capture. Everything is a person. There has to be a relationship. You say water, water, water. Water is not different from water. But that water decides personality or I should keep this person thirsty. You decide to keep thirsty. To quench the thirst or not to quench the thirst is the decision of the personality of water. But it is not different. So in the same way, the holy name, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. If the shabda is there, then automatically we should automatically become pure in a moment, because it is Krishna himself I am touching. No, he is a person. You deserve, you qualify. Are you begging? What is your mood? Have you fed up with material world? Are you ready to experience me? No, all those things. Yes. Now I will purify you. Chetodharpanam. Chetodharpanam Arjanam. That too, if you are hearing, is a relationship. Oh, and where is the question of, you know, even Chetodharpanam Arjanam, this fellow is completely offensive, this Jeeva. He is utilizing my name for so many things. He thinks I can, you know, I can be used for everything. For his power, knowledge, fame, everything, he can use Harinam. So will Harinam respond? Will not respond. He will respond. He will respond. He will respond appropriately. But the fact is that the Lord is there. Nama Incarnation is there. The Nama Incarnation, the specific character is there. He is very, very merciful. The most merciful incarnation is Nama Incarnation, which is brought down by Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. And this Hare Krishna Maha Mantra is non-different from Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. Radha Krishna Anya. This mantra is nothing but the personalities of Radha and Krishna. So spiritual hearing means you have to remove the shutter of rational mind. Submissively one should hear. Submissively and faithfully one should hear. It's a big, it's a very big transformative power, transformative process that we are all possessing.

How to read Prabhupada's books? How to read Prabhupada's books so that it enters your subconscious? Conscious mind, we are constantly filtering it. How does something go into the subconscious when you are very emotional? Isn't it? That is how people get trauma and all, isn't it? The situation is charged with emotions, your subconscious opens. That is how you say, by this experience I learnt. It opens. Otherwise rational mind will not, your lesson will just remain there. And in few days it will go away. Whatever you learn with your rational mind, so much logic you will tell and this is that, this is that. It will remain for two, three days and it will go away. Those things are not called realizations. Realization is when it goes into your subconscious. And there it brings about permanent change in your perception. Permanent change in your attitude towards life. Towards life. So how to make this shabda of Prabhupada enter into our subconscious? As I said, one of the things is to stop this, remove the shutter of rational filter that we have. Where we analyze and judge everything. I mean you are reading everything. How Krishna is God, why Krishna is God, all these things. Your rational mind. But actually that answer to your rational mind is not what made you devotee, by the way. Because how Krishna is God, why Krishna is God, you will never find the proof in the Bhagavad Gita. It is not a book of proof. It is a book of declaration. Krishna is going on saying, I am God, I am God. And he is saying so many authorities have told I am God. There is no evidence there given.

**Key Quote:** "Build first of all faith. And have a paradigm of descending analysis."

And how is that then? Is there a method by which we can actually make this enter? In my own story, very easily I can say. When I got Bhagavad Gita as it is, first time, I did not know why I followed that process. I followed that process. Something was so nice. I went on reading very fast. Did not say to analyze and all. That means whatever was going inside, was going inside. Whatever I did not understand, I didn't try to. Like this for long time, Gita, going on, reading, reading. Because it was very tasty because Krishna is, Prabhupada is repeatedly saying. So what Prabhupada is saying? Largely I was concentrating on the purpose of Prabhupada. Not even connecting the verses of Krishna's. I was just going on reading the Bhagavad Gita, purport, purport, purport, purport, purport, purport. Going on reading the purports. Sometimes the verse also I would skip. Going on reading the purports. So something entered me. Because there you are not trying to analyze at that stage. I am not saying you should not analyze, you should not study. That is another process. That is, we have to have control over our faith. We have to have control over our faith. We have to have so much faith. Build first of all faith. And have a paradigm of descending analysis. What is descending analysis? This is 100% correct. Not speculative analysis. Descending process analysis. This what is told is correct. I want to understand how it is correct. I know it is correct. Conclusion I have adopted. But I would like to relish how it is correct. Additional bonus. It is correct. So that way, this is the way in which the Shastras are also studied with rational mind. Because it has got, everything has got rationality behind it. **Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/INEV6vpZpUE?t=1058
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Our mind is full of three things: Perception of things, perception of people, perception of situation, and actions."
**Summary:** This segment explains how submissive hearing can lead to a paradigm shift by penetrating the subconscious and altering perspectives on things, people, situations, and actions.

Sometimes you hear, you are very submissively sitting in the class, you hear something. It goes and penetrates your subconscious. Your paradigm shifts. Because all these paradigms are nothing but perspectives which we have stored in our subconscious. How we see things. How we see people. How we see situations. Right? Our mind is full of three things. Three or four things you can see. If you take in mind, it contains in that box, there are four things. Perception of things. Here you see a camera. You see a kartal. You see this thing. Things, things. Room. Building. You have mapped those buildings into it. That is perception of things. Then there is perception of people. What do I think of this person? This person? This person? This person? Right? This person is a man. This person is a woman. You see. Or this person is nice person. This person is bad person. He is friend. He is enemy. So perception of people. Then there is perception of situation. Is it not? Continuously full of these things. Say today you are going out preaching. It is a preaching situation. It is a compound. You get at a glance. Just like I say guru issue. Moment I say guru issue. It is a situation. So many things you will derive out of it. What is guru issue? I tell you. Give me. What is this guru issue? If I ask you to come here. You will stand and you will describe what it is. Actually you are describing what you have your perception. Is it not? So perception of things. People. Situation. And actions. You also perceive actions. Sinful activity. Not sinful activity. Is it not? Actions. Good act. Bad act. Actions. Here there is a fight. There is a situation. And in the situation there is an action of a fight. So there is an action. So object. People. Situations. And actions. O. P. S. A. Our head consists of only O. P. S. A.

## HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### How the Principle of Gurutva Functions Universally | Sri Madhu Pandit Dasa | SB 3.22.8 | 06.08.2024
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

#### Guru-disciple relationship, Humility, Devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YgfS_Iid9sM?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When a disciple is perfectly in consonance with the spiritual master having received his message and executed it perfectly and sincerely he has a right to ask a particular favor from the spiritual master."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa begins by reciting the verse and translation, then reads Srila Prabhupada's purport, which discusses the relationship between a disciple and the spiritual master, emphasizing the importance of satisfying the guru before asking for favors.

Om namo bhagavate vasudevaya. Mad-Bhagavatam, Canto 3, Chapter 22, Text 8. Sabhavandh trasneha pariklishthatmano mama srotam arhasi dhinasya shravitam kripaya mune. Translation in purport by S. D. V. Bhai Charanarvinda Bhaktivedanta Swamishila Prabhupada. Translation: O great sage, graciously be pleased to listen to the prayer of my humble self, for my mind is troubled by affection from my daughter. Purport by Srila Prabhupada: When a disciple is perfectly in consonance with the spiritual master having received his message and executed it perfectly and sincerely he has a right to ask a particular favor from the spiritual master.

---
#### Guru-disciple relationship, Humility, Prayer
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YgfS_Iid9sM?t=61
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Swayambhuva Manu wanted to disclose his mind regarding the function he wanted to execute due to affection for his daughter."
**Summary:** The lecture continues with the purport, explaining that a pure devotee generally doesn't ask for favors, but if needed, it must be done after fully satisfying the spiritual master. Swayambhuva Manu's concern about his daughter's marriage and seeking Kardama Muni's attention are highlighted.

Generally, a pure devotee of the Lord or a pure disciple of a bona fide spiritual master does not ask any favor either from the Lord or the spiritual master. But even if there is a need to ask a favor from the spiritual master one cannot ask that favor without satisfying him fully. Swayambhuva Manu wanted to disclose his mind regarding the function he wanted to execute due to affection for his daughter. Thus ends the bhaktivedanta purport. Swayambhuva Manu is seeking the attention of Karadvama Muni to consider his prayer with regard to something that is disturbing his mind or troubled by the affection for my daughter.

---
#### Humility, Respect, Grace
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YgfS_Iid9sM?t=125
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The guru is pleased with the disciple when he receives service from the disciple."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa elaborates on Swayambhuva Manu's concern about his daughter's marriage and his respectful approach to Kardama Muni. He emphasizes the humility in addressing the spiritual master and the importance of pleasing him, not as a transaction, but through grace.

Swayambhuva Manu was concerned about getting his daughter married and obviously he had in his mind Karadvama Muni. So he is saying here, O great sage, graciously be pleased to listen to the prayer of my humble self. This is how one addresses the spiritual master even if he has to, if he has to be addressed with respect to any request. See the humility. He is not just going and asking something. He is respecting him so much. He says, graciously be pleased. I am not saying that, I qualify that you be pleased with me. Graciously be pleased. Please be graceful. If someone asks somebody, please be pleased. I want you to be pleased with me. When is somebody pleased? When there is an exchange. The guru is pleased with the disciple when he receives service from the disciple. When he receives that, he is pleased with the disciple. But the disciple is thinking, just because I did service, even if he is pleased, it is because of his grace and not because of my contribution.

---
#### Guru-disciple relationship, Grace, Humility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YgfS_Iid9sM?t=271
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is his free will to be pleased or not be pleased with me."
**Summary:** The lecture continues to emphasize that even with service, the guru's pleasure is due to his grace, not the disciple's merit. The relationship between guru and disciple is not about earning mercy, but about the guru's free will and grace.

Even if I do service, he need not be pleased. So he says, graciously be pleased. This is the way Swami Prabhupada Manu is addressing, the spiritual master would address, as a disciple will address a spiritual master. So everywhere, in the relationship between guru and disciple, this is a very key point. That, it is not a relationship of, you know, earning mercy or earning something by doing service to the spiritual master, you earn something. That is not the relationship. That is not the mood also. I will serve my spiritual master. It is his free will to be pleased or not be pleased with me. It is his own grace upon me, if he is pleased. That attitude is there.

---
#### Humility, Devotional Service, Krishna Consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YgfS_Iid9sM?t=377
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The spiritual master is pleased with pure devotional service."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the disciple's humility stems from recognizing their inability to offer pure devotional service directly to Krishna. The spiritual master, being situated in absolute purity, offers the disciple's service to Krishna, as the spiritual master is pleased with pure devotional service.

Because the disciple doesn't think that he can actually, he has the quality, humility to do pure devotional service to the Lord. The spiritual master is pleased with pure devotional service. Because spiritual master, he is situated in a platform of absolute purity in his relationship with Krishna. So whatever service the living entity is offering to the spiritual master, naturally, the spiritual master doesn't want any service. He offers it to Krishna. Ekala, Ishvara, Krishna, Arasava, Brijya. Only Krishna is Ishvara. So a pure devotee of the Lord is offering pure devotional service. So when the disciple is offering service to spiritual master for Krishna to please him, the disciple knows that whatever I am offering is not worthy of going to Krishna. It is only by his grace it is reaching Krishna.

---
#### Grace, Spiritual Master, Krishna Consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YgfS_Iid9sM?t=532
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If you are getting attached to Krishna, it is because of the mercy of the spiritual master."
**Summary:** The speaker uses an analogy of needing 100 units of humility for service to reach Krishna, and how the spiritual master compensates for the disciple's lack, offering it to the Lord. He emphasizes that advancement in Krishna consciousness, attachment to Krishna, and purification are all due to the spiritual master's mercy.

The disciple never thinks that I can serve the spiritual master directly. I can serve Krishna directly. I can serve my spiritual master directly but I cannot serve Krishna directly. I have no qualification to serve Krishna directly. Krishna is surrounded and served by pure devotees of the Lord. So therefore, if at all my service has to reach Krishna, it is reaching Krishna because of the spiritual master's grace upon us. Whatever is lacking, whatever is lacking from the disciple, whatever the disciple offers to Krishna, it is pure devotional service. Whatever is lacking, the spiritual master compensates it and then offers it to the Lord. This is his kindness. Suppose something, a quantity of let's say 100 is required. Just to give an example, 100 units of something is required. Say 100 units of humility is required for a service to go to Krishna. And we are offering service to the spiritual master. You may have one or you may have minus one or minus two or whatever. We offer that service to the spiritual master. The spiritual master, whether it is minus one or minus 100, he will make it plus 100 and offer it to Krishna because he sees something is there. So much is the transparent via media function of the spiritual master. So merciful he is. And if we are making advancement in Krishna consciousness, it is because of the mercy of the spiritual master. If you are getting attached to Krishna, it is because of the mercy of the spiritual master. If you are getting purified, it is due to the mercy of the spiritual master. That mercy, that is what graciously, the Lord is, the spiritual master, please be graciously be pleased with me. You cannot say, be pleased with me. Graciously be pleased with me. So sensitive. Because he recognizes that in terms of his absolute goal of going back to Krishna is most unqualified. So in that mood, he is praying, Swayambhu Ammanu is praying to him. That's this verse.

I thought I had a lot of questions were there the other day on the previous purport. I'll just read that once again. Previous translation. #### Krishna Consciousness, Service, Spiritual Master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YgfS_Iid9sM?t=690
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is the function of a saintly person to invoke Krishna Consciousness in every forgetful man."
**Summary:** The lecture continues with the purport, highlighting the reciprocal service between the guru and disciple, the guru's desire to elevate the disciple, and the importance of receiving the transcendental message through oral reception. It clarifies that the spiritual master knows the nature of the disciple and instructs them accordingly in Krishna Consciousness.

This is reciprocal service. A bona fide spiritual master, a saintly person always desires to elevate a common man who comes to him because everyone is under the delusion of Maya and is forgetful of his prime duty, Krishna Consciousness. Saintly person always desires that everyone becomes a saintly person. It is the function of a saintly person to invoke Krishna Consciousness in every forgetful man. Manu said that he was advised and instructed by Kardama Muni he was very much favored. He considered himself lucky to receive the message, by oral reception. It is especially mentioned here that one should be very inquisitive to hear with open ears from the authorized source of a bona fide spiritual master. How is one to receive? One should receive the transcendental message by oral reception. The word Karna Randhrahey means through the holes of the ears. The favor of the spiritual master is not received through any other part of the body but the ears. This does not mean however that the spiritual master gives a particular type of mantra through the ears in exchange for some dollars and if the man meditates on that he achieves perfection and becomes God within six months. Such reception through the ears is bogus. The real fact is that a bona fide spiritual master knows the nature of a particular man and what sort of duties he can perform in Krishna Consciousness and he instructs him in that way. He instructs him through the ear not privately but publicly. You are fit for such and such a work in Krishna Consciousness you can act in this way. One person is advised to work in Krishna Consciousness by working in the deity's room another is advised to act in Krishna Consciousness by performing editorial work another is advised to do preaching work another is advised to carry out Krishna Consciousness in the cooking department. There are different departments of activities in Krishna Consciousness and the spiritual master knowing the particular ability of a particular man trains him in such a way that he becomes perfect.

---
#### Service, Instructions, Perfection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YgfS_Iid9sM?t=817
**Verse References:** BG 18.45
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The faithful execution of the instruction which he receives from the spiritual master is the only duty of a disciple and that will bring him perfection."
**Summary:** The speaker continues reading the purport, explaining that one can attain the highest perfection by offering service according to their ability, as instructed by the spiritual master. The combination of the spiritual master's instruction and the disciple's faithful execution makes the process perfect.

Bhagavad Gita makes it clear that one can attain the highest perfection in spiritual life simply by offering service according to his ability just as Arjuna served Krishna by his ability in the military art. Arjuna offered his service fully as a military man and he became perfect. Similarly an artist can attain perfection simply by performing artistic work on the direction of the spiritual master. If one is a literary man he can write articles and poetry for the service of God under the direction of the spiritual master. One has to receive the message of the spiritual master regarding how to act in one's capacity for the spiritual master is expert in giving such instructions. This combination the instruction of the spiritual master and the fateful execution of the instruction by the disciple makes the entire process perfect. Srila Vishnu Achakravati Thakur describes in his explanation of his verse in Bhagavad Gita that one who wants to be certain to achieve spiritual success must take the instructions from the spiritual master as to what his particular function is. He should faithfully try to execute the particular instruction and should consider that his life and soul. The faithful execution of the instruction which he receives from the spiritual master is the only duty of a disciple and that will bring him perfection. One should be very careful to receive the instruction and execute it faithfully that will make one's life successful.

And we were respecting the chain of authority in a natural way. Nobody was questioning at all and everyone felt the service toward Srila Prabhupada. And then the element was such conviction that this chain ended in Srila Prabhupada that every one of us very willingly renounced this need that came as a little child needs to be with the father personally. So, that was his mercy through his service for his mission. #### Faith, Process, Spiritual Master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YgfS_Iid9sM?t=1024
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This process is so powerful having the right faith in the process itself that the presence of Srila Prabhupada is not only right now in front of me and you."
**Summary:** The devotee continues sharing their experience, noting that while they could write to Srila Prabhupada, they often didn't send the letters, respecting his time for service. They observed the answers to their questions emerging through the process of devotional service, highlighting the power of faith in the process and the continued presence of Srila Prabhupada.

was one element that solved or at least for several of us that impediment let's call it or distance it was that we could write Srila Prabhupada any moment but we knew also that there were hundreds of letters every day. In my memory I can still continue writing but never delivered it. These letters were accumulated and were checked by our own in the course of time because we were consider about his time for the service and then in due course of time we could see the letters being answered through the process of devotional service. And it's a very good practice even I regret I cannot do it now but I do in another way but it's very scientific and practical not having envy but most of them became too familiar. So this process is so powerful having the right faith in the process itself that the presence of Srila Prabhupada is not only right now in front of me and you.
