## HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### How to Become Free from Past Karmas? | Sri Madhu Pandit Dasa | SB 3.22.12 | 14.08.2024
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

#### sense gratification, karma, body
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lEvontw9tWU?t=32
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We have taken this body in this material world for enjoying, so therefore the Lord has mercifully given this body for just like every karma He gives, He gives us a body according to our karma."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains that everyone in material life desires sense gratification and that the body is given according to one's karma, with a fixed potential for Sukha and Dukha (happiness and distress) that unfolds over time.

To deny an offering that has come of itself is not commendable, even for one absolutely free from all attachment, much less one addicted to sensual pleasure. In material life, everyone is desirous of sense gratification. How we manage the forces of the mind, the body, and the senses that rise up? We are given guidelines by Krishna in Bhagavad Gita, by our Acharyas, by the Purvacharyas. How you conduct your life in a manner in which you handle these urges? We handle these natural, bodily, mental urges. We cannot deny that these urges are not there. Nobody can deny. We have taken this body in this material world for enjoying, so therefore the Lord has mercifully given this body. Just like every karma He gives, He gives us a body according to our karma. This body has got a certain value. It has got a certain potential of Sukha and Dukha, which is unveiled, it's a function of time. Tomorrow on Sukha Dukha, day after tomorrow on Sukha Dukha, next year, 100 years from now, 10 years from now, 15 years from now, it's a function of Kala, Sukha Dukha. But Prabhupada says and the Shastra says that Sukha Dukha is a function of time. It's a function of time. So Sukha Dukha, material Sukha Dukha for the body and the mind, is fixed for everybody when one takes birth. It is fixed. Sukha and Dukha is an experience. So Sukha Dukha is fixed. Not that this particular Sukha is fixed, this particular Dukha is fixed. Not that the full chart of where all you will get Sukha, where all you will get Dukha is fixed. That is not what it means. Then you have no choice. Your Sukha Dukha is fixed means, suppose there is a currency. Currency is used to buy something, no? Suppose there is a currency called Sukha currency. And there is another currency called Dukha currency. When you are born, you are given a purpose. It is not called Sukha currency. ये यह Kook LDK हो रहेंग्गी, जिसी पास आप आज 60 शत्riage लिया हैं, तारे से 60-60वें क Australia पाका लगा जाता है.

---
#### devotional service, austerity, karma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lEvontw9tWU?t=812
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If the devotee is having the, you know, has determined to work hard for Prabhupada, take trouble for Prabhupada, sweat for Prabhupada, take tension for Prabhupada, take anxiety for Prabhupada, please understand that you are burning your Dukkha currency."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that devotees use their 'currency' for Krishna's service, transcending the duality of the material world. Taking trouble for Prabhupada burns 'Dukkha currency,' while avoiding such trouble leads to suffering unrelated to Krishna's service.

What the devotee does is what? He uses that currency for Krishna's service. The duality of the material world is felt in terms of heat and cold, misery and happiness. A Krishna conscious person is above duality because he does not hesitate to act in any way for the satisfaction of Krishna. Just see, when you take trouble to serve Prabhupada and Krishna, bodily, mental struggle you take against your wish. Tapasya means against your default mode. Now your present wish is to serve Prabhupada and Krishna. But there is a default mode, lazy fellow, all these kind of things, don't want to do, live happily. All this kind of philosophy, you know, concocted, karmi philosophy will be there. If the devotee is having the, you know, has determined to work hard for Prabhupada, take trouble for Prabhupada, sweat for Prabhupada, take tension for Prabhupada, take anxiety for Prabhupada, please understand that you are burning your Dukkha currency. If you don't take trouble for Prabhupada, if you don't take tension for Prabhupada, if you don't take anxiety for Prabhupada, Dukkha will come to you some other way, which is not service of Krishna. It may even stop service of Krishna, right? If you become very sick, then you will not be able to even do service, isn't it? Now I am talking about how devotees manage pleasure and pain.

---
#### austerity, devotional service, struggle
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lEvontw9tWU?t=927
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In Krishna consciousness after you have come, if you don't struggle for your spiritual, for service, if you don't struggle to control your senses, then you will be made to struggle for material existence."
**Summary:** The first secret to managing pleasure and pain is to take authorized austerities voluntarily, such as preaching Krishna consciousness, raising funds, and convincing others. If one doesn't struggle for spiritual advancement and sense control, they will be forced to struggle for material existence, which is ultimately useless.

In Kota, which is there as per law of karma, management of pleasure and pain. So secret number one is, take authorized austerities. One of the things that disturb ones most in life, for everybody is what? When problems come. Isn't it? Nobody likes problems. So better we take voluntarily problems. In this material world, to preach Krishna consciousness is very problematic. To raise funds is very problematic. To convince somebody is very problematic. You need patience, tolerance, be behind him, be behind him, be behind him. Be behind him. You have to put chest. To make one devotee, right? Or you take up a project, you have to be behind it. Every problems that the karmas have in building a project, the devotee will also have to face. So all these things when we do for Krishna, it has got very high value to make our devotional life peaceful. Therefore austerity should be taken. So many times in different classes I have told, in Krishna consciousness after you have come, if you don't struggle for your spiritual, for service, if you don't struggle to control your senses, then you will be made to struggle for material existence. Struggle will not be, that struggle will not be taken away. When you struggle for material existence, it is useless. It doesn't help you in any way. It's all temporary. When you struggle for Krishna consciousness, it is giving you eternal benefit. So, where do you, suppose you say, anyway you have to struggle. If you struggle here, you will get something for this life. If you struggle here, you will get something eternal for your soul. Which will you choose?

---
#### austerity, sadhana, spiritual life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lEvontw9tWU?t=1077
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We should, body and mind should have austerity every day. Something you don't like, you will have to do every day."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of daily austerity for the body and mind, focusing on activities that one's 'default system' resists, such as completing 16 rounds of chanting. He cautions against generalized austerities not aligned with pleasing Vishnu, distinguishing them from those performed for karmic benefits or pleasing other deities.

This is intelligent management. Therefore, we should struggle. We should, body and mind should have austerity every day. Something you don't like, you will have to do every day. Sadhana, something your default system does not want. Maybe something got trained and still there is some small percentage it doesn't want. Take that percentage and hit it. Go, do it. Do it. Maybe you don't want to be in one shot and finish your 16 rounds. After few rounds your mind will say something else. No. Take austerity. Do it. Finish it. There are hundreds of ways in which in our personal life, in devotees personal life, in Krishna consciousness movement that Prabhupada has given, there are hundreds of ways of picking up austerities, bona fide austerity. We are not generalizing the principle of austerity. Okay, I will do one thing. Today, you know, I will, after morning program, you know, suddenly you will see one brahmachari in the middle of the brahmachari ashram, standing on one leg. Prabhupada, today I have decided, 10 hours I am going to stand like this. Why? No, tapasya. So that I can burn off my, you know, papa bijas. No, we are talking about tapasya which will give us eternal benefit. Oh, I have to do tapasya Prabhu, you told me. No, Mondays I will fast and Fridays I will fast. No. These are all good for karmas. Yes, it helps them, encourages them. Every kind of austerity. Austerity for pleasing Shiva, austerity for pleasing Ganesha. Monday for Shiva I will fast. Thursday I will fast for Ganesha. All this they have. Let them do. But we want to do those austerities which are pleasing to Vishnu, to the Lord. So we have to be very careful in simply, that is another extreme.

**Content Type:** philosophy

**Key Quote:** "Bhakti Siddhanta Saraswati Thakur used to blast any disciple if he goes to Radha Kunda and sits. He said, you are not qualified even to dip in Radha Kunda."
**Summary:** The speaker cautions against extreme austerity, emphasizing the importance of following the guidance of Srila Prabhupada. He uses the example of chanting 64 rounds at Radha Kunda, explaining that while it seems like a good austerity, it may not be appropriate for everyone and that Bhakti Siddhanta Saraswati Thakur discouraged unqualified disciples from even dipping in Radha Kunda.

Some devotees go into this whole austerity mode and all this kind of things. So there are bona fide austerities. Within even those bona fide austerities, Srila Prabhupada has given us, even within these bona fide austerities, again a set of austerities. Why he has given a set of austerities? Just suppose somebody says, oh I want to go to Radha Kunda and chant every day 64 rounds. See, it is a, how can you say no, it is not a good austerity. Now you have to again see what our bona fide, our spiritual master has told us. Stand 16 rounds nicely, probably another 10 lives. Then one day you will go to Radha Kunda. There is a stage for everything. Bhakti Siddhanta Saraswati Thakur used to blast any disciple if he goes to Radha Kunda and sits. He said, you are not qualified even to dip in Radha Kunda. Even Prabhupada, I think only once or something he went dip, but afterwards he stopped. Because all the foreign disciples started swimming and everything in Radha Kunda. So Prabhupada stopped that. That is why as a principle we are not encouraging everybody, oh go to. Now ISKCON has become big, lot of congregation, it has become a fad. Go, Radha Kunda, dip, dip, dip, Radha Kunda. It is a very sanctified place. Radha Kunda is your, you know, you can't experience Radha Kunda unless you are a very elevated devotee. It is not that gross eyes what you see that Kunda. You can make lot of offences. Unless you are free from, you know, lust, you cannot sit for bhajan like that. Niranthara Bhajan, continuing to chant 64 times. Or like Haridas Thakur. So austerity also is given to us by Srila Prabhupada.

#### austerity, chanting, holy name
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lEvontw9tWU?t=1347
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In fact, the greatest tapasya that one can do in this age is to hear the holy name of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that the austerity given by Srila Prabhupada, such as chanting 16 rounds nicely, is the path to qualification. He highlights that the greatest tapasya in this age is hearing the holy name of the Lord, which is easier than other austerities but yields incomparable results.

And that austerity, for instance, when you chant your 16 rounds nicely, hearing, without offences, then you get qualified to chant your 17th round. Otherwise put all your efforts in chanting 16 rounds nicely. Austerity. Plenty of austerity. Whole life austerity is there. In fact, the greatest tapasya that one can do in this age is to hear the holy name of the Lord. See, tapasya can be known, can be understood by two means. One is how difficult it is. Another is what is the result of it. See, don't mistake, always tapasya means pain. It's not so. There are authorized tapasyas. Each tapasya has a pain and a result. If you take chanting Hare Krishna Mahamantra, the hearing of Hare Krishna Mahamantra, definitely is not so difficult as standing on one leg for 10 hours. Right? Much easier. But the result of that one hearing of Hare Krishna cannot be compared to 100 years of standing on one leg. So that's what you have to understand. Don't think that tapasya only means, you know, tapasya means authorized tapasas. Authorized tapasas, authorized austerity, because there is some, definitely some tapas is there in hearing because mind goes here and there. But it is not, it is, it is, it is easy, easier than many other tapasyas. But the value of this tapasya is something which is, what is revealed in the scriptures. Discounted tapasya for Kali Yuga. Just simply you hear then you get, you know, the results of so many Ashwamedha Yajna, Shastras. Even then you cannot equal to this tapasya of hearing this Hare Krishna Mahamantra. So, we have to be very careful in understanding purport of Prabhupada, understanding these verses.

#### pleasure, pain, free will

He emphasizes that while the amount of pleasure is fixed, individuals have the free will to choose what they take pleasure in, making this a crucial aspect of managing pleasure and pain.

What about Sukha? That also is there in the purse everyday. It is delivered in the plate, Sukha and Dukha, everyday. Just like Dukha comes without asking, Sukha also comes without asking. Now again, don't think that Sukha means that specific Sukha. Then you have no free will. No. Suppose a specific Sukha is pushed upon you, then you have no free will at all. So there is a Sukha. Now whether you want to, what do you want to do with this Sukha? This is another very important clue, how to manage our pleasure and pain. Now pain management, I have told. Pleasure management, I will tell. How pleasure management? You can decide what to take pleasure in, you have got a free will. Just like you have got pleasure currency, either you can buy it, you know, tomatoes or orange or apple or what you want to buy, you can buy. Currency is fixed and you have to spend it by evening. It's a force on the body. It's a Vega in the body. Pleasure Vega is coming in the body, in the mind. Pleasure Vega is coming. You have to spend it. And I told you, to spend it, you can decide what to take pleasure in. What is it? You have got the freedom to decide what to take pleasure in.

Chanting, devotion service, dancing, taking prashadam. Take pleasure in every work that you do for Prabhupada and Krishna. Everyday your pleasure management of your body-mind system is taken care of. But you have to take pleasure. That is very important. All of you can research. Go into Prabhupada folio and see. Taking pleasure. About 300 times Prabhupada has written. Taking pleasure. That means it is your free will. You should take pleasure in Brahmanical life. You should take pleasure in sadhana. You should take pleasure in your work. See somebody is, let us say, the person is thinking, oh this is very, you know, I have to work, I have to work, I have to work, I have to work. Then he is not, that days all pleasure currency is piling up. By evening, by night he is frustrated. Because his pleasure currency is not burnt. These are all big tricks of the mind. You can take pleasure in a painful thing. Painful for the body, painful for the mind, but pleasure for the soul. So you can take pleasure, just like tapasya also you can take pleasure. So taking pleasure in chanting, taking pleasure in everything that you do, taking pleasure in the service, because nearly 8-10 hours, 7-8 hours you are spending in service. #### pleasure, pain, karma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lEvontw9tWU?t=1946
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Anything before it becomes pleasurable, you have to take pleasure. This is another rule."
**Summary:** The speaker explains how to manage pleasure and pain daily to avoid being bound by karma. He distinguishes between bona fide and sinful expressions of pleasure and pain, emphasizing the importance of taking pleasure in authorized activities like honoring prasadam and kirtan. He clarifies that taking pleasure is a conscious decision, a combination of external stimuli and one's own pleasure potency.

This way day to day we can manage the pleasure and pain quota. At the same time we don't get bound by karma. In the same time, if we are freed from sinful expressions of pain and pleasure. There are bona fide expressions of pain and pleasure. Taking prasadam is bona fide pleasure, expression of pleasure. Right? Eating nonsense, it is not a bona fide pleasure. So there are bona fide expressions of pleasure and pain, pleasure and pain in our Krishna consciousness life, that Prabhupada has given us. And you take pleasure and do it. Take pleasure in honoring prasadam. Take pleasure in kirtan. Somebody may say, no, take pleasure is all artificial. How can I take pleasure? It should become pleasurable. Anything before it becomes pleasurable, you have to take pleasure. This is another rule. Because pleasure is a combination of something from outside and your decision to enjoy. Your pleasure potency. Each of us have a tiny pleasure potency. We are sample of God. If you don't decide to take pleasure, even outside thing if it is pleasurable also, you will not feel pleasure. Both have to mix together. Hydrogen is gas. Oxygen is gas. Mix hydrogen and oxygen, you get something which is water, liquid. The property of water is neither of hydrogen or oxygen. Yes? In the same way, if you take my contribution of giving pleasure potency is hydrogen. Sense object is oxygen. Only when they mix together, you get what is sense gratification. Sense gratification is a combination of sense enjoyer and sense object. Is it not?

#### pleasure, pain, mind
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lEvontw9tWU?t=2143
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So a dheera is one whose consciousness is not pulled up and down."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by explaining that every enjoyment is learned, and the free will to take pleasure plays a significant role in the final pleasure experience. He advises taking pleasure in bona fide expressions of pleasure authorized in Krishna consciousness. Managing pleasure and pain in this way leads to a peaceful mind and minimizes the pull of dualities, allowing one to become a 'dheera' (sober) whose consciousness is not swayed by pleasure and pain.

So therefore, you have trained yourself, you have conditioned yourself to enjoy something. It simply means that you have already taught your mind to take pleasure in it. That is why now, you are not consciously taking pleasure. But you have trained your mind to take pleasure in it. It is already set inside. If you have not taken pleasure in it, you will not find, you will not, you know, you have to teach yourself fresh how to enjoy. Every enjoyment is not automatic. Every enjoyment that we have with the sense object, it is all learnt. From childhood slowly it is learnt. This is pleasurable. Okay, I will take pleasure in it. You see such a big role, this taking pleasure, free will to take pleasure. In the actual final pleasure experience, your taking pleasure is such an important factor. So we have to take pleasure in bona fide expressions of pleasure, which are many in Krishna consciousness movement, authorized for us as a Brahmachari, as a Grihastha, whatever, it is plenty. So this way, if we manage our expressions of pleasure and pain, Kota, which is in our destiny, as long as we have this body, that force is there. So as long as, if we can manage that in this, using these two formulas, then our mind will be peaceful. Mind cannot become sober, when there is pain and pleasure, these two things pull the mind apart, these dualities. You cannot be a sober person, you cannot be a dheera, dheera means sober, adheera means pleasure pulls, pain pulls, your consciousness, pleasure and pain may be there, but your consciousness is pulled up and down. So a dheera is one whose consciousness is not pulled up and down. So that your consciousness is not pulled up and down, you have to minimize the pull of pleasure and pain, by management of your pleasure and pain, Kota. So that the mind is peaceful, and it can execute positive devotional service.

Hare Krishna. Any questions? Yes. Hare Krishna. Yes, it manages, because two things you are already doing, taking pleasure in chanting, and while taking pleasure in chanting, while you are taking pleasure in chanting, you are doing austerity with your mind and body, which doesn't take pleasure. It wants freedom. You tell sit in one place and chant, it doesn't want. Mind and body is, by default goes in another direction. So you learn how to take pleasure, with your soul, and you are taking pain with your body and mind, lot of your Kota is burnt out. Whole day you can remain a Dheera. See, we have, unless purity comes, full purity comes, we cannot be a Dheera always. That is a pure devotee situation. But if you do what a pure devotee has told, on a day to day basis we can be a Dheera. Today you can be a Dheera, if you have done this chanting nicely. Tomorrow if you don't do chanting, you will be a Dheera. Whereas a pure devotee, who is fixed in Krishna consciousness, he is always Dheera.

So today we will talk about the glories of the holy name of the Lord, Krishna, Lord Krishna, the Sarvakarnakara, means the cause of all causes. He is the supreme original cause of the entire existence, the entire existence. He is working as per the scheme of the Supreme Lord, Lord Krishna. The purpose of material existence is Him. Everything, including us, this material energy, everything that we see here and beyond, matter, the Vaikunthaloka, everything is emanating from Him as the supreme cause. So this is this consciousness that Krishna, everything is Krishna, and or His energies, display of His energies, is what is the consciousness called Krishna consciousness. We are also tiny persons, part and parcel of God, as tiny persons, all the characteristics of a tiny person, if you study, that he can think, he can feel, he can will, he can have ego, he can enjoy, he can live, he can live, he can have ego, he can enjoy, he can love.

He can love, he can possess. If you take all these characteristics, just like a drop of ocean is salty, as much as the quality of the entire ocean is, quality quantity is less, in the same way, we are able to see that in this body there is a soul, there is a person who is having all these personhood features, and the body does not have these personhood features. This body does not have thinking, feeling, willing, egoing, does not have this. It's all coming from the resident of this body, body is a dead matter. The body is no different from any machine, but in illusion we think we are the body. So Krishna, the supreme lord, is also a person, person of, we are persons of tiny consciousness, he is a person of infinite consciousness. We are persons of different limited control of limited energies around us, like this body and bodily control over things outside the body, and this control is only temporary. If you extend that characteristic to Krishna, Krishna is the supreme proprietor of everything in existence, being the cause of all causes, he is in eternal control of everything in existence, unlike us. We are in control of things according to karma, little control today, somebody is rich today, someone will be poor tomorrow, someone will possess something today, tomorrow he will lose that possession, but Krishna is the eternal possessor of all existence being the supreme lord.

And therefore, Nama worship. Rama worship. Nama worship means you are worshipping Shuddha Nama only. But in the beginning you worship Shuddha Nama from a distance by worshipping Shakti Avaishanama. By worshipping Shakti Avaishanama, you get association of the Shakti Avaishanama. When you get the association of Shakti Avaishanama, you get purified. Satya energy, Chitya energy, Ananda energies are energies that will purify our heart. So why Japa, the spiritual master, gives us the holy name in the form of the Shakti Avaishanama to start with. And when you are qualified, what it means?

---
#### Nama Avatara, mercy, attention
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9PPovPGnK2g?t=990
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The speaker states that the Nama Avatara in Kali Yuga is the Hare Krishna mantra, which has spread mercy worldwide, and emphasizes that when our attention touches the sound of the mantra, it becomes purified.

And we all are aware that this is proved by the fact that today this Nama Avatara He is... He is giving out the mercy, has already given out the mercy all over the world to souls from different religion, different countries, everywhere. They all have received this Nama spread all over the world in this particular form. It's Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. So, this Nama Avatara is like one sun, Surya of Satchidananda. Just like one sun reflects on so many water bodies on this earth, but the sun is one. In the same way, one Nama Prabhu is reflecting on millions of tongues who vibrate and infuses Satya, Chitta and Ananda energies into that sound. So that, that sound when we hear, consciously when we hear, when our attention touches that sound, our attention becomes purified. Right now, our attention is influenced by Raja Guna, Tama Guna and Satya Guna. When we say we are influenced by three gunas, what does it mean? It means my attention is influenced by these three gunas. Therefore, this attention needs to be purified. This attention touches the Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. You have to electrify the attention. Satchidananda eyes the attention. And how can you do that? By constant attention touch of the sound incarnation of the Lord. This is a secret. That's all, as simple as that.

#### attention, chanting, purpose
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9PPovPGnK2g?t=1129
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In vain means you are not utilizing the purpose for which the Lord has appeared."
**Summary:** The speaker stresses the importance of continuous, steady, and undiluted attention while chanting, explaining that slow progress is often due to a lack of conscious connection with the Holy Name and that inattentive chanting is in vain.

How long you keep the attention touch? How continuously you keep the attention touch? How steadily you keep the attention touch? How undiluted from any other task you keep the attention touch? That much rapid, immediate, rapid, immediate progress is made by association of the Holy Name. One of the reasons why we see very slow effect on our consciousness when we chant Hare Krishna, is because our consciousness does not touch the Holy Name. Consciousness touches, our consciousness touches through attentive hearing. So, the ocean of Satchidananda, Surya of Satchidananda, non-different from Krishna, Krishna himself has taken avatar to save us from this material world, by giving us association. And we will be absolutely foolish, if we do not take this avatar that appears on our tongue, into our heart, through the process of attentive hearing. How you start with chanting one mantra, then you chant the next mantra, then you chant the next mantra, and if your attention is going away to some other thinking, then you are calling the Holy Name of the Lord in vain. In vain means you are not utilizing the purpose for which the Lord has appeared.

#### respect, Vaishnava etiquette, super-soul
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/N8YK8REL7t0?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Offering respect to his devotee is more valuable than offering respect to him."
**Summary:** This segment explains that offering respect to a Vaishnava is offering respect to Vishnu, as Vaishnavas do not identify with the body but see it as a temple of Vishnu, and that offering respect to a devotee is more valuable than offering respect to the Lord himself.

It may be argued that since Daksha was the father-in-law of Lord Shiva, it was certainly the duty of Lord Shiva to offer him respect. In answer to that argument, it is explained here that when a learned person stands up or offers obeisances in welcome, he offers respect to the super-soul who is sitting within everyone's heart. It is seen, therefore, among Vaishnavas that even when a disciple offers obeisance to a spiritual master, the spiritual master immediately returns the obeisances because they are mutually offered not to the body but to the super-soul. Therefore, the spiritual master also offers respect to the super-soul situated in the body of the disciple. The Lord says in Srimad Bhagavatam that offering respect to his devotee is more valuable than offering respect to him. Devotees do not identify with the body. So, offering respect to a Vaishnava means offering respect to Vishnu. It is stated there also that as a matter of etiquette, as soon as one sees a Vaishnava, one must immediately offer him respect, indicating the super-soul sitting within. A Vaishnava sees the body of a temple of Vishnu. Since Lord Shiva already has offered respect to the super-soul in Krishna consciousness, offering respect to Daksha, who has identified with the body and was already performed, there is no need to offer respect to his body, for that is not directed by any Vedic injunction. Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta Purport.

---
#### social convention, transcendental knowledge, respect
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/N8YK8REL7t0?t=107
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Those who are elevated to transcendental platform being intelligent, offer such respects to super souls."
**Summary:** Lord Shiva explains the difference between social conventions of respect and the transcendental understanding of giving respect, emphasizing that transcendentalists base their actions on pure knowledge, offering respect to the super-soul within the body rather than to the person who identifies with the body.

So, a very important principle, Srimad Bhagavatamin Θahahaha D sympathy, kenasṁ davanti één ye que Caitanya who does not believe Therefrom and contemplate only the He is being explained by Lord Shiva to his young wife, Sati. Basically, Lord Shiva is saying that I never disrespected Dakshya. There is a social convention, which is first line. Certainly, friends and relatives offer mutual greetings by standing up, welcoming one another and offering obeisances. This is Vivaharik social convention. Next he says, Paramarthi, those who are elevated to transcendental platform being intelligent, offer such respects to super souls. Those who are sitting within the body, not to the person who identifies with the body. So, the two different perspectives. And Lord Shiva is saying that those who understand the soul, the super soul and the body, which is the body of, which the soul identifies with, the three things. There is nothing much to explain about the social convention. There is not much philosophy in it. Socially somebody is more respectable, less respectable, no doubt. Father-in-law should be respected more, must be respected by the son-in-law. These are social conventions. So, there is nothing much to explain about that.

#### transcendentalism, knowledge, false ego
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/N8YK8REL7t0?t=228
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When we give respect to the super soul, the pure soul is included in it."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the transcendental understanding of respect, stating that it is based on pure knowledge and directed towards the super-soul within the body, not the false ego, and that giving respect to the super-soul includes respect for the pure soul.

When it comes to the transcendental understanding of giving respect, it is, everything that the transcendentalist does, is based on knowledge, pure knowledge. So, externally the act may be the same as, the other act, same as the social act. But, the way the transcendentalists do the act, why they do the act, is different. Lord Shiva is explaining here that when a person who is enlightened about the things as they are, then they, when they, they give respects to the super soul within the body and not to the false ego. When we give respect to the super soul, the pure soul is included in it. The pure soul or the awakened soul or the pure soul in a body which is driven by false ego, is practically sleeping. So, one gives obeisances to the super soul. There is nothing, the soul who is accompanied with the super soul or the super soul is accompanied by the soul, either way, they are never separated. False ego is separated, soul is separated, in the mind of an enlightened person. And he gives, he offers his obeisances to the super soul and the soul. And the soul is not the false ego because they have identified with the body. That here, Lord Shiva clearly says, not to the person who identifies with the body.

---
#### social convention, Krishna consciousness, enlightenment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/N8YK8REL7t0?t=356
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Paying obeisances, physically, or paying obeisances in the mind, is no difference as far as the internal consciousness of the enlightened person is concerned."
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies that paying respects to someone identified with the material body is a social convention, not of interest to devotees acting from a transcendental platform, and that even if a self-realized person acts within social conventions, their actions are rooted in knowledge, with obeisances paid to the super-soul.

So, is it that we never pay obeisances, we never pay respects to the soul? Do we pay respects only to the super soul? No, no, no, no, no. Anybody who is, person who is identified with the, with the material body, to that person is, if you pay obeisances, then that is social. False ego. Devotees are not interested in that. Interested means they act out of, out of transcendental platform. That doesn't mean they don't act, they don't, they are not affected. Even if, let us say, somebody is, because Krishna says, even self-realized person, they act within the conventions of this material world. Just to set example. But when they do that also, even if Lord Shiva had paid obeisances to, to Daksha, not that he should not have, should never, even if he had paid obeisances to Daksha, it will be in knowledge. Just to, for social convention. He knows it just for social convention. Because he has to set an example. Here it is not that Lord Shiva, last purport became very clear, that it is not that Lord Shiva intentionally said, Oh, I am greater than him, so I should not pay obeisances. He did not mean that. By the time he observed Daksha entering the sacrificial arena, already he lost his temper, and Daksha started attacking. He did not notice Daksha entering the sacrificial arena. That is explained in the last verse. So, paying obeisances, physically, or paying obeisances in the mind, is no difference as far as the internal consciousness of the enlightened person is concerned.

#### enlightenment, social convention, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/N8YK8REL7t0?t=478
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He acts in each situation, in service of the Supreme Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that an enlightened person's internal consciousness remains focused on the soul and super-soul, regardless of external actions dictated by social conventions, and that they act in service of the Supreme Lord in every situation.

And whether externally he should pay obeisances, or not, whether, whatever internally he is paying respects to the soul and the super soul, and to the false ego, if for social conventions. And to the false ego for social conventions. Either way, he does not act in ignorance. He acts in each situation, in service of the Supreme Lord. If out of social convention, if he has to do that, he will do it. But he knows that it is only out of social convention. The soul is identifying with the body. Therefore, you know, this is only a social convention. But for those who do not identify with their bodies, they are as good as the super soul in their quality. They are the same quality as the super soul. So, it is a great occasion to offer respects to that body, body itself. Because, the body itself is serving the soul and the super soul. So, it becomes a temple of God, an active temple of God, you can say. Everybody's body is a temple of God, because super soul is seated in their heart. But those who are acting as a servant of the super soul, engaged in devotion service of the Lord, disidentifying with their body, but utilizing their body as an instrument to serve the Lord, to such persons, you are actually paying obeisance to the super soul and the person together.

#### spiritual master, disciple, false ego
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/N8YK8REL7t0?t=593
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We are all servants of Krishna and we are serving the... I am your servant as a spiritual master."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the dynamic between spiritual master and disciple, explaining that the spiritual master sometimes pays obeisances back to the disciple to teach the principle that all are servants of Krishna, but this is not always appropriate with neophyte disciples due to the risk of inflating their false ego.

And here, Prabhupada is saying that the spiritual master also sometimes pays obeisances back to the disciple. Various conceptions could, two, three different conceptions could be there in the spiritual master's mind in doing that. One is to teach the principle to the disciple that we are all servants of Krishna and we are serving the... I am your servant as a spiritual master. You are servant of Krishna as my disciple. I am servant of Krishna as a spiritual master. Both have their role to please the Supreme Lord in that spirit. But normally the disciples are not very enlightened to understand these things. So, even this transcendental message to convey, if the spiritual master pays obeisances to the disciple, then the disciple pays obeisances to the spiritual master, it can happen, but it does not have to happen, because the spiritual master also has a duty. If there is a neophyte, if he does not, if he is advanced, yes, very nice. But the principle is understood. If you, if the spiritual master pays obeisances to, back to this disciple, a neophyte disciple, now comes to social convention because he is in the platform of false ego. So, the obeisances will be taken by the false ego and then it will become, he makes an offense because he thinks that spiritual master is respecting him. I am such a great disciple, I am being respected by my spiritual master. So, immediately he makes an offense to the spiritual master. So, the spiritual master has to act in a very, in a way in which he serves his function as a guru. Therefore, he does not pay back obeisances to the false ego. And through social convention, the disciple only pays obeisances to the spiritual master and the spiritual, the false ego will become, more, false ego will decrease or the false ego will, will purify. By, false ego means what? Lauding, lauding. So, by paying obeisances, the false ego will, the lauding false ego will decrease. And at least for that moment, he will act, I am servant of somebody, I am subordinate to somebody, I have to respect somebody. So, we have to understand this principle beautifully explained by Lord Shiva in this verse.

Now, in other cases, in other situations, even in, even in the case of a neophyte disciple, the spiritual master, ok, take spiritual master or even take a senior, a senior devotee or a spiritual master, in his heart he pays obeisances to the super soul. At no cost, any living entity, even spiritual master, receives somebody's respects without offering it to the Lord. Any respects that is received, if it ends with you, then, if it has to end with you, it has, that respect has to be eaten by your false ego, consumed, consumed by your false ego.

#### humility, service, false ego
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/N8YK8REL7t0?t=861
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The basic principle is to learn how to be the servant of the servant of the servant."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of humility and service in spiritual life, explaining that a senior devotee should not feel hurt if not respected by junior devotees, but rather be concerned about teaching them the proper way to offer respect, and that the ultimate goal is to be the servant of the servant of the servant.

So, anybody who is interested in spiritual life, the basic principle is to learn how to be the servant of the servant of the servant. If within the heart, if anybody pays respects and if a senior just receives it, thinking ok, he is, he is, he is, he ought to give me respect, then he is on the bodily platform, that means his false ego is receiving respect. If somebody is not respecting, then he gets hurt. Why I am senior devotee, why I have not been respected by the junior devotees? He gets hurt, he should never get hurt, but he should be concerned, why is that junior devotee not learning what he has to learn to respect the senior devotees? Why is he not learning? So, in that, in that aspect, he has a duty, because that is the purpose of somebody giving respect and taking respect in the heart and offering it to the Supreme Lord. Because the neophyte devotee, neophyte devotee, he cannot, he is not, he is not understanding, he cannot give respect to the super soul and the soul, all those things, because he is in a social platform, so he pays obeisances, he gives respect, it is the responsibility of the senior to take it and offer it to the super soul. Then it becomes, then the soul is receiving that respect for, on behalf of the super soul. So to, it takes a long time of spiritual journey of practice of Bhakti Yoga, to see every living entity as this, you know, combined, inseparable combination of soul and super soul. It takes time. We have to come out of our bodily concept, we have to take everybody out of their bodily concept in our mind.

#### bodily concept, super soul, false ego
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/N8YK8REL7t0?t=991
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Enlightened person does not give respect to false egos."
**Summary:** The speaker continues to emphasize the importance of transcending the bodily concept of others, focusing on the super-soul within, and that enlightened individuals do not offer respect to false egos, even when following social conventions.

They may be in bodily concept, but we should take everybody out of the bodily concept. We should not see others as their bodies. So if the principle is understood, basically what is this principle? In one line, these paying obeisances, as for a spiritualist are concerned, it is not for false ego. At the same time, we are in this material world. So, these conventions are also required. If at all by, we follow the conventions, it is sake of convention only. There also we only, we follow the convention, but actually in our heart, we are paying obeisances, we are giving respect to the super soul. Because Lord is everywhere, I can give respect anywhere. No bar. So enlightened person does not give respect to false egos. The person with false ego may be thinking out of, he does not know that out of convention, a person is paying obeisances to him. An enlightened person is paying obeisances to him. Maybe just because he is father-in-law or whatever it is. He may not understand. He will take, okay for my false ego is given. But he is enlightened person, even if it is father-in-law, he is giving it to the super soul. All this bodily identification, father-in-law, son-in-law, all those things are purely on the social convention. Enlightened people do it for the super soul. Super soul, soul, a pure soul, a pure enlightened soul is not different from the super soul. So it is for the combination. Because question may ask, so you do not respect the soul at all? Only it is for the super soul we pay obeisances? No. If the soul is spiritual, if the soul is enlightened, soul has attained the quality of the super soul, then when you are paying obeisances to the super soul, you are already paying obeisances.

#### soul, awakening, false ego
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/N8YK8REL7t0?t=1391
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "To the degree to which the soul is awakened, the soul will receive the, spiritually."
**Summary:** The speaker answers the question by explaining that the extra respect given to a devotee is due to the degree to which their soul is awakened, using the analogy of a sleeping person to illustrate that respect is exchanged to the degree of wakefulness.

What is, in a devotee, more of his soul is manifest. Manifest means, more of it is awakened. If there is awakened, if there is awakening, then there is, for instance, a person is sleeping. So you, you pay obeisance to him. What is, you, he is sleeping, no, might as well don't pay. So if somebody is little awake, okay, there is exchange of respect. So it, to the degree to which the soul is awakened, the soul will receive the, spiritually, the degree to which he is awakened and the degree to which there is a combination. There is a false ego most of the time and there is, sometimes there is manifestation of, of one's real ego. Or it's a mixture, just like you wake up from the dream. Right. You are in a mixed state. Your half mind is in the dream, half mind, half mind is in the reality. So it takes some time for you to come out of the dream world and go into the reality. So we are all like that. We are all half awake, quarter awake, some percentage we are awake. We are not fully awake. So because we are not fully awake, when half awake, suddenly some sense gratification comes, we become fully sleepy and full false ego takes over. So we are oscillating, our state of consciousness is oscillating between sleeping state and wakeful state.

#### devotional service, false ego, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/N8YK8REL7t0?t=1520
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Devotion service is never independent work."
**Summary:** The speaker continues by explaining that when one is spiritually more awake, they can perform service free from false ego, and that doing service with false ego or for social recognition leads to complications and disturbances in devotional service, emphasizing that devotion service should be for the satisfaction of Prabhupada and Krishna directly, not for societal approval.

When you are in good association, when you are fired up, when you do your good chanting and all these things, then when you associate with the holy name, nice kirtan and all those things, after that, you actually, you are spiritually more awake. And when you are spiritually more awake, we will be able to do our service free from false ego. When we do our service with false ego, then it has all complications are there. So many disturbances even in our devotional service comes because of doing service with false ego or doing service for social rasa, social recognition, criterion for or motivation for service is not independent motivation between you, your thoughts, authorities, Prabhupada and Krishna. It is not that you have your own motivation of what the devotees will think, what the society will think, how much result I brought, how much result I did not bring. Criterion, we become servant of the society and not servant of Prabhupada and Krishna. When you become servant of the society, then when you become depressed, then you get unnecessarily stress in your service, then there is, it affects one's spiritual life. If one is doing service for the satisfaction of Prabhupada and Krishna directly, that's my criterion, then, even if results don't come, then Krishna says, you are spiritually situated, you see it is at, Krishna says, don't be attached to results. You work in a Krishna conscious way, Madashraya, depend upon me, take shelter of me and work, don't independently work. Not that I independently work and go and give something. Devotion service is never independent work. Devotion service is, just like taking something, just like Ganges water you take and pour back into the Ganges. Devotion, nature of devotion service is that, not that somewhere you bring water which belongs to somebody and you give it to Krishna. Everything, my body belongs to him, but I am taking it and I am offering it. So depending on him, where I will get water, you depend upon him. Results depend upon him and then serve Krishna. Krishna wants that love of offering. Krishna wants, Bhavagrahi Janardana, that is what he wants. Result, he is the one who is doing, he is the one who is giving. The service, the work that leads to the result is your job.

#### karma, detachment, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/N8YK8REL7t0?t=1677
**Verse References:** BG 2.47
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Results are always in the domain of the Supersoul."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the result is Krishna's job, referencing the Bhagavad Gita's teachings on how to work, emphasizing the importance of taking pressure and stress in the work itself, without becoming obsessed with the outcome, and that the super-soul is the awarder of all results.

The result is his job. That is the first thing that Krishna teaches in Bhagavad Gita. How to work, the art of work. Take pressures, take stress. In the work itself of the day, the day's work, you take pressure. What I have to do, what I have to do. Don't give a little space for Maya to come. Don't sit the whole day and think, you know, get obsessed with the result. Result will come or not. Result will come or not. And none of your business will come or not will be decided by Krishna. That's what he is telling, Krishna is telling in the Bhagavad Gita. Super soul is the awarder of all results. So for devotees also, he is the awarder of. That's his domain. Result is his domain. I have to intellectually think of the result, not emotionally, getting it, not getting it. You have to intellectually think about the result because you will, it will give you direction how to do work. So we do think about the result of the activity, but we do not emotionalize the results, outcome of the results. We want results, we will be happy if we can offer to Krishna. But the outcome, you don't emotionalize. I should succeed. If I succeed, if I don't succeed, it is a failure of devotional service. If you have sincerely put your work towards the result, then it is already success for devotee, devotional service. That is, you can be succeed only, you can succeed only in your domain.

**Summary:** The speaker explains that results are always in the domain of the Supersoul, who is the sanctioner of desires for every living entity, including devotees, whose desires are for Krishna.

Results are always in the domain of the Supersoul. For every living entity, Karmi, everybody, he is a sanctioner of the desires. Devotees have desires for Krishna, that is the only difference. But desires for Krishna also, Krishna only has to satisfy in this material world, it is like that. Because he is the Parameshwara. Any other question? The second question is, you told that Prabhupada has also Jeevatma and Paramatma. So we have to offer respect to both. So now, the question is, I am seeing him as a manifestation of Paramatma. Tatva wise, okay, it is Jeevatma and Paramatma. But because all the knowledge, what all experience he has given about Krishna, or devotional service, is actually coming from him. So I am seeing him as directly the instructions or means from Supersoul.

#### spiritual master, super soul, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/N8YK8REL7t0?t=1852
**Verse References:** SB 1.2.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You have to give devotional credit for his transparent via media."
**Summary:** The speaker addresses the question of seeing Prabhupada as a manifestation of Paramatma, explaining that while Prabhupada is tattva-wise Jeevatma and Paramatma, one must also recognize and give devotional credit for his transparent via media, his service to the Supreme Lord, and his role in directing us in our service.

So is that attitude correct? Directly, last line? No, means whatever, I am seeing, I am not seeing that duality in Prabhupada, like Jeevatma, Prabhupada is a Jeeva, or I am just seeing him as a, okay, Supersoul is acting through him completely. So is that attitude right? Tatva wise, there is Jeevatma and Paramatma. That is true. But I am asking, that I am, whatever experience… Yeah, but the Jeevatma has got, you have to see Jeevatma with his devotion, with his spirit, to serve the Supersoul. So you are getting that instruction from the Supersoul. He is a transparent via media. So you have to give him devotional credit for his transparent via media. You have to give devotional credit for his service to the Supreme Lord. And therefore, he is serving us. So where do we get such souls who will be transparent and will act as a medium? Because I cannot receive instruction from the Supersoul, who, by my spiritual Master's mercy, I get directed in my service. Asya Prasadat, Bhagavata Prasadat, so by his mercy I am able to get direction of the Supersoul who is in my heart. So therefore, we have to see the truth is to be seen, no? It is not simply, you know, transparent via media means it's, it's a language used, but it's a personal transparent via media. It's not an impersonal transparent via media like a glass. It's a, it's a personal via media, personal via media means only his wishes, it goes to him and he's the soul enjoyer. Supersoul is a soul enjoyer. But that spiritual Master is a person. So therefore, we start appreciating the qualities of that person, qualities of Supersoul. Then we become qualities of the spiritual Masters. Then we become attached to the spiritual Master. So all these things are very much part of the devotee's life. He has to become attached to the spiritual Master, grateful to the spiritual Master, all these things

**Key Quote:** "Food is when it is offered to the Lord, it is spiritualized food; it is matter, but it becomes spiritualized."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains how food offered to Krishna becomes sanctified and spiritualized, having a curative and transcendental effect.

We distribute food that is sanctified. It's not about even material purity when it becomes sanctified by offering to the Lord with gratitude to the Lord for providing this for existence. That makes it super sanctified, spiritually sanctified. It has got a very curative, healing, transcendental effect for the good of the person who honors sanctified food, and that is what we are distributing. How is it sanctified? The Lord sanctifies it transcendentally. He sees the... the Lord eats our... it's a mystical process. Every lot is not offered. The Lord knows thousands of people are coming every day. Mass distribution of prasadam means, you know, it's a different kind of, you know, leela of the Lord, you see. Just like shaktivesha nama, Lord shakti enters in it. Similarly, food is when it is offered to the Lord, it is spiritualized food; it is matter, but it becomes spiritualized.

## HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

#### spiritual master, mercy, parampara
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Dy9VGtyDXi8?t=41
**Verse References:** BG 4.34
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Unless one is fortunate enough to have the dust of the lotus feet of a Mahatma on one's head, there is no possibility of perfection in spiritual life."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of the spiritual master and the parampara system in achieving spiritual perfection, highlighting the necessity of receiving the mercy of a great soul.

Translation on purpose by His Divine Grace. It is my good fortune that I have been able to see you, for you cannot easily be seen by persons who are not subdued in mind or control the senses. I am all the more fortunate to have touched my head to the blessed dust of your feet. Purport by Srila Prabhupada: The perfection of transcendental life can be achieved simply by touching the holy dust of the feet of a holy man. In the Bhagavatam, it is said, which means to be blessed by the holy dust of the lotus feet of a Mahatma, or great devotee, as stated in Bhagavad Gita, those who are great souls are under the spell of spiritual energy, and their symptom is that they fully engage in Krishna consciousness for the service of the Lord. Therefore, unless one is fortunate enough to have the dust of the lotus feet of a Mahatma on one's head, there is no possibility of perfection in spiritual life. The parampara system of disciplic succession is very important as a means of spiritual success. One becomes a Mahatma by the grace of his Mahatma spiritual master. If one takes shelter of the lotus feet of a great soul, there is every possibility of one's also becoming a great soul. When Maharaj Rahugana asked Jadabharata about his wonderful achievements of spiritual success, he replied to the king that spiritual success is not possible simply by following the rituals of religion, or simply by converting oneself into a sanyasi, or offering sacrifices as recommended in the scriptures. These methods are undoubtedly helpful for spiritual realization, but the real effect is brought about by the grace of a Mahatma. In Vishvanatha Chakravarti Thakura's eight stanzas of prayers to the spiritual master, it is clearly stated that simply by satisfying the spiritual master, one can achieve the supreme success in life. In spite of executing all ritualistic performances, if one cannot satisfy the spiritual master, one has no access to spiritual perfection. Here, the word akritatmanam is very significant. Atma means body, soul, or mind, and akritatma means the common man who cannot control the senses or the mind. Because the common man is unable to control the senses and the mind, it is his duty to seek the shelter of a great soul or a great devotee of the Lord and just try to please him. That will make his life perfect. A common man cannot race to the topmost stage of spiritual realization simply by following the rituals and religious principles. He has to take shelter of a bona fide spiritual master and work under his direction faithfully and sincerely. Then he becomes perfect without a doubt. That's in the Bhaktivedanta purport.

#### mercy, devotion, spiritual advancement
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Dy9VGtyDXi8?t=408
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The dust of the lotus feet of a pure devotee is highly potent for making advancement in spiritual life."
**Summary:** This segment elaborates on the various ways mercy is transmitted, including prasadam, darshan, hearing, and worship, with a special emphasis on the potency of the dust of the lotus feet of a pure devotee.

And there are unlimited ways in which this mercy is actually transmitted. We can list so many things which for our senses, like prasadam. By contacting the prasadam, we get mercy with our tongue. By having darshana of the deities, we get mercy. By hearing, we get mercy. By worshiping, we get mercy. By taking, we get mercy. One more item is here which is highly potent for making advancement in spiritual life. What is that? The dust of the lotus feet of a pure devotee. This is not metaphorical. It is actually the real dust of the lotus feet of a pure devotee. If one is able to place the dust on his head, then one gets special mercy. These are all ways of receiving the mercy which are not phenomenally calculable through some material phenomena. What is the value of a dust? Take the dust of the lotus feet of Prabhupada. Here is deity. Whole day deity is here. Some dust will collect. Water, water, water, water, water, water, water, water.

#### mercy, spiritual master, bhakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Dy9VGtyDXi8?t=570
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Just by reading you don't get transcendental knowledge. When you read, Prabhupada should act on you through these pages, then you get transcendental mercy, then you get understanding, then you get realization."
**Summary:** This segment explains that spiritual relationships are not transactional and emphasizes that transcendental knowledge comes through the mercy of Srila Prabhupada, not just by mechanically reading his books.

So this is the meaning of mercy. Our mind is always used to transactional things. That means, you know, I pay for it, I get it. Our relationship, spiritual relationship with the deity, relationship with the holy name, our relationship with the spiritual master, our relationship with Vaishnavas, we cannot think in terms of a transaction. What do you get from Srila Prabhupada? No, I get transcendental knowledge. How do you get transcendental knowledge? Because books are there, I read, I get transcendental knowledge. Just by reading you don't get transcendental knowledge. When you read, Prabhupada should act on you through these pages, then you get transcendental mercy, then you get mercy, then you get understanding, then you get realization. So one should understand that reading Prabhupada's books or anything, contacting any of the forms of mercy of the Lord, is not something that can be mechanically, ritualistically achieved. Prabhupada explains it, just by ritual, one cannot, you know, get it done by the Lord. It is bhakti. Even if one chants 16 rounds, it may help, Prabhupada is saying, it may help, but he will have no access to spiritual perfection. Access to spiritual perfection means you have to get the personal mercy of a pure devotee, not general mercy. General mercy is available. Prabhupada's books are available everywhere. General mercy. Personal mercy means, yes, Prabhupada gets linked to you. When you get initiated, he links to you, and he is your personal spiritual master. He is not only teaching you, there are so many other things he is doing to you. He is monitoring to you. He and Chaitya Guru together is monitoring to you, monitoring your growth, spiritual growth.

#### spiritual master, mercy, service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Dy9VGtyDXi8?t=797
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Vaishnavas always say that I am made up of the mercy of my spiritual master. Whatever I have is a kripa of my spiritual master. I have not earned it."
**Summary:** Using the analogy of tuition, this segment explains that a spiritual master provides personal care and guidance, aware of the disciple's spiritual level and problems, and that advancement comes through the mercy of the spiritual master, not just personal effort.

Just like the student has to be, you know, taught personally. You see, when you go to the school, hundred students are taught by the teacher, right? After that, there is tuition. In tuition, what does the tuition master do? Where is he particularly weak? Then he will give him special lessons, personal care. Simply reading Prabhupada's book is like sitting in the classroom, and that's it, and reading. Prabhupada's action upon his disciple is not just that. We need to understand that Prabhupada is actually, you know, aware of the disciple's level of spiritual attainment. He is aware of where the devotee is having problems, not material problems, in getting detached. All that he is aware of is that he is aware of where the devotee is having problems. S.A. Prasadat, Bhagwat Prasadat, one is constantly receiving knowledge from within the heart of the disciple is receiving in order to make advancement in spiritual life. Therefore the Vaishnavas always say that I am made up of the mercy of my spiritual master. Whatever I have is a kripa of my spiritual master. If I have some standing, if I have some detachment, it is given by my spiritual master. I have not earned it. He believes it. I have not earned it. Why? Because he understands that actually by any bodily action, by tongue, moving your tongue sixteen rounds, and all you cannot get bhakti. You can get bhakti when you move sixteen rounds. It will give you some foundation, but ultimately you have to get the personal mercy of the spiritual master which comes by here Prabhupada is giving that secret here.

#### spiritual master, mercy, service, attitude
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Dy9VGtyDXi8?t=937
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The real perfection or the result of all that comes only by the personal mercy of the spiritual master."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes that serving a bona fide spiritual master is key to receiving mercy and purification, and that the result of all spiritual practices comes from the spiritual master's personal mercy, which requires faith and gratitude.

By serving a bona fide spiritual master, by serving him, one gets that. Because the common man is unable to control the spiritual life and the mind, it is his duty to seek shelter of a great soul or a great devotee of the Lord and just try to please him. Just see, do service to him and try to please him. Then, even if you cannot control your senses and mind, still by his mercy, you will purify. Very soon you will be able to control your senses. Very soon you will be able to control your mind. So therefore, one should understand that the easiest, though we may do all the exercises that we are trying to do, so many secondary instructions are there, control this, samas, damas, tapas, all these things are there. We are trying to follow all those things. While following those things, one should understand that the result of the real perfection or the result of all that comes only by the personal mercy of the spiritual master. And this has to be practiced by the devotee because you don't see with your eyes. It is a matter of faith from this, esya prasadat bhagavata prasadat, everyday we are singing, but we cannot see how it is happening. When you see something somebody gives you something, you know, gross in your hand, you say thank you because you see he has given. But we have to learn how to transcendentally see this through the descending process that everyday we have to say thank you, thank you, thank you. Every moment we have to go on saying thank you, thank you, thank you to Srila Prabhupada, to Guru and Krishna, continuously they are giving. So what is the spiritual process of spiritual advancement?

#### mercy, attitude, determination
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Dy9VGtyDXi8?t=1074
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "To attract the mercy of the Lord, it is not the quantum of skill, the quantum of what you have given, and all these things is not what the attitude is."
**Summary:** This segment explains that spiritual progress involves begging for the mercy of devotees and Vaishnavas, emphasizing that one's efforts are to show seriousness and attract the Lord's mercy, illustrated by the story of the sparrow and the ocean.

Spiritual progress in our sampradaya, it is always beg for the mercy of the devotees, beg for the mercy of the Vaishnavas. Then one may think what is my role? Shouldn't I do anything? No, one should do, but one should understand that what one does is simply to show our attitude to the spiritual master, that I am serious about it, I want your mercy. I am serious about following your instruction. Just like the story in Bhagavad Gita, Prabhupada explains about the sparrow trying to recover her eggs from the ocean. She was so determined that she was trying to take out drop by drop water through her beaks and trying to empty the ocean, but finally, her obviously, you know, by material calculation, can a sparrow empty the ocean? Not possible. Her goal was what? To get the eggs back, but then she got mercy of Garuda. She got the egg back. Why was Garuda attracted? Because of her determination. In the same way, to attract the mercy of the Lord, it is not the quantum of skill, the quantum of what you have given, and all these things is not what the attitude is. If the attitude to attract the mercy of the Lord is there, then what you need, guru will give, whatever you need for his service, whatever you need for your advancement, the guru will bestow upon us. So this is a process by which, you know...

I cannot spare anything, I have to do. As far as result is concerned, oh, I chanted so many years very nicely, now I have advanced. You can say that I have chanted nicely so long for by Prabhupada's mercy, my chanting has become fruitful. I got mercy. You can say that you cannot take away Prabhupada, you cannot take away guru's mercy. So these are some of the fundamental principles of the sources of advancement for any Vaishnava. #### hearing, submissiveness, mercy, rational mind
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Dy9VGtyDXi8?t=1382
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The rational mind is the biggest enemy for all these things."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the importance of submissive hearing to receive the feelings and transformation from the speaker, contrasting it with intellectual hearing, and warns against the rational mind as an enemy to receiving mercy.

Just like let us say we hear Srimad Bhagavatam class. It is said that one has to submissively hear. You can submissively hear, you can intellectually hear also. When you intellectually hear, you will only get the ideas. When you submissively hear, you will get the feelings of the speaker. When somebody speaks, he speaks what? Ideas, feelings, realization, right? He speaks. You submissively hear, then the feelings also you get, and the feelings go, the transformation that happens for a person is faster when you can get the feelings of let us say Prabhupada's feelings. If you put a filter of only intellectual thing, let us say Prabhupada lecture you are hearing, okay, okay, philosophical, philosophical, all these things intellectually you are adjusting, but Prabhupada says rascal, then, you know, it is nothing philosophical. It is not a philosophical idea at all. You get the feeling why Prabhupada is calling this fellow rascal. So that submissiveness is, if you have submissiveness, how will you have submissiveness? Only when there is anticipation you will have submissiveness, otherwise, oh, you would be submissive, oh, I am expecting something, anticipating. So anticipating mercy of deity, you go before the deity, you have to have anticipation. It does not mean specific prayers, but there is mercy coming. Chanting Hare Krishna, you have to have anticipation of mercy. Being in the presence of a spiritual master, you should get anticipate. So there has to be preparedness for receiving this mercy also. And the rational mind is the biggest enemy for all these things. The rational mind. Sometimes devotees get stuck in this rational thing for whole life. Everything they intellectualize. They sit in the class, they intellectualize. They go before the deity, they intellectualize. They take association, they take association, they intellectualize.

#### rationality, bhakti, knowledge, service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Dy9VGtyDXi8?t=1642
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Bhakti never stands without knowledge. There is knowledge always."
**Summary:** This segment addresses the question of how much rationality should be applied in spiritual life, explaining that while rationality has its place, over-rationalization and speculation can hinder the reception of mercy, and that bhakti is founded on knowledge, not ignorance.

You see rational, when you are, the good question. Everywhere rationality is there. The issue is about being over rational. Just like you say bhakti and vedanta. Vedanta is rational path. Bhakti is not the rational path. Bhakti is not the rational path, it is the mercy path. So in order to glorify, in order to appreciate, in order to, one uses rationality, even in bhakti. When rationality is not used in the spiritual life, then, when rationality is used to understand the mercy, then it becomes speculation. Just like you say one truth you receive, you don't understand, philosophically you don't understand. Now if you go deep, deep, deep, going on and on and on, speculating too many things, then you are over rational. You need to understand that the rational understanding at the right time, I have to do more service. We can all rationally analyze and somehow to control the senses and all these things, rationally analyze. That's what the mayavadis, impersonalists do. They rationally, they analyze what is self concept and this and that all those things. And as devotees, when the right time comes, he comes out of the bodily concept. Now, that's what it said, when you have bhakti, you have knowledge also. Bhakti never stands without knowledge. There is knowledge always. There is a very foundation is knowledge. Bhakti is not foundation is not ignorance, is knowledge, knowledge of truth, tattva is always there as a foundation. So how much we should apply our intelligence? Don't over endeavor if that intelligence is not coming to you. Now serve, continue serving, you will understand. So that is, that is the extent to which you should exercise your, put all your efforts in serving, put your efforts in appreciating the mercy of Prabhupada, all these things. Any other question?

#### effort, anticipation, mercy, humility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Dy9VGtyDXi8?t=1820
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Philosophy in life is to put best efforts, but whether I really put the best efforts, I do not know."
**Summary:** This segment addresses the question of how to be sure one is putting in their all effort, explaining that one should strive to put in their best effort but not think they have necessarily done so, maintaining humility and acknowledging that success comes from Krishna and Prabhupada's mercy.

Any other question? Hare Krishna Prabhu, you said Prabhu that we should put our all effort and anticipate. How should I be sure that the effort which I am putting in my devotional service is my all effort or we may be sometime in the miser condition also that I may think that I am putting all my effort, but I may not be putting. You should put all your efforts, but not think that you have put all your efforts. While putting the efforts, you should think I should put best of my efforts, but should not think that actually whether I put the best or not, I do not know, maybe I withheld so many things inside. You should not say I put my best efforts. You say philosophy in life is to put best efforts, but whether I really put the best efforts, I do not know. So even if you know something fails, you will say that I have not put best effort. If he succeeds, he will also say I have not put best efforts. Whatever was lacking, Krishna and Prabhupada gave and therefore I did it. Okay. Krantarachchimad Bhagavatam Ki Jaya. Jagatguru Srila Prabhupada Jaya. Jagatguru Srila Prabhupada Ki Jaya.
