**Key Quote:** "If the head is clear and has not gone mad, then everything is in proper position."
**Summary:** The speaker reads and explains a verse and purport discussing the roles of Brahmanas and Kshatriyas in society, emphasizing the importance of protecting the Brahmanas as the head of the social body.

OM NAMO BARVATE VASUDEVAYA. Reading from Srimad Parvatam, Canto 3, Chapter 22, Verse 3. TATRANAYA SHRI CHACCASMAN. DOHA SAHASRAT SAHASRAPAD. RIDAYAM TASHYAHI BRAMMA. SATRAM ANRAM PRACHAT SATE. Translation and purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Shila Prabhupada. For the protection of the Brahmanas, the thousand-leaded Supreme Being created us, the Satryas, from His thousand arms. Hence the Brahmanas are said to be His heart and the Satryas, His arms. Purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Shila Prabhupada. Satryas are specifically meant to maintain the Brahmanas because if the Brahmanas are protected and the head of civilization is protected, Brahmanas are supposed to be protected. Brahmanas are supposed to be the head of the social body. If the head is clear and has not gone mad, then everything is in proper position. The Lord is described thus, Namo Brahmania Devaya, Gro Brahmana Hitayacha. The purport of this prayer is that the Lord specifically protects the Brahmanas and the crows, and then He protects all other members of society. It is His will that universal welfare work depends on the protection of crows and Brahmanas. Thus Brahmanical culture and crow protection are the basic principles for human civilization. Satryas are especially meant to protect the Brahmanas, as is the supreme will of the Lord. Gro Brahmana Hitayacha. As the Supreme Will of the Lord, Grow Brahmana Hitayacha. Because within the body, the heart is a very important part, so the brahmanas are also the important element in human society. The satryas are more like the whole body. Even though the whole body is bigger than the heart, the heart is more important. Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta Parvat.

#### Lord's energies, simultaneous oneness and difference, Vishnu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZQJ8JEmcW0Q?t=177
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Everybody, ultimately everything in existence is the Lord through His energies."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Manu's address to Kardama Muni, emphasizing the Lord's simultaneous oneness and difference from His creation, and how everything is the Lord through His energies, functioning for the pleasure of Krishna and His expansions.

This is being explained by Manu to Kardama Muni, starting with saying that, O Kardama Muni, you are a brahmana, and Brahma created you from his face, and you are full of austerity, knowledge, mystic power, and averse to sense gratification. And then for protection of brahmanas like you, the Lord created kshatriyas, who are like him, like Manu, kshatriyas, and the body of a kshatriya, the body of a brahmana, and the lord, through his thousand-legged Supreme Being, created us, the kshatriyas, from his thousand arms. And this way, the brahamanas and kshatriyas protect each other as well as themselves. And the lord who is both the cause and the effect protects them through each other. So, how this is slowly becoming obvious is because there is a time interval here where these two events are lived here. That is not necessary. They don't matter. We are like water. This entire material world function with the Lord as the cause and effect is finely explained here, who is both the cause and effect and is yet immutable, protects them through each other. See, the Lord is simultaneously one and different from everything in His creation. So when, just like when Manu appeared before Kardamuni, the way Kardamuni looked at him, the perspective with which he looked at him, he was addressing him as a Lord because he saw that, because he understood that a perfect Kshatriya is representing the Lord. And he is a Lord in one sense. So everybody, ultimately everything in existence is the Lord through His energies. We are one kind of, one kind of energies, expansion of the Lord. Material energy is another kind of energy. So, so many different energies are, the Lord has got unlimited, vividaya shakti, unlimited varieties of shaktis are there. It belongs to the Lord and ultimately all the shaktis are functioning for the pleasure of one single personality, Krishna, Adipurusha and His expansions, different Vishnus. So in the material world, a particular Vishnu as Shirodakshaya Vishnu, Garbhodakshaya Vishnu, Karnodakshaya Vishnu, Garbhodakshaya Vishnu

is given here. Just like the sun is on the head of different people and each person feels relationship with the sun above his head. In the same way, the super soul is never divided. The two kinds of living entities. So, living entities between themselves, like the Brahmanas are given certain qualities, Kshatriyas are given certain qualities, the Kshatriya body is given certain qualities, the Brahmana body is given certain qualities. The metal energy itself has to reconfigure. For instance, we have to build this temple, we have to bring cement, so many things from different places, brick and everything. What is our objective? We want to create a visual shelter for the Lord, a visual beautiful place like this. This is our purpose. And then we reorganize everything. So, activity in this material world means nothing but reorganizing matter. And we are... The key element for reorganizing matter is buddhi, intelligence. And Prabhupada says that the super soul is realized in this material world as intelligence form. The all-pervading intelligence, everywhere that intelligence is there. Of course, that intelligence is given according to one's karma. There is a... Each individual body has got certain intelligence which is deposited. Certain intelligence, certain type of intelligence, brahmana intelligence, kshatriya intelligence, shudra intelligence, is all deposited in the sharira, the body of the living entity. And then that works. And that's how the society is running. That is the reason why atheists are not able to locate the factor of God. Where is God? You don't need God at all. Everything is running. I don't see God's factor at all. One human being is protecting another human being. Why do you say God protected? One set of human beings are feeding the other set of human beings. Why is it so? It is so because it is designed like that. God wants to create an atmosphere where you can forget Him. This material world is meant for that. So it is working very perfectly. Those who don't want to see God will never see God. Those who don't want to see God will be thoroughly convinced there is no God. Because whatever the Lord does, He will do it perfectly. He wanted to create a godless atmosphere. He has created it. And in the midst of it, now come to the what role the Paramatma, the Lord plays with respect to those who want to serve Him. Those who want to serve Him, the Lord acts as the Chaitya Guru for the living entity. Not only for Vaishnavas, for everybody. He is a Chaitya Guru for everyone. He is a teacher for everyone. We rise our consciousness sometimes up, then we fall down also. Our test we fail, test we pass. We keep going up. Up and down. Same way, every living entity is going up and down. Going on and like this. This journey is facilitated by the Supersoul with utmost respect for the living entity's freedom. This is the key thing. What is the Lord's feeling towards the material world? Lord's feeling towards the material world is of high respect for our free will.

Yes but you mentioned something peculiar because you say the temple is existing. It's existing. Commander I see. Training. Can you elaborate please? You see nothing, nobody created anything. Before let us say only plain land was there. Then what happened? Jnana, Bala, Kriya, three things mixed together and this temple was manifest. Energy did not manifest. Energy was already there. Jnana, Bala and Kriya, what does it do? It reconfigures matter. Somewhere else different matter is there, it brings together. What is a rich man and a poor man? Difference. In the configuration of a rich man, money is in his pocket. When so many donors gave and we built this temple, what did it happen? You went, you used Jnana, Bala, Kriya. The money moved from his pocket to you as a collector. Then it came and the money moved from here to the shopkeeper. He moved that matter here. That's all. It's just nothing but very... Achieving anything in this material world is nothing but very nicely to oversimplify. Just reconfiguring matter. And to reconfigure matter, you need three things. Intelligence. Jnana. Bala

**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

#### sadhana, karma, sense control
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EzWpNuRHul8?t=1

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Chanting sixteen rounds every day is like building a dam across your past karma."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa emphasizes the importance of morning sadhana, especially chanting, as a means to counteract past karma and sense disturbances, leading to a more peaceful spiritual life.

Just because you have come to the shelter of Prabhupada, you have joined this gun movement, Prabhupada's movement, please always remember, according to Kala, our karma is continuously unfolding, whether it is maybe sickness or mental disturbance or this and that and all. And that will disturb your Krishna consciousness practice. Chanting sixteen rounds every day is like building a dam across your past karma. You can have a peaceful spiritual life, not only chanting sixteen rounds. Morning sadhana full if you do, you are completely insulated from all kinds of so many sins we have done in our past lives. That tendency is there. Vasana is there. Force is there. Sense gratification is there. All that will all harass. Senses will come and disturb. All these things, all those things can be countered if you have a very strict sadhana, very strict sadhana. Till ten o'clock, nine o'clock, till class is over, just focus on this sadhana.

### Importance of the Spiritual Lineage | Sri Madhu Pandit Dasa | ISKCON Bangalore
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

#### reality, purpose, creation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1JoChAd6kSU?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This world is a real world with a real purpose created by absolute real personality of Godhead, Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker establishes the Vaishnava perspective that the world is real, purposeful, and created by Krishna.

Devotees as spiritualists understand that this world is not illusion. This world is a real world with a real purpose created by absolute real personality of Godhead, Krishna. They see this world having a purpose and a plan in the mind of the Supreme Personality of Godhead who is the creator of everything, maintainer of everything, and annulator of everything.

**Audience:** Newcomers, General public

**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the Vaishnava view with that of the 'Karmis' who are focused on sense gratification and lack a higher purpose.

The only perspective the Karmis have is enjoy life in this life, enjoy your senses, enjoy the body. That is the only goal, which is completely self-centered. So they don't have a plan.

**Summary:** The speaker explains that true peace requires understanding dharma and the divine plan, including societal organization and the performance of yajnas.

Even if somebody says, we want to bring peace on earth, they cannot because they have to know what is dharma, what is the design of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, how the society should be organized, yajnas have to be performed. In the early days when I had just joined ISKCON. At that time, the first few years, we were, you know, I was going on Sankirtan party and taking, you know, van and loading books and going from all over South India, different places. And then we used to camp in different places. And then one, in one such camp, we reached Kerala, one place called Wayanad. Lot of estates are there, tea estates. So we happened to, you know, we always find some contact. And go and stay with them and then distribute the books, all those things. We stay for 15-20 days in that town and then go to the next town. So we went, we have 4-5 devotees, we went and we had a standard van. In that van we went, we reached there to, that estate belonged to one Jain. We went to that, he was very happy to receive us and all. Then we went and then he opened his guest house. So devotees were all in different, different rooms were given. They gave me one room also, main guest house, one room only. Opened the guest house and then I, you know, the caretaker opened the guest house and door and I went inside. It was a big room and a big room. And then I saw, you know, on the two sides, L-shaped, there was, there was interior pebbles and all, you know, the planter box kind of thing. There were pebbles and all, big, big pebbles. So I just went inside. Then as I was looking at that, then one corner of that pebble box, I saw one cobra standing with the hood up. I got scared. Immediately fear came into me and I saw that. And then I, you know, immediately called the caretaker and I said, see, see, see, there is a cobra standing up. Panicking. And I am supposed to stay in that room. Then caretaker came in. What happened? What happened? What, what is this? No, no. Look at that. There's a cobra there. Then he said, then he laughed. Oh, it's a plastic cobra, he said. Oh plastic cobra. Then because I got so scared, what happened? Then he went. He told it's plastic cobra. But somehow or the other, I don't want to go near it. Even after he's telling it is plastic cobra. And every time I see, I'm only seeing a real cobra. Then I know I called him and then somewhere around say 4 o'clock we reached. 4 o'clock to 6 o'clock, 7 o'clock I'm battling with my mind. That is a rubber. It's a rubber cobra. Why am I fearing? All this when I'm not seeing it. When I see, immediately trigger. There is a rubber cobra. Fear. So it took some time like that. Somehow that night I slept in that same room only.
